summary: RadioPad delivers a meaningful subset of §14.19/§14.20 but the two flagship clinical mobile features and the entire WASM-plugin runtime are absent. STRONG, production-grade implementations exist for: push notifications (real APNs ES256 token-auth + FCM HTTP v1 OAuth2 in PushSender.cs, registration in PushController), biometric unlock (frontend/lib/biometric.ts + ShellBridge gating token release), a Capacitor 6 shell wrapping the Next.js export, offline drafts, on-device voice dictation, and a fully-functional content marketplace with Stripe Connect revenue-share (MarketplaceController). NOTE the marketplace is a CONTENT marketplace (rulebooks/templates/prompt-packs), and the "plugin SDK" (desktop/plugin-sdk) is a PACS-vendor signed-manifest adapter SDK (Ed25519+SHA-256) — NOT the PRD's Rust+WASM/WASI code-plugin runtime. CRITICAL GAPS: MOB-003 critical-results notifications with WebAuthn acknowledgement (no critical-results flow, no WebAuthn-ack linkage — WebAuthn exists only for login), MOB-004 peer-review assignments/scoring (no controller, no UI), MOB-002 'no editing in MVP for safety' is contradicted (the only dedicated mobile pages — frontend/app/mobile/{dictate,reports/edit,reports/sign} — are editors), MOB-009 screenshot protection (doc-only, no FLAG_SECURE/iOS native code), PL-001/PL-002/PL-006 (no WASM ABI, no WASI sandbox with CPU/memory quotas — the only sandbox, InProcessMcpSandbox, explicitly states 'no hard OS-level isolation' and only enforces a wall-clock timeout), PL-005 per-tenant plugin allowlist/pinning, PL-003 listing reviews/security-scan/SBOM/license, PL-004 certification tiers. Stack-lock note applied: function judged on Next.js/.NET/Tauri/Capacitor stack regardless of OSS libs named in PRD §9.

requirements.title: MOB-001 — Read-only worklist with filters
requirements.evidence: Backend worklist data source exists: backend/.../Controllers/ReportsController.cs GET [HttpGet] (line ~103) accepts FromQuery modality, status (ReportStatus), q, skip, take and filters reports. The Capacitor shell (mobile/capacitor.config.ts, webDir '../frontend/out') wraps the FULL Next.js app, so the web worklist/home renders on device. No DEDICATED mobile worklist screen and no explicit read-only worklist 'peek' UI under frontend/app/mobile/ (only dictate/edit/sign pages exist there).
requirements.gap: Add a purpose-built read-only mobile worklist view (touch-optimised, filter chips for modality/status/date/assignee) rather than relying on the desktop-shaped table reflowed in the WebView. Confirm filters cover the PRD-implied set (modality, body part, priority, status).

requirements.title: MOB-002 — Draft report viewer (no editing in MVP for safety)
requirements.evidence: Dedicated mobile pages exist but they EDIT, not view-only: frontend/app/mobile/reports/[reportId]/edit/MobileEditClient.tsx (collapsible per-section editor), frontend/app/mobile/dictate/[reportId]/MobileDictateClient.tsx (dictation into draft), frontend/app/mobile/reports/[reportId]/sign/MobileSignClient.tsx. mobile/README.md (lines 68-80) describes these as editor/dictation/sign pages.
requirements.gap: PRD explicitly requires a read-ONLY draft viewer for the MVP 'for safety' and defers editing. Current implementation does the opposite (mobile editing is enabled). Either ship a read-only viewer and gate/remove mobile editing for MVP, or document an intentional deviation from the safety clause and reconcile with CLAUDE.md safety boundaries.

requirements.title: MOB-003 — Critical-results notifications with WebAuthn acknowledgement
requirements.evidence: Push infrastructure exists (PushController, PushSender) and WebAuthn exists for LOGIN only (backend/.../Controllers/WebAuthnController.cs — register-options/register/signin-options/signin, AUTH-001). No critical-results notification entity/flow found (grep for critical-result/CriticalResult finds only rulebook YAML 'critical language' test cases and report-status 'Acknowledged' analytics in OtherControllers.cs, which is the signing acknowledgement — unrelated). No endpoint or client flow ties a critical-results push to a WebAuthn step-up acknowledgement.
requirements.gap: Build the full feature: critical-results notification model/trigger (likely off rulebook critical findings), targeted push dispatch (kind=critical), and a mobile acknowledgement screen that performs a WebAuthn assertion to record a non-repudiable ack with audit. None of this clinical-safety-critical chain exists yet.

requirements.title: MOB-004 — Peer-review assignments and quick scoring
requirements.evidence: No peer-review controller in backend/.../Controllers/ (full file list reviewed — Reports, ReportsLifecycle, Marketplace, WebAuthn, Push, etc.; none for peer review). grep peer.?review|PeerReview across backend src and frontend/app returns no application code (only PROGRESS.md/PRD text). No peer-review/scoring UI under frontend/app.
requirements.gap: Implement peer-review assignment model + endpoints (assign reviewer, list assignments, submit score/rubric) and a mobile quick-scoring screen. Entire feature absent.

requirements.title: MOB-005 — Approval flows (rulebook promotion, template publishing) with step-up auth
requirements.evidence: Approval endpoints exist server-side: MarketplaceController approve/reject submissions (RequireRole MedicalDirector/ItAdmin); rulebook promotion exists in RulebooksController; WebAuthn machinery exists for step-up. The marketplace approve/reject UI (frontend/app/marketplace/page.tsx 'Review' tab) is desktop-web and reachable in the WebView. NOTE: PROGRESS.md reuses the label 'MOB-005' for the /offline page, so prior iteration claims conflict with PRD IDs — verified against PRD text here.
requirements.gap: No mobile-specific approval surface and, critically, no step-up (WebAuthn/re-auth) enforced AT the approval action. Wire a step-up assertion before approve/promote/publish and provide a mobile approvals inbox.

requirements.title: MOB-006 — Biometric unlock; no PHI cached locally beyond session unless explicitly enabled
requirements.evidence: frontend/lib/biometric.ts (isBiometricAvailable, isBiometricLockEnabled, enableBiometricLock, unlockWithBiometric, gateAuthTokenWithBiometric via @aparajita/capacitor-biometric-auth). frontend/app/ShellBridge.tsx gates bearer-token release behind the biometric prompt when radiopad.biometricLock=1 before exposing the token to the API client. Auth token persisted in OS secure store (secureAuth.ts per PROGRESS iter-16). Push payloads are deliberately PHI-free (PushSender.cs comments; mobile/README.md). Offline draft buffer holds report content only when the user creates offline drafts (opt-in by action), not an ambient PHI cache.
requirements.gap: Minor: there is no explicit per-tenant/per-user 'allow local PHI caching' toggle named in the clause; behaviour is effectively 'no ambient cache' which satisfies the default. Add a settings toggle to make the 'unless explicitly enabled' branch first-class if product wants it.

requirements.title: MOB-007 — Offline read with encrypted cache (size-limited), opt-in per tenant
requirements.evidence: frontend/lib/offlineDrafts.ts implements an offline draft store + /offline page (frontend/app/offline/page.tsx) + auto-sync on reconnect (startAutoSync via @capacitor/network). On DESKTOP (Tauri) it uses an AES-256-GCM encrypted store keyed in the OS keyring (offline_drafts_save/get/list/delete invoke commands). On MOBILE (Capacitor) it falls back to @capacitor/preferences storing plaintext JSON; web uses localStorage.
requirements.gap: Three deficiencies vs clause: (1) mobile cache is NOT encrypted (plaintext Preferences) — only desktop is AES-GCM; (2) NO size limit/eviction policy; (3) NOT opt-in per tenant (no tenant flag gating offline cache). Also this is a draft WRITE buffer, not the 'offline READ with encrypted cache' the clause describes. Add encrypted mobile storage (e.g. capacitor-secure-storage-plugin is already a dependency), size cap, tenant opt-in flag, and a read-cache of recent studies/reports.

requirements.title: MOB-008 — Push notifications via APNs (iOS) and FCM (Android); EE self-hosted relay
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Services/Push/PushSender.cs: ApnsSender uses token-based auth (.p8 ES256 JWT) to https://api.push.apple.com/3/device/{token} over HTTP/2; FcmSender mints RS256 service-account OAuth2 token and POSTs FCM HTTP v1 messages:send with cached access token. PushSenderRegistry resolves by platform. PushController /api/push/devices register/unregister + /test (RBAC ItAdmin/ReportingAdmin); tokens hashed in audit. frontend/lib/push.ts registerForPush/unregisterFromPush; capacitor.config.ts requests alert/badge/sound. mobile/README.md documents required env vars.
requirements.gap: EE self-hosted relay option is not implemented (only direct APNs/FCM). PROGRESS.md mislabels push as MOB-007 vs PRD's MOB-008, but the function is present. Add an optional EE relay sender behind the same IPushSender abstraction.

requirements.title: MOB-009 — Screenshot protection (FLAG_SECURE / iOS guidance)
requirements.evidence: grep FLAG_SECURE / screenCaptureDidChange / UIScreen.captured across the repo (excluding node_modules) returns only PRD.md, CHANGELOG.md, PROGRESS.md, and an auth test — NO native Android setFlags(FLAG_SECURE) and NO iOS screen-capture handling. mobile/ has no android/ or ios/ platform folders generated yet (cap add not run), and no Capacitor plugin or custom native code enforces screenshot protection.
requirements.gap: Add FLAG_SECURE on the Android Activity (or a Capacitor plugin) and iOS screen-recording/screenshot mitigation (blur on background, UIScreen.isCaptured observer). P0 security control currently entirely absent in code.

requirements.title: MOB-010 — Quick voice memo to attach to a study (transcribed locally)
requirements.evidence: frontend/app/mobile/dictate/[reportId]/MobileDictateClient.tsx provides on-device dictation using Web Speech API with @capacitor-community/speech-recognition on native and a localStorage draft buffer (mobile/README.md lines 68-94). Local transcription requirement is met for dictation.
requirements.gap: This is dictation-into-report-sections, not a 'quick voice memo attached to a study' as a discrete artifact. No memo entity, no attach-to-study linkage, no audio capture/storage. Implement a memo object (transcribed text, optional audio) attachable to a study/report.

requirements.title: MOB — PWA fallback (section intro requirement)
requirements.evidence: §14.19 intro states 'PWA fallback.' No web app manifest (manifest.webmanifest) or service worker found for installability/offline PWA behaviour; mobile/README describes only the Capacitor native wrapper of the static export. frontend/app/offline/page.tsx is an in-app offline-drafts view, not a PWA offline shell.
requirements.gap: Add a PWA manifest + service worker (next-pwa or App Router equivalent) so the app is installable and minimally usable as a PWA when native isn't available.

requirements.title: PL-001 — Plugin SDK (Rust + WASM ABI; TypeScript shim for connectors)
requirements.evidence: desktop/plugin-sdk/ provides a PACS-vendor adapter SDK: manifest.schema.json + README describe SIGNED MANIFESTS (Ed25519 + SHA-256) declaring capabilities (window-detect, accession-grab, paste-back, hanging-protocol, study-launch) that map to documented Tauri commands; loader in desktop/src-tauri/src/pacs_plugins.rs. Explicitly 'Plugins do not run arbitrary code' (README line 5). No WASM/WASI ABI and no TypeScript connector shim package.
requirements.gap: PRD's SDK is a code-extension SDK (Rust compiled to WASM with a defined ABI + a TS shim for connector authors). Current SDK is a declarative PACS-bridge manifest format with no executable plugin model. To reach the PRD intent, define a WASM component ABI and ship a TS connector shim — large new subsystem. (Per stack-lock, WASM via wasmtime in the .NET/Tauri host is acceptable, but it does not exist yet.)

requirements.title: PL-002 — Plugin manifest with capabilities, permissions, signing, semver
requirements.evidence: desktop/plugin-sdk/manifest.schema.json: required id/name/vendor/version(semver pattern)/sha256/capabilities; optional executable+executable_sha256+executable_sig_b64, minDesktopVersion, compliance{phi, auditTopics}. Signing: Ed25519 detached sig + SHA-256 verified in pacs_plugins.rs/sandbox.rs. Semver enforced via regex. Capabilities enumerated.
requirements.gap: Two PRD elements weak/absent: (1) a general PERMISSIONS model (the schema has fixed PACS-capability enums + a coarse compliance.phi field, not a granular requested-permissions/scopes list a sandbox would enforce); (2) capabilities are PACS-bridge-specific, not the broad plugin capability surface PRD implies. Generalise the manifest to a permission/scope model once a real plugin runtime exists.

requirements.title: PL-003 — Marketplace listing with reviews, downloads, security scan status, license, SBOM
requirements.evidence: MarketplaceListing entity (Entities.cs lines 805-837) has Name, Description, Kind, ArtifactBody, PriceCents, Status, Version (semver), InstallCount, ReviewNotes (reviewer feedback), RejectionReason, Stripe fields. MarketplaceController.List returns approved listings; frontend/app/marketplace/page.tsx shows install counts and a Browse catalogue.
requirements.gap: Missing fields/features: user REVIEWS/ratings (ReviewNotes is reviewer approval feedback, not buyer reviews), no security-scan status, no LICENSE field, no SBOM artifact/link. 'Downloads' is partially covered by InstallCount. Add review/rating model, security-scan + SBOM ingestion, and a license field to the listing.

requirements.title: PL-004 — Plugin certification track (clinical / connector / utility tiers)
requirements.evidence: No certification-tier concept on MarketplaceListing (Entities.cs) or in MarketplaceController; Kind is only rulebook/template/prompt_pack. grep certification|tier across the marketplace entity returns no matches. PACS manifest schema has no certification tier either.
requirements.gap: Add a certification-tier field + a certification workflow (clinical/connector/utility) with reviewer gating and badge display. Absent.

requirements.title: PL-005 — Per-tenant plugin allowlist and pinning
requirements.evidence: No per-tenant plugin allowlist or version-pinning found: grep allowlist|pin|Pinned across desktop returns nothing relevant; pacs_plugins.rs only has a local per-machine .enabled toggle (no tenant scope, no allowlist, no pin to a specific version). Tenant entity has MCP/sandbox flags but no plugin allowlist. (Note: an MCP TOOL allowlist exists for MCP tools, which is a different subsystem.)
requirements.gap: Implement a tenant-scoped plugin allowlist + version pinning (admin-managed), enforced at plugin load/enable time with audit. Entirely absent for plugins.

requirements.title: PL-006 — Plugin sandbox (WASI capability-limited) with CPU/memory/time quotas
requirements.evidence: The only sandbox is backend/.../Services/Mcp/InProcessMcpSandbox.cs, whose own doc-comment states: 'There is no hard OS-level isolation here — that's the next iteration's job (likely WASM via Wasmtime). The single guarantee this layer provides is the wall-clock timeout ... and the connector-allowlist regex gate.' It enforces only a CancellationTokenSource timeout — no CPU or MEMORY quota, no WASI. PACS plugins are non-executing signed manifests (no sandbox at all because no code runs). grep WASI|wasmtime returns only this MCP file + PRD text.
requirements.gap: Build a real WASI/Wasmtime (or equivalent) sandbox enforcing capability limits + CPU, memory, and wall-time quotas for executable plugins. Currently only a wall-clock timeout exists and only for in-process MCP delegates, not plugins.

requirements.title: PL-007 — Hot install/uninstall without daemon restart
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/pacs_plugins.rs: pacs_plugins_list re-reads the plugins directory on demand and pacs_plugins_set_enabled toggles a .enabled file WITHOUT mutating the signed manifest or restarting (enable/disable is live). Manifests are loaded by dropping a folder into the plugins dir and verified on next list/launch.
requirements.gap: Hot ENABLE/DISABLE works without restart, but there is no first-class hot INSTALL/UNINSTALL command (install = manually copy a folder; uninstall = manually delete) and new plugins are picked up on relaunch/list, not via a live install API. Add install/uninstall commands that register/unload plugins live (and, once executable plugins exist, hot-load the WASM module without restart).

requirements.title: PL-008 — Revenue-share model for paid plugins (post-GA)
requirements.evidence: MarketplaceController implements Stripe Connect destination charges: Checkout computes application_fee_amount = price * (1 - RevenueShareBps/10000), routes remainder to publisher via TransferData.Destination; ConnectOnboarding (Express account onboarding), ConnectStatus (charges/payouts readiness gating checkout), Webhook flips purchases to paid and handles disputes, Refund with ReverseTransfer + RefundApplicationFee. MarketplaceListing.RevenueShareBps defaults 7000 (70% to publisher). Note this applies to paid CONTENT listings; the same engine would cover paid plugins once plugins are sellable.
requirements.gap: Functionally complete for the marketplace; only caveat is it is wired to content listings, not 'plugins' specifically (because executable plugins are not yet sellable artifacts). When plugin listings exist, reuse this engine. Effort to extend is small.

area: PRD v2 §14.4 — Rulebooks and Prompt Engineering System (RB-001 … RB-023)
summary: RadioPad has a genuinely substantial rulebook subsystem on the locked .NET 8 / Next.js stack: a YAML rulebook model (RadioPad.Validation/Rulebook/RulebookSpec.cs), a rich deterministic validation engine (ReportValidator.cs, ~35 rule resolvers incl. RADS families), full CRUD/approve/deprecate/rollback API (RulebooksController.cs), a visual + YAML frontend editor (frontend/app/rulebooks/...), a CLI validate/test/approve toolchain, versioned validation packs (ValidationPackService.cs + packs CLI), a production gate that blocks non-Approved rulebooks (ReportingService.ResolveRulebookEntityAsync + RulebookGovernanceException, RB-010), CI that validates every YAML and runs every golden-case suite (.github/workflows/ci.yml), and a marketplace publish/install pipeline (MarketplaceController.cs). Of the 23 requirements, roughly 8 are implemented, 6 partial, and 9 missing. The biggest concrete gaps: RB-009 (the AI audit event in AiGateway.cs does NOT record rulebook id/version — only provider/model/hashes), no rulebook `extends:` inheritance/composition (RB-016; RB-007 is satisfied only by department-tag row selection, not YAML inheritance), no .rpkg signed bundle import/export (RB-019), no per-rulebook PHI policy override (RB-020), no Ed25519 signing/daemon verification of rulebooks specifically (RB-012 — Ed25519 infra exists but is wired to PACS/MCP plugins, not rulebooks), no semantic diff + reviewer comments (RB-013), no rulebook telemetry (RB-017), no deprecation grace period (RB-021), and no cross-tenant masked share (RB-022). Several anatomy fields from §14.4.1 (extends, clinical_owners, license, structured_fields, per-rule implementation, evidence_links, embedded test_cases, signed_by, applies_to.procedures/age_ranges) are not modeled in RulebookSpec. NOTE: a doc (docs/05-clinical/rulebook-authoring.md) references RulebookSpec.WithPromptOverrides which does not exist in code (doc drift).

requirements.title: RB-001 — Create, edit, clone, archive, version rulebooks (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: Create/edit/version implemented: RulebooksController.Save (POST /api/rulebooks) parses YAML via RulebookSpec.FromYaml and persists a Rulebook row (backend/.../Controllers/RulebooksController.cs L52-80); each Save can carry a new Version; frontend editor + list page allow create/edit (frontend/app/rulebooks/editor/RulebookEditorClient.tsx, page.tsx). Versioning is row-per-version keyed by (RulebookId, Version). 'Archive' is approximated by Deprecate (RulebookStatus.Deprecated). No explicit Clone/duplicate endpoint or UI button, and no distinct Archive state separate from Deprecated.
requirements.gap: Add a Clone action (server-side copy of SourceYaml into a new draft row with bumped version, and a UI button on the list/detail pages). Decide whether Archive is distinct from Deprecated; if so add an Archived status + transition. ~Both are thin additions over existing CRUD.

requirements.title: RB-002 — YAML/JSON source editing AND visual editing (P1/CE)
requirements.evidence: YAML source editing: textarea + Save/Validate in frontend/app/rulebooks/page.tsx and [id]/RulebookDetailClient.tsx (YAML/Visual tabs). Visual editor: frontend/app/rulebooks/editor/RulebookEditorClient.tsx composes MetadataPanel/StylePanel/SectionsPanel/RulesPanel/PromptBlocksPanel with a live YAML preview driven by lib/rulebookYaml (rulebookToYaml/yamlToRulebookEditor). Detail page also has a read-only Visual summary parsed from YAML.
requirements.gap: JSON (vs YAML) source editing is not separately offered, but the PRD intent (source + visual) is met. Visual editor edits a structured subset; some §14.4.1 fields (structured_fields, evidence_links, test_cases) are not surfaced in the visual panels.

requirements.title: RB-003 — Approval workflow Draft→Review→Approved→Deprecated with electronic signatures (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: RulebookStatus enum has Draft/InReview/Approved/Deprecated (Domain/Enums/Enums.cs L26-32). Approve (POST /{id}/approve) and Deprecate (POST /{id}/deprecate) exist, RBAC-gated via RbacPermission.RulebooksApprove and audit-logged (RulebooksController.cs L128-168; RulebookGovernanceTests verifies the audit events). Status persisted on the entity.
requirements.gap: No transition INTO the InReview state (no submit-for-review endpoint for rulebooks; the enum value is defined but unused for rulebooks). No electronic signature captured on approval (no signer identity/cryptographic signature stored on the Rulebook; the §14.4.1 signed_by block is not modeled). Add a submit-for-review action and capture an e-signature (signer, timestamp, optionally Ed25519) at approval.

requirements.title: RB-004 — Test cases (golden cases) per rulebook with diff viewer (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: Golden cases exist on disk (rulebooks/_tests/<id>/*.json) and as versioned ValidationPacks (ValidationPackService.cs: import/export/run; ValidationPack entity). Runners: RulebooksController.RunTests (POST /api/rulebooks/test), CLI 'rulebook test --cases', ValidationPacks CLI 'packs run', and frontend admin/validation-packs page. Run output reports per-case missing/unexpected rule ids.
requirements.gap: No visual diff viewer comparing expected vs actual output text per case (only set-difference of fired rule ids is shown). Golden cases are stored separately (disk + ValidationPack rows), not embedded in the rulebook per §14.4.1 test_cases with must_contain/must_not_contain assertions — only must_pass_rules (expectFlagged) is supported. Add must_contain/must_not_contain assertions and a side-by-side diff UI.

requirements.title: RB-005 — Prompt blocks, output schemas, style rules, forbidden language, required sections, validation rules (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: RulebookSpec models PromptBlocks (dict), OutputSchema (type/required/properties+enum), Style (tone, impression_max_bullets, avoid_terms, approved_followups), RequiredSections, Rules (id/severity/description) — RulebookSpec.cs. ReportValidator enforces required sections, style.avoid_terms, ~35 rule resolvers incl. laterality/measurement/negation/critical-language/RADS families + unauthorized_followup; PromptBlocks are consumed by ReportingService.BuildPromptForMode with per-mode resolution.
requirements.gap: output_schema is parsed but no evidence it is enforced against AI output (no JSON-schema validation step found). Per-rule 'implementation: deterministic|hybrid|ai_assisted' from §14.4.1 is not parsed or honored — all rules run deterministically by id. Otherwise the clause is functionally covered.

requirements.title: RB-006 — Modality- and subspecialty-specific rulebooks (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: applies_to.modalities/body_parts/report_types modeled (RulebookSpec.AppliesToSpec) and persisted (Rulebook.AppliesToModalities/AppliesToBodyParts). 16+ shipped rulebooks span modalities/subspecialties (rulebooks/*.yaml: chest_ct, brain_mri, mammo_birads, prostate_pirads, liver_lirads, lung_lungrads, thyroid_us, cardiac_mri, etc.). Department scoping via Rulebook.DepartmentTag enables subspecialty selection.
requirements.gap: applies_to.procedures and applies_to.age_ranges from §14.4.1 are not modeled in AppliesToSpec (only modalities/body_parts/report_types). Selection is by modality+body_part+department, not procedure or age. Minor extension to add those filters.

requirements.title: RB-007 — Tenant/department/user inheritance with explicit override semantics (P1/CE)
requirements.evidence: Department override implemented: Rulebook.DepartmentTag + ReportingService.ResolveRulebookEntityAsync prefers a department-scoped sibling (same RulebookId, matching Report.DepartmentTag) over the tenant-wide row, never crossing tenants (ReportingService.cs L396-410). Tenant-level prompt overrides layer on top via IPromptOverrideStore (overrides win over rulebook prompt_blocks in BuildPromptForMode). Documented in docs/05-clinical/rulebook-authoring.md.
requirements.gap: Two of three inheritance levels: tenant + department covered; per-USER inheritance/override is not implemented. Override semantics are limited to row-selection + prompt-block overrides; there is no field-level merge/override of style/rules/sections. This is row-selection, not the §14.4.1 'extends' composition model (see RB-016). Doc references RulebookSpec.WithPromptOverrides which does not exist in code (doc drift).

requirements.title: RB-008 — Rollback to prior approved version (single-command) (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: POST /api/rulebooks/{id}/rollback {version}: RulebooksController.Rollback (L179-223) finds a prior Approved row with same RulebookId and materializes a new Approved copy versioned '<prior>+rollback-<ts>', never mutating history; RBAC-gated (RulebooksApprove) and audit-logged. Frontend detail page exposes a 'Rollback to…' dropdown of prior approved versions (RulebookDetailClient.tsx L122-137). CLI not required but API+UI single-action.
requirements.gap: None material. (Rolled-back version string carries a +rollback suffix rather than restoring the exact prior semver, which is a deliberate non-mutating design choice.)

requirements.title: RB-009 — Rulebook version captured in every AI event audit log (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: AiGateway.RouteAsync writes the AiResponse audit event with DetailsJson containing only provider/adapter/model/input+output hashes/latency — NO rulebook id or version (backend/.../Services/AiGateway.cs L127-146). Grep for RulebookVersion/rulebookId in AiGateway.cs returns no matches. ReportVersion entity has a RulebookVersion field but that is the report-edit snapshot, not the AI event. The rulebook entity is resolved in ReportingService but its id/version is not threaded into AiCompletionRequest or the audit detail.
requirements.gap: Thread the resolved rulebook RulebookId + Version (and ideally the prompt-block/prompt version) into AiCompletionRequest and include them in every AiResponse/ProviderBlocked/PolicyViolation audit DetailsJson. This is a P0 compliance/traceability gap and a focused change in ReportingService.RunAsync → AiGateway.RouteAsync.

requirements.title: RB-010 — Block unapproved rulebooks from production; sandbox mode explicit (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: ReportingService.RunAsync throws RulebookGovernanceException when the resolved rulebook Status != Approved unless Tenant.AllowSandboxRulebooks is true (ReportingService.cs L91-99; exception class in same assembly). Surfaced as 409 {kind:'rulebook_governance'} (PROGRESS iter-12). Sandbox is an explicit per-tenant flag (Tenant.AllowSandboxRulebooks default false). SandboxCompareController also requires this flag + Sandbox-class providers.
requirements.gap: None material for rulebooks. (Production gate is per-AI-run; there is no separate guard preventing a draft rulebook from being attached to a report, but the AI path — the production usage — is gated.)

requirements.title: RB-011 — Rulebook regression CI; promotion gated on golden-case pass rate ≥ threshold (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml 'cli' job validates every rulebooks/*.yaml ('rulebook validate') and runs every rulebooks/_tests/* suite ('rulebook test --cases'); the CLI test command sets a non-zero exit on any failing case (Program.cs BuildRulebookTestCommand L206 'if (passed != total) ExitCode=1'), so CI fails on any golden-case regression. ValidationPackService.RunAsync returns pass/fail summaries usable in pipelines.
requirements.gap: The gate is an implicit 100%-pass requirement, not a configurable threshold (e.g. ≥95%). There is no explicit 'promotion' pipeline that ties an approve/promote action to a pass-rate check — approval (RB-003) and CI regression are independent. Add a configurable threshold and wire the pass-rate result into the rulebook promotion/approve flow.

requirements.title: RB-012 — Cryptographic signing of approved rulebooks (Ed25519); daemon verifies before activation (P1/EE)
requirements.evidence: Ed25519 verification infrastructure EXISTS but is scoped to plugins/PACS/MCP manifests, not rulebooks: cli/RadioPad.Cli/PluginVerifier.cs (Ed25519 detached-sig verify via BouncyCastle), McpManifestVerifier/PluginManifestSignatureVerifier, desktop sandbox.rs. The Rulebook entity has no signature/signed_by fields; approval (RulebooksController.Approve) performs no signing; no daemon-side verify-before-activation for rulebooks; §14.4.1 signed_by is not modeled.
requirements.gap: Add an Ed25519 signature field + signer metadata to the Rulebook entity, sign on approval, and verify the signature in ReportingService.ResolveRulebookEntityAsync / daemon activation before a rulebook is used. The crypto primitives are already in-repo (reuse PluginVerifier patterns).

requirements.title: RB-013 — Rulebook diffing (semantic) with reviewer comments (P1/CE)
requirements.evidence: No semantic rulebook diff found (grep 'semantic diff'/'reviewer comment' → no code matches; only PRD/docs). The detail page shows YAML source and a parsed visual summary but no version-to-version diff. No comment entity/endpoint tied to rulebook review. (Generic text diff utilities are not applied to rulebooks.)
requirements.gap: Implement structured diff between two rulebook versions (sections/rules/prompt-blocks/style added/removed/changed) and a reviewer-comment thread persisted per version/field. Net-new feature.

requirements.title: RB-014 — Rulebook A/B testing in sandbox tenant (no PHI) (P2/EE)
requirements.evidence: SandboxCompareController (POST /api/ai/sandbox/compare) runs the same prompt across up to 4 Sandbox-class PROVIDERS side-by-side, gated on Tenant.AllowSandboxRulebooks and refusing non-Sandbox providers, while still enforcing PHI policy (SandboxCompareController.cs). Frontend admin/model-eval page drives provider comparison.
requirements.gap: This is provider A/B, not RULEBOOK A/B — there is no path to run two different rulebook versions against the same input and compare validation/draft outputs, and no dedicated no-PHI sandbox tenant construct. Extend the compare endpoint to accept two rulebook ids/versions and diff their validation+draft results.

requirements.title: RB-015 — Rulebook marketplace (publish/install/rate); curated endorsement badges (P2/CE)
requirements.evidence: MarketplaceController.cs implements full publish→submit→review(approve/reject)→catalogue→install lifecycle for rulebooks (and templates/prompt_packs), with Stripe Connect rev-share, install copies rulebook YAML into a Draft tenant rulebook (Install L667-733), InstallCount tracked, audit events (MarketplaceSubmission/Approved/Rejected/Installed). Frontend app/marketplace/page.tsx.
requirements.gap: No rating/review system (no rating entity/endpoint; only InstallCount). No curated 'clinical-society endorsement' badge concept on listings. Add ratings + a curation/endorsement badge field and surface in the catalogue.

requirements.title: RB-016 — Bilateral rulebook composition (compose by extends + override) (P1/CE)
requirements.evidence: RulebookSpec has NO 'extends' field (RulebookSpec.cs L10-23) and FromYaml ignores unmatched properties, so an 'extends:' line in YAML is silently dropped. Grep for extends/inherit/BaseRulebook in backend returns no composition logic (only unrelated text). The shipped chest_ct_v1.yaml etc. do not use extends. RB-007's department-tag selection is row-choice, not base+override composition.
requirements.gap: Add an 'extends: <id>@<version>' field to RulebookSpec, resolve the parent at load time, and deep-merge style/required_sections/rules/prompt_blocks/output_schema with explicit child-override semantics. Net-new composition engine.

requirements.title: RB-017 — Rulebook telemetry: which rules fire how often, which are most overridden (P1/CE)
requirements.evidence: No rule-firing/override telemetry found. ValidationResult returns per-run findings but nothing aggregates rule-fire counts over time; grep 'telemetry'/'fire how often'/'most overridden' yields only PRD/docs, no code. Audit log records validation events generically but there is no per-rule counter, and no concept of a rule being 'overridden' by the radiologist is tracked.
requirements.gap: Persist per-rule fire counts and per-rule override/dismiss events (radiologist acknowledging/ignoring a finding), then expose an analytics endpoint + governance dashboard panel. Net-new telemetry pipeline.

requirements.title: RB-018 — Rulebook editor with inline lint (YAML schema, prompt token estimation, breaking-change warnings) (P1/CE)
requirements.evidence: YAML schema lint exists: POST /api/rulebooks/validate (RulebooksController.ValidateYaml) returns problems for missing rulebook_id/name/version/required_sections and YAML parse errors; CLI 'rulebook validate'; editor + detail page surface problems inline (frontend editor handleValidate, detail validate()).
requirements.gap: No prompt token estimation in the editor (grep token/estimat in PromptBlocksPanel.tsx → none). No breaking-change warnings (e.g., removing a required section or changing a rule severity vs the prior approved version). Add a token-count estimator on prompt blocks and a diff-against-last-approved breaking-change check.

requirements.title: RB-019 — Rulebook import/export as a single signed .rpkg bundle (zip + signed manifest) (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: No .rpkg handling anywhere (grep rpkg across repo → only PRD/docs text, no code). Export paths that exist are unrelated (billing invoice ZIP via 'bundle export-invoices', validation pack JSON export, template export). Marketplace transfers raw YAML in a DB row, not a signed zip bundle. PluginVerifier handles signed plugin manifests but not rulebook bundles.
requirements.gap: Implement .rpkg create/import: zip the rulebook YAML + schemas + golden cases + a manifest, sign the manifest (reuse Ed25519/PluginVerifier), and add import that verifies the signature before installing. This is a P0 net-new packaging feature.

requirements.title: RB-020 — Per-rulebook PHI policy override (e.g., this rulebook may only use local models) (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: PHI policy is enforced only at the PROVIDER level: AiGateway.EnforcePhiPolicy checks ProviderComplianceClass (PhiApproved/LocalOnly/etc.) per the report's PHI content; there is no PHI-policy field on the Rulebook entity (Entities.cs Rulebook L371-392 has no phi/compliance field) and RulebookSpec has no phi_policy/local_only field. ReportingService does not constrain provider selection based on the rulebook.
requirements.gap: Add a per-rulebook PHI/compliance policy (e.g., RequiredComplianceClass = LocalOnly) to the entity + RulebookSpec, and enforce it in AiGateway/provider routing so a rulebook can pin itself to local-only models. P0 gap.

requirements.title: RB-021 — Rulebook deprecation grace period (default 90 days) with usage warnings (P1/CE)
requirements.evidence: Deprecate (POST /{id}/deprecate) flips Status to Deprecated immediately (RulebooksController.Deprecate L149-168) with no grace window, no DeprecatedAt/SunsetAt timestamp on the Rulebook entity, and no usage-warning emission when a deprecated-but-in-grace rulebook is used. A docs/08-user-docs/deprecation-policy.md exists but no enforcing code. (Note: AIDeprecation/grace concepts elsewhere relate to providers/models, not rulebooks.)
requirements.gap: Add DeprecatedAt + GraceDays (default 90) to the Rulebook entity, allow continued use during grace while emitting a warning finding/audit, and hard-block after the window. Net-new lifecycle behavior.

requirements.title: RB-022 — Cross-tenant share with selective masking (org-internal sharing) (P1/EE)
requirements.evidence: All rulebook queries are strictly tenant-scoped (RulebooksController + ReportingService use ResolveContextAsync / ListAsync(tenant.Id); ResolveRulebookEntityAsync explicitly 'never crosses tenant boundaries'). The only cross-tenant transfer path is the public marketplace (a copy-on-install), which is not selective org-internal sharing with masking. No share-grant entity or masking logic found.
requirements.gap: Add an org/tenant-group sharing grant for rulebooks with field-level masking (e.g., hide owner/clinical_owners or proprietary prompt text) for recipient tenants. Net-new EE feature; must preserve tenant isolation invariants.

requirements.title: RB-023 — Conformance test runner ensures rulebooks comply with RadioPad rulebook spec (P0/CE)
requirements.evidence: Schema/structural conformance is checked by RulebookSpec.FromYaml + RulebooksController.ValidateYaml + CLI 'rulebook validate' (required fields, parseability, at-least-one required_section), run over every rulebook in CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml validate loop). Severity normalization is enforced (RulebookSpec.NormalizeSeverity).
requirements.gap: Validation is shallow: it checks a handful of required top-level fields but not full spec conformance (no JSON-schema for the rulebook itself, no validation of rule ids against the engine's known resolvers, no check that referenced prompt blocks/output_schema/$ref exist, no §14.4.1-completeness check). Build a formal rulebook-spec schema + a conformance runner that validates structure, known rule ids, schema refs, and signature presence.

summary: RadioPad implements the spine of §17 — a per-tenant, append-only, hash-chained AuditEvent log with a working VerifyChain endpoint (UI + API + CLI), a retention worker honoring RetentionDays + LegalHold, and an AiRequest usage ledger that already stores input/output as hashes only. However, the implementation is materially shallower than the PRD specifies in three areas. (1) §17.1 entity catalog: ~13 of 21 named entities exist; six clinical/longitudinal entities (Critical Result, Peer Review, Teaching Case, Citation, Worklist Item, Lesion) and dedicated AI Response / Validation Result / Report Final entities are absent. (2) §17.2 audit schema: the rich structured event (uuidv7 event_id, ISO event_time, actor{auth_method}, subject{hashes}, ai{model_version,token counts,phi_class,input/output blake3}, validation[], chain{prev/this}, ed25519 signature) is collapsed into a flat {Action enum, DetailsJson} row; hashing is SHA-256 not blake3, there is NO per-event ed25519 signature, NO RFC 3161 timestamp anchoring, and NO Rekor-style transparency log. (3) §17.3 retention: retention is a single tenant-wide RetentionDays value, NOT configurable per data class; HashOnlyAuditMode is a stored/settable flag that is never enforced at write time; there is no right-to-delete workflow; the NDJSON/CEF export exists but is NOT a signed bundle. Per the CLAUDE.md stack-lock rule, items are judged on function (ed25519/blake3/Rekor naming is not penalized for being absent libraries — they are penalized because the FUNCTION, tamper-evident per-event signing and external anchoring, genuinely does not exist on any stack).

requirements.title: §17.1 Tenant entity (isolation boundary)
requirements.evidence: backend/RadioPad.Api/src/RadioPad.Domain/Entities/Entities.cs:12 `Tenant` with Slug/DisplayName + isolation flags (RequirePhiApprovedProvider, AllowExternalMcp); DbSet at RadioPadDbContext.cs:12; isolation enforced via TenantedController.ResolveContextAsync (referenced across controllers).
requirements.gap: None. Tenant is the canonical isolation boundary across the codebase.

requirements.title: §17.1 User entity (radiologist/admin/reviewer/IT/researcher/auditor)
requirements.evidence: Entities.cs:39 `User` (Role, IsActive, MFA, lockout) + GlobalUser:101 / ExternalIdentity:114 / TenantMembership:133 enterprise identity root. UserRole enum drives role-scoped access.
requirements.gap: PRD lists six personas; verify UserRole enum covers researcher/auditor distinctly (Radiologist/admin/reviewer/IT present). Minor taxonomy gap only.

requirements.title: §17.1 Role / Permission (fine-grained, tenant-scoped)
requirements.evidence: RolePermissionMap.cs / RolePermissionService.cs (IPermissionService); RbacPermission enum used in AuditController (AuditRead/AuditVerify/AuditExport) at OtherControllers.cs:594/649/689. Tenant-scoped via TenantedController.
requirements.gap: RBAC is a static role→permission map, not a per-tenant editable fine-grained grant table; 'fine-grained, tenant-scoped' custom roles are not persisted as entities.

requirements.title: §17.1 Device entity (studio install / daemon, signed cert)
requirements.evidence: DeviceAuthRequest (Entities.cs:755) implements RFC 8628 device pairing with DeviceFingerprint; AuthSession (Entities.cs:149) stores DeviceFingerprintHash; DevicesController at Iter31Controllers.cs:380.
requirements.gap: No first-class persistent Device entity with a signed device certificate; device identity is a fingerprint hash on auth/session rows, not a cert-bound enrolled device record. No X.509/signed-cert device attestation.

requirements.title: §17.1 Study Context (modality/body part/accession/patient ref HL7 PID + FHIR Patient.id)
requirements.evidence: StudyContext value object (Entities.cs:414): AccessionNumber, Modality, BodyPart, Indication, Comparison, PriorReportSummary, PatientReference; owned by Report.Study (Entities.cs:433). HL7/FHIR ingest populates it (IngestController, Hl7MessageHandler).
requirements.gap: PatientReference is a single string; PRD wants explicit HL7 PID + FHIR Patient.id binding. Functionally covered by ServiceRequestRef + PatientReference but not structured as two typed fields.

requirements.title: §17.1 Report Draft (CRDT document)
requirements.evidence: Report entity (Entities.cs:425) is the editable draft with sectioned fields + AiHighlightsJson; offline drafts at frontend/lib/offlineDrafts.ts.
requirements.gap: Draft is a plain mutable row, NOT a CRDT document — no conflict-free replicated data type / collaborative merge structure. Multi-editor concurrency relies on versions/signatures, not CRDT.

requirements.title: §17.1 Report Version (snapshot per edit/export with content hash)
requirements.evidence: ReportVersion (Entities.cs:464): Sequence, SnapshotJson, RulebookVersion, AuthorUserId, Action, IsAddendum; created on edit/addendum (ReportingService, ReportsLifecycleController).
requirements.gap: ReportVersion has NO content-hash field — PRD requires a per-version content hash. Snapshot is stored but not cryptographically fingerprinted on the row.

requirements.title: §17.1 Report Final (immutable export record)
requirements.evidence: ReportSignature (Entities.cs:489) with SHA-256 Hash chains signatures (Primary/CoSigner/Addendum); export gate in ReportsLifecycleController; ReportStatus enum tracks Signed/Exported.
requirements.gap: No dedicated immutable 'Report Final' export-record entity capturing the exact exported artifact + destination + content hash. Finalization is inferred from status + signatures; the immutable export snapshot record does not exist as its own entity.

requirements.title: §17.1 Template (versioned, bound rulebook(s))
requirements.evidence: ReportTemplate (Entities.cs:394): TemplateId, Modality, BodyPart, SectionsJson, Variant, Status (Draft→Approved), ApprovedBy/ApprovedAt. TemplatesController lifecycle at OtherControllers.cs.
requirements.gap: Template→rulebook binding is via Report.RulebookId/TemplateId at report time, not an explicit bound-rulebook list on the template row. Versioning exists via Status but no semver field on template.

requirements.title: §17.1 Rulebook (versioned governance package)
requirements.evidence: Rulebook (Entities.cs:371): RulebookId, Version (semver), Status (Draft/InReview/Approved/Deprecated), SourceYaml, CompiledJson, DepartmentTag; governance + promotion in RulebooksController, RulebookGovernanceTests.
requirements.gap: None material — full versioned governance lifecycle exists.

requirements.title: §17.1 Prompt Block (reusable component)
requirements.evidence: PromptOverride (Entities.cs:782): RulebookId, BlockKey, Body, Status (Draft→Approved) with approval gate; consumed by ReportingService.BuildPromptForMode + EfPromptOverrideStore (IPromptOverrideStore).
requirements.gap: Prompt blocks are tenant overrides keyed to a rulebook, not free-standing globally-reusable components with their own versioning; functionally adequate.

requirements.title: §17.1 AI Request (input to provider)
requirements.evidence: AiRequest (Entities.cs:508): Provider, Model, Mode, ContainsPhi, InputHash, OutputHash, LatencyMs, InputTokens, OutputTokens, Status. Written per route call via IAiUsageStore.RecordAsync (Interfaces.cs:218).
requirements.gap: None for the request itself; see AI Response (it is merged into this same row rather than a separate entity).

requirements.title: §17.1 AI Response (output, token counts, latency, cost)
requirements.evidence: AiRequest row carries OutputHash, OutputTokens, LatencyMs (Entities.cs:519-523); cost computed downstream via ProviderConfig CostPer*KToken in UsageByProvider (Interfaces.cs:245).
requirements.gap: No separate AI Response entity; response is folded into AiRequest. Per-row cost is not persisted on the AI row (computed at summarise time). Acceptable functionally but diverges from PRD's distinct entity + stored cost.

requirements.title: §17.1 Validation Result (per check, severity, outcome, span refs)
requirements.evidence: ReportValidator.cs produces findings with severity (Blocker/Warning/Info); results surface in API/UI and are referenced in audit DetailsJson; ValidationPack golden suites (Entities.cs:1053).
requirements.gap: Validation results are not persisted as a dedicated entity with span references; they are recomputed and embedded in audit DetailsJson / responses. No stored per-check row with span anchors for longitudinal querying.

requirements.title: §17.1 Provider Config (compliance class, endpoints, credentials by ref)
requirements.evidence: ProviderConfig (Entities.cs:167): Adapter, Model, EndpointUrl, ApiKeySecretRef (ref not raw), Compliance class, cost/quality/retention-label, OAuth refresh vault (KMS-wrapped). Managed in ProvidersController.
requirements.gap: None material — credentials stored by reference; compliance class enforced in AiGateway.

requirements.title: §17.1 Audit Event (immutable, cryptographically chained per tenant)
requirements.evidence: AuditEvent (Entities.cs:526) + EfAuditLog.AppendAsync (Repositories.cs:20-58): per-tenant SHA-256 hash chain under a serializable transaction + tenant semaphore; VerifyChainAsync (Repositories.cs:70-98); UI verifier frontend/app/audit/verify/page.tsx.
requirements.gap: Chain exists and is per-tenant, but the EVENT is a flat {Action enum, DetailsJson} not the rich §17.2 schema, and there is NO per-event cryptographic signature (see §17.2 rows). Immutability is by-convention (no DB trigger / append-only constraint preventing UPDATE/DELETE).

requirements.title: §17.1 Subscription (plan, seats, usage, billing)
requirements.evidence: TenantSettings (Entities.cs:556) Plan/Stripe linkage + SubscriptionLifecycleService.cs + BillingController; PlanQuotaService enforces seats/usage; StripeWebhookEvent dedup (Entities.cs:863).
requirements.gap: Subscription state is spread across TenantSettings rather than a discrete Subscription entity; seats enforced via plan limits not an explicit seat ledger. Functionally complete.

requirements.title: §17.1 Critical Result (notification, ack, escalation chain)
requirements.evidence: No CriticalResult entity in Entities.cs; no DbSet in RadioPadDbContext.cs; grep for CriticalResult in backend/src and frontend returns no entity/page (only PACS-adapter false positives).
requirements.gap: Entire critical-results notification/acknowledgement/escalation-chain entity and workflow is absent.

requirements.title: §17.1 Peer Review (case, reviewer, score, rationale)
requirements.evidence: No PeerReview entity/DbSet; grep across backend/src + frontend returns no match.
requirements.gap: Peer-review entity and workflow entirely absent.

requirements.title: §17.1 Teaching Case (anonymized, categorized)
requirements.evidence: No TeachingCase entity/DbSet; no match in backend/src or frontend.
requirements.gap: Teaching-case entity (anonymization + categorization) entirely absent.

requirements.title: §17.1 Citation (bibliographic record, persistent identifier)
requirements.evidence: No Citation entity/DbSet; no match in backend/src or frontend.
requirements.gap: Citation entity with persistent identifier (DOI/PMID) entirely absent.

requirements.title: §17.1 Worklist Item (DICOM UPS or HL7-derived)
requirements.evidence: No WorklistItem entity/DbSet; HL7/DICOM ingest creates Reports directly (IngestController, Hl7MessageHandler) without a worklist abstraction. No worklist UI page.
requirements.gap: No dedicated Worklist Item entity (DICOM UPS / HL7-derived queue). Orders flow straight into Report drafts.

requirements.title: §17.1 Lesion (longitudinal cross-study with measurements)
requirements.evidence: No Lesion entity/DbSet; no longitudinal lesion tracking in backend/src or frontend (grep negative).
requirements.gap: Longitudinal lesion entity + cross-study measurement tracking entirely absent.

requirements.title: §17.1 Plugin (manifest, signature, capabilities, version)
requirements.evidence: McpTool (Entities.cs:895): ManifestJson, ManifestSha256, ManifestSig (Ed25519), Version, ScopeString, Status lifecycle; TrustedPluginPublisher (Entities.cs:1035) ed25519 publisher keys; PluginManifestSignatureVerifier.cs; desktop plugin-sdk manifest.schema.json.
requirements.gap: Plugin manifest+signature+version+capabilities are fully modeled and verified; minor: 'capabilities' is encoded as scope tokens rather than a typed capability list.

requirements.title: §17.2 Structured event envelope (event_id uuidv7, event_time, event_type)
requirements.evidence: AuditEvent uses Guid Id (Entity base, Guid.NewGuid() — v4, not v7), CreatedAt timestamp, and an AuditAction enum (Enums.cs:87) instead of a free-form dotted event_type string. AuditAction has ~40 members covering ai/report/rulebook/etc.
requirements.gap: event_id is UUIDv4 not uuidv7 (no time-ordered id); event_type is a fixed enum, not the PRD's open 'ai.request.completed | report.export.signed | ...' dotted namespace. event_time is CreatedAt (adequate, ISO-serializable).

requirements.title: §17.2 actor sub-object (user_id, role, device_id, auth_method)
requirements.evidence: AuditEvent has only UserId; no role/device_id/auth_method captured on the event. No phi_class/auth_method fields anywhere (grep negative). Actor context lives only in transient request, not stamped on the event.
requirements.gap: Structured actor block absent; role, device_id, and auth_method (webauthn|totp|saml|oidc) are not recorded per event. Could be carried in DetailsJson but is not done consistently.

requirements.title: §17.2 subject sub-object (report_id, study_ref hash, patient_ref hash, modality)
requirements.evidence: AuditEvent has ReportId; study/patient hashes + modality are not first-class columns. Some details land in DetailsJson per-call.
requirements.gap: No structured subject block; study_ref/patient_ref hashing and modality are not consistently stamped on every event.

requirements.title: §17.2 ai sub-object (provider, model_id, model_version, prompt_block_versions, rulebook_version, template_version, input/output blake3 hash, token counts, latency, phi_class)
requirements.evidence: AiRequest entity (separate from audit) holds Provider, Model, InputHash, OutputHash, InputTokens, OutputTokens, LatencyMs, ContainsPhi. ReportVersion holds RulebookVersion.
requirements.gap: Spread across AiRequest, not embedded in the audit event's ai block. Missing model_version, prompt_block_versions[], template_version, and phi_class (phi|de-identified|meta). Hashes are SHA-256 not blake3. No phi_class enum exists (grep negative).

requirements.title: §17.2 validation sub-object (results[] with rule/outcome/severity)
requirements.evidence: Validation findings are produced by ReportValidator and occasionally serialized into DetailsJson; AuditController dashboard counts contradiction/validation events.
requirements.gap: Validation results are not a structured, queryable array on the audit event; embedded ad-hoc in DetailsJson at best.

requirements.title: §17.2 user_action (accepted | edited | rejected)
requirements.evidence: ReportAcknowledged (AuditAction=4) + AiHighlightsJson acknowledgement flow + ReportEdited capture acceptance/edit; AiRequest.Mode tracks draft/rewrite.
requirements.gap: No single normalized user_action field (accepted/edited/rejected) on the AI/report audit event; acceptance is inferred from separate action types.

requirements.title: §17.2 export sub-object (destination ris|pdf|docx|fhir|hl7v2|none, destination_id)
requirements.evidence: ReportExported audit action (=3); exporters exist (FhirDiagnosticReportSerializer, Hl7OruSerializer, ReportDocumentRenderer for pdf/docx). Destination likely recorded in DetailsJson at export time.
requirements.gap: No structured export{destination,destination_id} block standardized on the event; destination metadata is free-form in DetailsJson, not a typed enum field.

requirements.title: §17.2 chain sub-object (prev_event_hash, this_event_hash) — per-tenant hash chain
requirements.evidence: EfAuditLog.AppendAsync (Repositories.cs:32-40): reads prior tenant IntegrityChain, computes SHA-256 over `{Id}|{TenantId}|{(int)Action}|{DetailsJson}|{prev}`, stores in IntegrityChain. VerifyChainAsync recomputes (Repositories.cs:70-98). Serializable tx + per-tenant lock prevent races.
requirements.gap: Functionally satisfies prev/this hash chaining per tenant. Divergence from PRD: single IntegrityChain column conflates prev+this (prev is not separately stored), and digest is SHA-256 not blake3. The function (tamper-evident chain) is present — judged implemented per stack-lock rule.

requirements.title: §17.2 Per-event signature (ed25519)
requirements.evidence: AuditEvent has no signature field; no ed25519 signing of audit events anywhere (ed25519 is used only for MCP/plugin manifests in PluginManifestSignatureVerifier, not for audit). grep ed25519 over audit code = none.
requirements.gap: No cryptographic per-event signature at all — the hash chain proves ordering/integrity but not authorship/non-repudiation. The FUNCTION (signed events) is absent on any stack.

requirements.title: §17.2 Periodic anchoring to external timestamping authority (RFC 3161)
requirements.evidence: No RFC 3161 / timestamp-authority client; grep '3161|TimestampAuthority|anchor' yields only docs/PRD mentions, no code. No background job submits chain heads to a TSA.
requirements.gap: External tamper-evidence anchoring entirely absent — chain can be wholly rebuilt by a DB-admin without external detection. Function missing on any stack.

requirements.title: §17.2 EE transparency log inclusion (Rekor-style)
requirements.evidence: No Rekor/transparency-log integration; grep 'Rekor|transparency' yields only doc/PRD references, no client or inclusion-proof storage.
requirements.gap: Enterprise transparency-log inclusion entirely absent. Function missing on any stack.

requirements.title: §17.3 Tenant-configurable retention PER data class (drafts, finals, AI logs, audit, telemetry)
requirements.evidence: TenantSettings.RetentionDays (Entities.cs:642) + RetentionWorker.cs purges AiRequests and ReportVersions older than one global cutoff; audit events explicitly never purged.
requirements.gap: Retention is a SINGLE tenant-wide RetentionDays applied to AI logs + report versions only. No per-data-class policy (separate windows for drafts vs finals vs AI logs vs telemetry). Finals/telemetry classes not addressed.

requirements.title: §17.3 PHI minimization default (do not retain raw AI input/output beyond N days)
requirements.evidence: AiRequest stores only InputHash/OutputHash — raw bodies are never persisted (Entities.cs:518-519, confirmed by RetentionWorker.cs:13 comment 'already PHI-minimised — only input/output hashes are stored, never the bodies'). McpToolCall (Entities.cs:952) likewise hash-only.
requirements.gap: Effectively exceeds the default — raw AI bodies are never stored at all, so 'beyond N days' is moot. Compliant. Minor: no per-tenant toggle to opt INTO retaining raw bodies for tenants whose policy requires it.

requirements.title: §17.3 'Hash-only audit' mode (store only hashes of input/output)
requirements.evidence: TenantSettings.HashOnlyAuditMode flag exists (Entities.cs:643), is surfaced in GET /api/tenant/settings and settable via POST (OtherControllers.cs:1602/1698), persisted in migrations.
requirements.gap: The flag is NEVER READ/ENFORCED at audit-write time — grep shows HashOnlyAuditMode is only get/set, never branched on in EfAuditLog or AiGateway. Because AI bodies are already hash-only by default, the toggle is currently a no-op dead setting rather than an enforced mode.

requirements.title: §17.3 Legal hold flag overrides retention
requirements.evidence: TenantSettings.LegalHold (Entities.cs:644); RetentionWorker.cs:73 filters `!s.LegalHold` so a held tenant is short-circuited entirely (no deletions). Documented in worker header comment.
requirements.gap: None for the retention-override behavior. Legal hold is tenant-wide only (not scoped to a specific report/case), which is coarser than typical legal-hold granularity but satisfies the clause.

requirements.title: §17.3 Right-to-delete workflow (with audit of the delete itself)
requirements.evidence: No right-to-delete / GDPR erasure endpoint or workflow; grep 'right-to-delete|RightToDelete' yields only PRD/docs (eu-aiact-gdpr-profile.md) and api docs, no controller/service. The only patient-data deletion is the time-based RetentionWorker.
requirements.gap: No subject-initiated erasure workflow, no per-patient delete with its own audit entry. Retention purge does emit a RetentionPurge audit event, but on-demand right-to-delete is absent.

requirements.title: §17.3 Exportable compliance logs (NDJSON, signed bundle)
requirements.evidence: GET /api/audit/siem streams NDJSON (application/x-ndjson) and CEF (OtherControllers.cs:680-744); CLI `audit sync` writes resumable NDJSON with watermark (cli/RadioPad.Cli/Commands/AuditSync.cs).
requirements.gap: NDJSON export is present and good, but it is NOT a SIGNED bundle — no detached signature / manifest / sealed archive over the export. SIEM export also deliberately omits DetailsJson, so it is a metadata-only feed rather than a full signed compliance bundle.

area: PRD v2 §20 UX Requirements + §21 Billing & Subscription + §22 Analytics & KPIs
summary: RadioPad implements a strong, locked-stack slice of these three PRD sections. §21 Billing is the most complete area: a full Stripe surface exists (checkout with 14-day trial, customer portal, signed/deduped webhook, invoices list, refund with tenant-ownership check, AI-credit balance, plan-feature flags, plan quota enforcement with grace→suspend lifecycle, and BILL-003 bulk invoice export). §22 analytics computes nearly all product+governance KPIs from real data through AnalyticsService + AnalyticsController, with frontend dashboards at /analytics, /analytics/quality, /admin/usage and /admin/billing, plus a synthetic availability monitor for §22.3. §20 UX is partially realized: the canonical left-sidebar shell carries most §20.1 modules, the reporting workspace has Study Context/Editor/AI Actions/Validation/Prior/Export panels, the Tauri desktop registers global hotkeys + secure-clipboard, the Prompt Studio (§20.4) is largely complete, and Capacitor biometric+push helpers exist. The biggest gaps are: several §20.1 modules have NO page (Worklist, Peer Review, Teaching Files, Users & Roles, Integrations, Plugins, general Settings), no command palette (§20.3.1), no PACS mini-overlay, no Provenance Inspector / Notes panel, no TipTap (plain textareas), no live token counter or "Explain this prompt" mode, no mobile bottom-nav / critical-result free-text-+-biometric flow / DND push, and accessibility §20.6 is thin (6 LTR-only locales, NO RTL for AR/HE/UR, no high-contrast theme, no dyslexia font, no WCAG CI gate, no AI-streaming aria-live announcements). Several KPIs are proxies (edit distance hardcoded to 0; vTTQR = created→updated delta), and BILL alternates (Lago/Killbill), multi-currency PO invoicing, auto-recharge, edu/non-profit tier, and marketplace revenue share are absent. Verdict per CLAUDE.md stack-substitution rule: function-presence judged on the locked stack (Stripe counts for BILL-013's "open billing engine" intent only partially since no alternate engine exists; biometric/push count even though native, etc.).

requirements.title: §20.1 Web app navigation — primary modules present in shell
requirements.evidence: frontend/components/shell/nav.config.tsx defines sidebar groups (workspace/library/integrations/admin). Present: Reports (Dashboard), Validation Center (/validation), Audit Logs (/audit), Analytics (/analytics), Rulebooks, Templates, Prompt Studio (/prompts), Marketplace, Terminology, AI Providers (/providers), Copilot, PACS, FHIR import, Offline, Governance, Model-Eval, Security, Feature Flags, Billing (/admin/billing), Usage (/admin/settings). Reporting Workspace = report editor reached via Reports rows.
requirements.gap: No dedicated nav entries/pages for: Worklist, Peer Review, Teaching Files, Users & Roles, Integrations (consolidated), Plugins (separate from Marketplace), and a general Settings page (only /admin/settings exists). Glob for app/{worklist,peer-review,teaching,users,roles,integrations,plugins,settings} returned no files.

requirements.title: §20.2 Reporting Workspace — Study Context panel
requirements.evidence: frontend/app/reports/[id]/ReportClient.tsx renders a 'Study context' panel with modality, body part, indication, rulebook selector, template, AI provider.
requirements.gap: No prior-report summary line or RVU class field in the Study Context panel as the PRD table specifies.

requirements.title: §20.2 Reporting Workspace — Editor (TipTap, section-aware, structured fields)
requirements.evidence: ReportClient.tsx SECTIONS array (indication/technique/comparison/findings/impression/recommendations) each rendered as a <textarea>; section-aware and structured-by-section.
requirements.gap: Editor is plain <textarea>, not TipTap/rich structured editor. No rich-text, no inline source-span highlighting inside the editor body, no structured field widgets.

requirements.title: §20.2 Reporting Workspace — AI Actions panel (generate/rewrite/summarize/impression/validate/compare/evidence)
requirements.evidence: ReportClient.tsx toolbar: Generate impression (runAi), Rewrite menu (concise/formal/patient_friendly/referring_summary), 'Rewrite in my style', 'Compare prior', Validate, Voice Cmds, Copy-to-RIS, exports, Acknowledge & lock.
requirements.gap: No explicit 'summarize' or 'evidence' action surfaced; 'compare' is a prior-compare toggle not a general AI compare. No grouped AI Actions side panel — actions are inline toolbar buttons.

requirements.title: §20.2 Reporting Workspace — Rulebook Panel (active rules, required fields, 'why this suggestion?')
requirements.evidence: ReportClient.tsx only has a rulebook <select> in Study Context; no panel listing active rules/warnings/required fields or a 'why this suggestion?' explainer.
requirements.gap: Build a Rulebook side panel showing active rules, required fields, and per-suggestion rationale.

requirements.title: §20.2 Reporting Workspace — Prior Report Panel (findings, semantic diff, lesion tracker)
requirements.evidence: frontend/app/reports/[id]/PriorComparePanel.tsx (RPT-009) shows current vs most-recent prior in a 2-col grid with rp-diff-add/rp-diff-remove highlighting; backend /compare-prior with fallback to /prior.
requirements.gap: Diff is per-section changed/unchanged flag, not true semantic diff; no lesion tracker (longitudinal measurement tracking).

requirements.title: §20.2 Reporting Workspace — Validation Panel (contradictions, missing sections, style, unsupported claims, source-span)
requirements.evidence: ReportClient.tsx Validation panel groups findings by severity (Blocker/Warning/Info) with quality score badge; UnsupportedClaimFinding renders quoted offending sentence in .ai-mark with 'Edit Findings' scroll-to (source-span proxy). Severity→color map honored.
requirements.gap: Source-span is sentence-level quote, not token-level highlight inside the editor.

requirements.title: §20.2 Reporting Workspace — Export Panel (Copy, PDF, DOCX, FHIR, HL7 v2, RIS)
requirements.evidence: ReportClient.tsx export buttons: Export text/JSON/FHIR/PDF/DOCX (gated on Acknowledged/Exported) + CopyToRisButton.tsx for RIS copy.
requirements.gap: No HL7 v2 export button in the workspace export panel (PRD lists HL7 v2). Exports are toolbar buttons, not a dedicated Export panel.

requirements.title: §20.2 Reporting Workspace — Worklist (filterable queue with priority)
requirements.evidence: frontend/app/page.tsx Dashboard provides a filterable report list (search, status, modality filters, pagination).
requirements.gap: No priority column/sort, no dedicated Worklist module; it is the generic Reports list, not a priority-ordered worklist queue.

requirements.title: §20.2 Reporting Workspace — Notes / Comments collaboration sidebar
requirements.evidence: No notes/comments component found in ReportClient.tsx or components/.
requirements.gap: No collaboration sidebar for notes/comments on a report.

requirements.title: §20.2 Reporting Workspace — Provenance Inspector (token-level provenance graph, model, prompt, rulebook)
requirements.evidence: Grep for provenance/token-level/provenanceGraph in frontend returned no files. Backend stores AiRequest trace fields (PromptVersion/Provider/Model/InputHash/OutputHash) used for audit completeness, but no UI inspector.
requirements.gap: No Provenance Inspector UI; token-level provenance graph not surfaced anywhere.

requirements.title: §20.3.1 Global hotkey opens command palette in ≤200 ms
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/main.rs registers Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R/N/I/W/D/C global shortcuts emitting events; no command-palette UI. Grep for command palette/cmdk in frontend returned no matches.
requirements.gap: No command palette component; hotkeys map to fixed actions, not a searchable palette. No ≤200ms perf assertion.

requirements.title: §20.3.2 Mini overlay floats above PACS without stealing focus
requirements.evidence: main.rs has no second/overlay window or always_on_top config; grep for overlay/always_on_top found no implementation.
requirements.gap: No floating mini-overlay window above PACS.

requirements.title: §20.3.3 Dictation starts within ≤300 ms of hotkey
requirements.evidence: main.rs Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+D emits radiopad://dictate; ReportClient.tsx listens and routes to mobileDictateHref. Mobile dictation via @capacitor-community/speech-recognition (mobile/package.json).
requirements.gap: Hotkey routes to a dictation page (navigation), not an in-place ≤300ms dictation start in the desktop workspace; no latency budget/measurement.

requirements.title: §20.3.4 AI actions accessible by keyboard alone
requirements.evidence: Desktop global shortcuts for generate-impression/rewrite/dictate; rewrite menu uses aria-haspopup/aria-expanded; buttons are focusable.
requirements.gap: In the web app (no desktop shell) there is no keyboard shortcut layer for AI actions; full keyboard reachability/logical order not verified or CI-gated.

requirements.title: §20.3.5 Secure paste into PACS with timeout indicator
requirements.evidence: main.rs secure_copy writes clipboard then clears after ttl_ms, emits radiopad://clipboard-cleared; clear-on-blur option. CopyToRisButton.tsx for RIS copy.
requirements.gap: Clipboard TTL exists but no visible countdown/timeout indicator in the UI for the secure paste.

requirements.title: §20.3.6 Offline mode clearly indicated (badge + tooltip + tray icon)
requirements.evidence: frontend/app/offline/page.tsx exists; DesktopStatusBanner.tsx surfaces desktop/offline status; offline_drafts.rs encrypted draft store.
requirements.gap: No tray icon indicator; badge+tooltip+tray triad not fully realized (no system tray entry in main.rs).

requirements.title: §20.3.7 Provider/compliance mode visible (e.g. 'Local-only' badge) but not intrusive
requirements.evidence: BillingStatusBanner.tsx and plan badges exist; providers page shows provider config; AiGateway enforces PHI policy (ProviderPolicyException).
requirements.gap: No explicit non-intrusive 'Local-only'/compliance-mode badge shown in the workspace chrome tied to the active provider.

requirements.title: §20.3.8 Recovery from sleep/suspend resumes last draft state
requirements.evidence: Report editor auto-saves on blur (update() patch) and desktop offline_drafts.rs persists encrypted drafts.
requirements.gap: No explicit sleep/suspend resume handler restoring the exact last in-memory draft/cursor state; relies on autosave + reload.

requirements.title: §20.4 Prompt Studio — visual prompt-block editor with live token counter
requirements.evidence: frontend/app/prompts/page.tsx left pane Prompt Block Editor (extractPromptBlocks, editable textareas, save draft, add custom block).
requirements.gap: No live token counter on prompt blocks.

requirements.title: §20.4 Prompt Studio — rulebook preview + sample outputs against golden cases
requirements.evidence: prompts/page.tsx Golden Cases tab runs api.promptStudio.testGolden returning per-case expected/actual rules, score, pass/fail.
requirements.gap: None material; golden-case run is present.

requirements.title: §20.4 Prompt Studio — test-case runner with pass/fail and diff
requirements.evidence: prompts/page.tsx Test Runner tab (handleRunTest → validate → quality score + findings) and Golden Cases pass/fail table.
requirements.gap: Test runner validates against the first existing report as context rather than isolated test fixtures.

requirements.title: §20.4 Prompt Studio — output diff viewer (semantic + literal)
requirements.evidence: prompts/page.tsx Output Diff tab loads override versions and renders computeDiff (lib/textDiff.ts) with rp-diff-add/remove.
requirements.gap: Diff is literal line-diff only; no semantic diff mode.

requirements.title: §20.4 Prompt Studio — golden-case library editor
requirements.evidence: Golden Cases tab can run cases; backend has golden-case test support (api.promptStudio.testGolden).
requirements.gap: No in-UI editor to create/edit golden cases (run-only).

requirements.title: §20.4 Prompt Studio — approval workflow with electronic signatures
requirements.evidence: prompts/page.tsx Approval tab: Medical Director can Approve drafts (api.promptOverrides.approve), status badges, approvedAt timestamp; isMedicalDirector RBAC gate.
requirements.gap: Approval records approver+timestamp but no cryptographic electronic-signature attestation on the prompt approval.

requirements.title: §20.4 Prompt Studio — prompt/rule version comparison and rollback
requirements.evidence: listVersions + diff comparison present (Output Diff tab loads version history).
requirements.gap: No rollback action to restore a prior version from the Prompt Studio UI.

requirements.title: §20.4 Prompt Studio — 'Explain this prompt' plain-language mode
requirements.evidence: No 'explain'/plain-language translation feature in prompts/page.tsx.
requirements.gap: Add an 'Explain this prompt' mode that renders a plain-language description of the prompt text.

requirements.title: §20.5 Mobile — one-handed reachable bottom navigation
requirements.evidence: Mobile pages exist (app/mobile/dictate, reports/edit, reports/sign) but grep for bottom-nav/MobileBottomNav/tabbar found no component.
requirements.gap: No bottom navigation bar for mobile companion.

requirements.title: §20.5 Mobile — push notifications respect DND except tenant-defined critical classes
requirements.evidence: frontend/lib/push.ts registers APNs/FCM tokens (PHI-free payloads), backend api.push.registerDevice; capacitor.config.ts PushNotifications alert/badge/sound.
requirements.gap: No DND-aware delivery logic or tenant-defined critical-class bypass; backend critical-result push channel with DND exceptions not implemented.

requirements.title: §20.5 Mobile — biometric unlock; lock screen shows only non-PHI badge
requirements.evidence: frontend/lib/biometric.ts (MOB-008) wraps @aparajita/capacitor-biometric-auth: checkBiometry, gateAuthTokenWithBiometric releases cached token only after biometric success.
requirements.gap: Biometric app-unlock is implemented, but no enforcement that the lock screen / background snapshot shows only a non-PHI badge (no privacy-screen overlay).

requirements.title: §20.5 Mobile — critical-result acknowledgement with mandatory free-text + biometric
requirements.evidence: app/mobile/reports/[reportId]/sign/MobileSignClient.tsx has mandatory review checkboxes + warning-ack checkbox before acknowledge/export, but no critical-result workflow, no mandatory free-text field, and no biometric step on acknowledgement.
requirements.gap: Implement a critical-result acknowledgement requiring biometric AND a mandatory free-text confirmation.

requirements.title: §20.6 Accessibility — WCAG 2.2 AA conformance is CI-gated
requirements.evidence: PROGRESS.md references a manual '07-accessibility-audit.md' WCAG 2.1 AA static review with 13 findings; no automated WCAG CI gate (no axe/pa11y job found).
requirements.gap: Add automated WCAG 2.2 AA checks in CI (e.g. axe-core/pa11y) and fix the audit findings.

requirements.title: §20.6 Accessibility — color never the sole carrier of meaning
requirements.evidence: Severity findings carry text labels + badges (blocker/warning/info text) alongside color in ReportClient.tsx and MobileSignClient.tsx; status badges include text.
requirements.gap: Not formally verified across every surface, but the pattern is consistently followed.

requirements.title: §20.6 Accessibility — keyboard reachability in logical order + labeled controls
requirements.evidence: Form controls carry aria-label/htmlFor (page.tsx search/filter inputs; ReportClient selects; bulk-export labels in admin/billing).
requirements.gap: No skip-link, no documented focus order, no automated keyboard-traversal test; AppShell.tsx has no skip-to-content link.

requirements.title: §20.6 Accessibility — screen-reader announcements for AI streaming ('AI is generating'… 'AI completed')
requirements.evidence: Grep for aria-live/'AI is generating'/'AI completed' across frontend found only role=status/alert in error/empty/skeleton components — none for AI streaming progress in ReportClient.tsx (aiBusy only toggles button label).
requirements.gap: Add an aria-live polite region announcing AI generation start/completion.

requirements.title: §20.6 Accessibility — high-contrast theme switchable per-user
requirements.evidence: Grep for prefers-contrast/high-contrast in globals.css returned no matches; CLAUDE.md forbids dark mode but high-contrast theme is a distinct a11y need and is absent.
requirements.gap: Add a per-user high-contrast theme toggle (tokens permitting).

requirements.title: §20.6 Accessibility — dyslexia-friendly font option
requirements.evidence: Grep for dyslexi/OpenDyslexic returned no matches in frontend.
requirements.gap: Add a per-user dyslexia-friendly font option.

requirements.title: §20.6 Accessibility — RTL layouts complete for AR/HE/UR locales
requirements.evidence: frontend/lib/i18n.ts SUPPORTED_LOCALES = en/es/de/fr/pt/hi (all LTR); messages/ has only those 6. Grep for dir=/rtl/direction:rtl in frontend returned no matches.
requirements.gap: No AR/HE/UR locale bundles and no RTL (dir=rtl) layout support.

requirements.title: BILL-001 Seat-based subscription
requirements.evidence: BillingController.cs Checkout creates a subscription-mode Stripe session; PlanLimits.cs encodes Seats per plan (Trial 5 / Team 25 / Enterprise unlimited); SubscriptionLifecycleService applies subscription status.
requirements.gap: Seat count is a plan ceiling, not actively metered/enforced per-active-user at checkout quantity time.

requirements.title: BILL-002 Usage-based AI credits
requirements.evidence: BillingController.Credits returns plan limits/used/remaining for calls+input/output tokens via PlanQuotaService.CheckAsync; /admin/billing 'AI credit usage this period' panel renders it; PlanQuotaService enforces monthly AI-call/token caps (402 mapping).
requirements.gap: Credits are quota-style caps, not purchasable metered credit balances; no credit top-up.

requirements.title: BILL-003 Enterprise invoicing (PO, net 30/60, multi-currency)
requirements.evidence: BillingController.InvoicesExport (Iter-30) bulk-exports invoices as CSV or ZIP(+PDF+SHA-256 manifest); Invoices endpoint lists Stripe invoices with currency; admin/billing bulk-export panel.
requirements.gap: No purchase-order field, no net-30/60 terms configuration, no multi-currency selection/quoting flow — only Stripe's default currency per customer.

requirements.title: BILL-004 Tenant-level usage dashboard
requirements.evidence: frontend/app/admin/usage/page.tsx renders last-30-days + month-by-month tables (active users, AI requests, tokens) and per-model breakdown via /api/usage/summary + /api/usage/analytics; tenant-scoped (UsageController.ResolveContextAsync).
requirements.gap: reportsTotal/reportsExported columns are hardcoded 0 in the per-month rows (full report counts only on main analytics page).

requirements.title: BILL-005 Provider cost attribution
requirements.evidence: PROGRESS.md + analytics page: IAiUsageStore.SummariseAsync joins each byProvider row to ProviderConfig pricing emitting costInputUsd/costOutputUsd/costTotalUsd + unpriced flag; /admin/usage and /analytics render per-provider Cost (USD); analytics ProviderCostPerReport = aiUsage.CostTotalUsd / completedReports.
requirements.gap: None material.

requirements.title: BILL-006 Plan-based feature flags
requirements.evidence: BillingController.Features returns per-plan flags (scim, siemExport, marketplacePublish, advancedAnalytics, stripeConnect, customKms, ipAllowlist, priorCompare, voiceDictation, mcpReadOnly); /admin/billing 'What's included' panel gates UI; frontend /admin/feature-flags page exists.
