requirements.gap: Flag set is hardcoded per plan in the controller rather than data-driven; fine for current scope.

requirements.title: BILL-007 Trial tenants and sandbox environments
requirements.evidence: BillingController.Checkout adds 14-day TrialPeriodDays for Trial-plan tenants; Status/Credits return trialEndsAt; /admin/billing renders trial countdown + ≤3-day warning banner (BILL-007 comment).
requirements.gap: Trial countdown + provisioning exist, but no separate sandbox environment concept; PROGRESS.md lists BILL-007 auto-trial provisioning UI as remaining.

requirements.title: BILL-008 Hard budget ceilings with grace + notifications
requirements.evidence: PlanQuotaService.CheckAsync blocks AI calls when monthly call/input/output caps hit; SubscriptionLifecycleService.MarkPaymentFailed opens a dunning grace window then escalates to suspension; /admin/billing shows grace + suspended banners; webhook handles invoice.payment_failed/succeeded.
requirements.gap: Ceilings are fixed plan quotas (no tenant-configurable hard $ budget); grace/notifications are payment-failure dunning, not budget-overrun notifications; no proactive 'approaching budget' email/notification.

requirements.title: BILL-009 Pre-paid credits + optional auto-recharge
requirements.evidence: No pre-paid credit balance or auto-recharge logic in BillingController.cs / PlanQuotaService.cs; credits are quota caps only.
requirements.gap: Implement purchasable pre-paid AI credit balances with optional auto-recharge.

requirements.title: BILL-010 Tax handling (Stripe Tax / VAT / GST)
requirements.evidence: BillingController.Checkout sets AutomaticTax = Enabled (Stripe Tax) on the checkout session.
requirements.gap: Relies on Stripe automatic tax only; no explicit VAT/GST id collection or validation flow surfaced.

requirements.title: BILL-011 Educational / non-profit pricing tier
requirements.evidence: TenantPlan enum is Trial/Team/Enterprise (PlanLimits.cs); no edu/non-profit tier in plans or feature flags.
requirements.gap: Add an educational/non-profit plan tier with appropriate limits/pricing.

requirements.title: BILL-012 Marketplace revenue share for plugin authors
requirements.evidence: stripeConnect feature flag exists (Enterprise) and FEATURE_LABELS maps it to 'Marketplace payouts', but no Stripe Connect onboarding, no revenue-share/payout calculation, no marketplace billing code found.
requirements.gap: Implement Stripe Connect onboarding + revenue-share payout engine for plugin authors.

requirements.title: BILL-013 Open billing engine — Lago or Killbill as alternates to Stripe
requirements.evidence: BillingController.cs is hard-coupled to Stripe SDK (Stripe.Checkout, BillingPortal, InvoiceService, RefundService); grep for Lago/Killbill found matches only in PRD/docs, not code.
requirements.gap: No pluggable billing-provider abstraction; no Lago/Killbill adapter. (Per CLAUDE.md, Stripe is the chosen engine, but BILL-013's intent of an open/alternate engine is unmet.)

requirements.title: §21.2 Usage metering — full metric set (seats, AI requests, tokens, dictation minutes, report gens, validation runs, storage, integration calls, daemon activations, rulebook test runs, plugin executions, federated rounds)
requirements.evidence: IAiUsageStore/EfAiUsageStore writes one AiRequest row per gateway call (status ok/blocked/error) with input/output tokens, latency, byProvider; UsageController.Summary/Analytics expose seats(plan), AI requests, tokens, report counts; template usage analytics endpoint exists.
requirements.gap: Not metered/surfaced: dictation minutes, validation runs, storage, integration calls, daemon activations, rulebook test runs, plugin executions, federated rounds. Only AI requests/tokens + report counts are tracked.

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — Draft acceptance rate
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService.ComputeAsync: AiDraftsAcknowledged / TotalAiGeneratedReports; OtherControllers.cs computes raw counts from AiRequests (distinct reportIds) and reports reaching Acknowledged; /analytics Kpi with rateSeverity vs 0.8.
requirements.gap: Acceptance = reached Acknowledged status (proxy for 'used after review'), not measured against actual reject events.

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — Impression acceptance rate
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService impressionAcceptanceRate = ImpressionsLightlyEdited / TotalAiGeneratedReports; rendered on /analytics.
requirements.gap: ImpressionsLightlyEdited is a proxy set equal to aiDraftsAcknowledged (OtherControllers.cs comment: 'assumed <20% edit') — not an actual edit-distance threshold on the impression.

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — Edit distance per section
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService computes editDistance = EditDistanceSum / EditDistanceSampleCount and /analytics renders 'Edit distance'.
requirements.gap: OtherControllers.cs sets EditDistanceSum=0 and EditDistanceSampleCount=0 (comment: 'edit-distance tracking requires version diff; future iteration') — so the KPI always reports 0; not actually computed.

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — vTTQR (verified time to quality report)
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService TurnaroundTimeImpact = MedianSecondsToAcknowledge and TimeSavedPerReport = avg seconds; OtherControllers.cs computes median/total from (UpdatedAt - CreatedAt) over acknowledged reports; rendered as 'TAT impact (median)' / 'Time saved / report'.
requirements.gap: Uses created→updated delta as a proxy for 'study open to clean export'; no true study-open timestamp, no baseline comparison to show -40% target.

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — Report validation pass rate
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService validationPassRate = ValidatedReports / TotalReports; OtherControllers.cs counts reports with status >= Validated; /analytics renders with rateSeverity vs 0.9.
requirements.gap: Pass = status reached Validated, not strictly 'passed all blocker rules at first validation' (proxy).

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — Contradiction detection rate
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService contradictionDetectionRate = ContradictionFindings/TotalReports*100; OtherControllers.cs counts AuditEvents(AiResponse) containing negation_conflict/laterality_consistency; rendered '/100'.
requirements.gap: Counts audit-event keyword hits rather than persisted finding rows; reasonable proxy.

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — Critical-finding capture rate
requirements.evidence: No critical-finding-capture KPI in AnalyticsService/ProductKpis; analytics page has no such metric.
requirements.gap: Add a critical-finding capture-rate KPI (confirmed criticals flagged / total).

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — Active radiologists (WAU/MAU)
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService ActiveRadiologists from raw.ActiveRadiologists; OtherControllers.cs counts distinct Radiologist users with AiRequests or report edits in window; rendered on /analytics and /admin/usage.
requirements.gap: Single active-count for the window, not split into WAU vs MAU.

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — Rulebook adoption
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService rulebookAdoption = ReportsWithRulebook / TotalReports; OtherControllers.cs counts reports with non-null RulebookId; rendered vs 0.8 threshold.
requirements.gap: Counts any attached rulebook, not specifically an APPROVED rulebook as the PRD target implies (≥90% under approved rulebook).

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — Provider cost per report
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService providerCostPerReport = aiUsage.CostTotalUsd / CompletedReports; /analytics renders 'Provider cost / report' to 4 decimals.
requirements.gap: None material.

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — TAT impact (turnaround before/after, -20% at 90 days)
requirements.evidence: TurnaroundTimeImpact (median seconds to acknowledge) rendered as 'TAT impact (median)'.
requirements.gap: No before/after baseline comparison or 90-day trend to express the -20% target; single median only.

requirements.title: §22.1 KPI — Peer-review discrepancy rate
requirements.evidence: No peer-review module/page and no discrepancy-rate KPI in AnalyticsService.
requirements.gap: Requires a Peer Review feature first; then a discrepancies/reviews KPI.

requirements.title: §22.2 Governance KPI — Unapproved-prompt usage attempts
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService GovernanceKpis.UnapprovedPromptUsage; OtherControllers.cs counts AuditEvents(PolicyViolation) with DetailsJson contains 'unapproved'; /analytics renders ok when 0.
requirements.gap: None material.

requirements.title: §22.2 Governance KPI — PHI policy violations blocked
requirements.evidence: GovernanceKpis.PhiViolationsBlocked from AuditEvents(ProviderBlocked) count; rendered on /analytics.
requirements.gap: None material.

requirements.title: §22.2 Governance KPI — Rulebook regression failures
requirements.evidence: GovernanceKpis.RulebookRegressionFailures from AuditEvents(ValidationPackRun) DetailsJson contains '"failed"'; rendered.
requirements.gap: None material.

requirements.title: §22.2 Governance KPI — Model drift alerts
requirements.evidence: GovernanceKpis.ModelDriftAlerts from AuditEvents(SystemAlert) DetailsJson contains 'model_drift'; rendered.
requirements.gap: Counts alerts; the drift-detection harness producing them is referenced in PROGRESS.md as Enterprise-GA polish (model-eval page exists).

requirements.title: §22.2 Governance KPI — Audit completeness (% AI events with complete provenance)
requirements.evidence: GovernanceKpis.AuditCompleteness = AiRequestsWithFullTrace / TotalAiRequests; OtherControllers.cs checks PromptVersion/Provider/Model/InputHash/OutputHash non-empty; rendered vs 0.95.
requirements.gap: None material.

requirements.title: §22.2 Governance KPI — Time-to-incident-acknowledgement
requirements.evidence: No incident-acknowledgement timing KPI in GovernanceKpis or analytics UI.
requirements.gap: Add a time-to-incident-ack metric (alert → first response).

requirements.title: §22.2 Governance KPI — Vulnerability MTTR / Open critical CVEs / SBOM freshness
requirements.evidence: No CVE-MTTR, open-critical-CVE, or SBOM-freshness KPIs in GovernanceKpis; these are supply-chain metrics not surfaced in the analytics dashboard.
requirements.gap: Surface dependency-security KPIs (CVE MTTR, open critical CVEs=0, SBOM age) — likely sourced from CI/SBOM tooling.

requirements.title: §22.3 Reliability KPI — Web app & AI gateway availability
requirements.evidence: AvailabilityMonitorService.cs (Iter-35 PERF-004) runs a 5-min rolling synthetic probe, computes error rate, emits metrics + availability_burn_rate SystemAlert; ObservabilityController exposes GET /observability/availability snapshot.
requirements.gap: Single-target health probe with a burn-rate alert; no per-edition availability SLA targets (99.95/99.9/99.5), no AI-gateway-specific availability number, no availability dashboard panel in the UI.

requirements.title: §22.3 Reliability KPI — Critical-result notification delivery (99.99% within SLA)
requirements.evidence: No critical-result notification delivery tracking; push (lib/push.ts) has no delivery-confirmation/SLA metric.
requirements.gap: Implement delivery tracking + SLA metric for critical-result notifications.

requirements.title: §22.3 Reliability KPI — Audit event durability (RPO ≤1 min; RTO ≤5 min)
requirements.evidence: Audit log is append-only (IAuditLog.AppendAsync) but no RPO/RTO measurement or backup/replication target surfaced.
requirements.gap: Define + measure RPO/RTO for the audit/control plane.

requirements.title: §22.3 Reliability KPI — Error budget burn (tracked weekly)
requirements.evidence: AvailabilityMonitorService computes a rolling error rate and a configurable burn-rate threshold (RADIOPAD_AVAILABILITY_BURN_RATE_THRESHOLD) raising alerts.
requirements.gap: Burn rate is a 5-min rolling window with alerting, not a weekly error-budget accounting/report.

area: NLP & AI Report Generation (PRD v2 §14.3, AI-001 … AI-028)
summary: RadioPad implements the §14.3 P0 "drafting + impression + validation" core well on the locked ASP.NET Core stack. The AiGateway (backend/.../Application/Services/AiGateway.cs) is the single egress point and enforces PHI routing policy, full audit/usage tracing (input/output SHA-256 hashes, prompt version, model, provider, tokens, latency), and tenant-month quota. Generation modes (impression, cleanup/dictation-to-structured, draft, rewrites) live in ReportingService + DictationCleanupService + ReportRewriteService. The deterministic ReportValidator covers required-section detection, laterality conflict, measurement mismatch, negation/contradiction, modality mismatch, and a deterministic HallucinationDetector covers unsupported-claim detection. Layered prompt overrides with an approval gate (AI-009), composite cost/quality/latency model routing (AI-010), and local providers (Ollama/llama.cpp/vLLM for AI-011) are all real. The biggest gaps are squarely in the AI runtime-control and EE-tier safety features that were never built: there is NO streaming/stop/regenerate (AI-013), NO centralized temperature ceiling plumbed through AiCompletionRequest (AI-014 — temperature is hardcoded only in 2 of ~7 adapters and absent from the OpenAI-family body), NO enforced structured-output / JSON-Schema binding (AI-015 — OutputSchema is parsed from YAML but never used), NO prompt-injection / adversarial-input detection (AI-017), NO per-request/per-user-day token budget (AI-018, only tenant-month quota + rate limit), NO Presidio-style pre-egress PHI scrubbing pass (AI-020 — PhiRedactor only masks log lines; PHI is handled by routing-block, not scrub-then-send), and the entire EE/advanced set is absent: watermarking (AI-016), ensemble + cross-provider disagreement (AI-019/AI-022), quote-it source citation (AI-021), multilingual translation provenance (AI-023), personal style memory (AI-024), and speculative field completion (AI-025). Note: per CLAUDE.md the stack is locked to .NET/Next.js, so absence of Go/Rust/Keycloak/Presidio-the-library is NOT counted against — only the missing FUNCTION is.

requirements.title: AI-001 — Convert raw dictation into clean structured report sections without altering clinical meaning
requirements.evidence: backend/RadioPad.Api/src/RadioPad.Application/Services/DictationCleanupService.cs (CleanupAsync) routes raw dictation through AiGateway, asks for a strict section-keyed JSON object (indication/technique/findings/impression/recommendations), parses it (ParseSections), and the system+default prompts explicitly require preserving 'every measurement, laterality, and negation exactly'. Exposed via ReportsController POST /api/reports/{id}/dictation/cleanup. ReportingService also has a 'cleanup' mode. PROGRESS.md line 1239/1350 marks AI-001 ✅.
requirements.gap: Meaning-preservation is enforced only by prompt instruction; there is no automated post-check that the cleaned output did not add/drop a measurement, side, or negation vs the raw dictation (the deterministic checks run only on the persisted report, not as a cleanup diff).

requirements.title: AI-002 — Generate report drafts from structured inputs, free text, measurements, prior snippets
requirements.evidence: ReportingService.BuildPromptForMode 'draft' branch builds a full structured draft (Indication/Technique/Comparison/Findings/Impression) from modality, body part, indication, comparison and dictation. Prior context is surfaced via ReportsController GET /api/reports/{id}/prior and /compare-prior, and StudyContext.PriorReportSummary is fed into context. RunAsync/RunAutoAsync dispatch via POST /api/reports/{id}/ai.
requirements.gap: Prior-report snippets are available to the UI/validation but are not automatically injected into the draft-generation user prompt (BuildPromptForMode 'draft' uses Study.Comparison text, not the retrieved prior report body). Structured measurement objects are extracted (MeasurementExtractionService) but not fed back into the draft prompt.

requirements.title: AI-003 — Generate impression from findings preserving clinical content
requirements.evidence: ReportingService.GenerateImpressionAsync → RunAsync(mode='impression'); BuildPromptForMode 'impression' = 'Generate a concise impression (max 5 bullets) faithful to the Findings section. Do not introduce new findings.' System prompt forbids inventing findings and mandates preserving laterality/measurements. Frontend ReportClient.tsx runAi('impression') marks the result with .ai-mark.
requirements.gap: Content preservation is enforced by prompt + the separate HallucinationDetector check, not by a hard guarantee; acceptable for P0 but not span-level traced (see AI-021/AI-028).

requirements.title: AI-004 — Detect contradictions between findings and impression
requirements.evidence: ReportValidator.CheckNegationConflict ('negation_conflict' rule) flags concepts denied in Findings but asserted in Impression; CheckLaterality and CheckLevelConsistency add side/level contradiction; CheckMeasurements flags impression measurements absent from findings. Wired through ReportingService.ValidateAsync and POST /api/reports/{id}/validate.
requirements.gap: Contradiction detection is regex/keyword-based on negation cues and specific concept lists; semantic contradictions without explicit 'no <concept>' phrasing (e.g., 'stable' vs 'enlarging', size up vs down) are not caught.

requirements.title: AI-005 — Detect missing required sections based on rulebook
requirements.evidence: ReportValidator.ValidateCore iterates rulebook.RequiredSections and emits a Blocker 'required_section:<name>' finding when a required section is missing/empty. RulebookSpec.RequiredSections parsed from YAML. Confirmed by ValidationTests / Iter35ValidationPackTests.
requirements.gap: None material. Only top-level named sections are checked; sub-section/field-level completeness within a section is not modeled.

requirements.title: AI-006 — Detect laterality conflicts, measurement mismatches, modality/body-part mismatch
requirements.evidence: ReportValidator: CheckLaterality ('laterality_consistency'), CheckMeasurements ('measurement_consistency', normalizes cm↔mm), CheckModalityMismatch ('modality_mismatch' — flags 'MRI' phrasing in a CT study etc.). Negated-side handling via HasAssertedSide. Rulebooks (e.g. chest_ct_v1.yaml, musculoskeletal_xr_v1.yaml laterality_flip test) exercise these.
requirements.gap: Body-PART mismatch (vs body REGION in the study context) is not explicitly checked — only modality phrase mismatch. Measurement mismatch is set-difference of normalized strings, so a value that changed slightly (5mm→6mm) reads as an orphan rather than a true 'mismatch with prior/finding'.

requirements.title: AI-007 — Detect uncertain/unsupported/hallucinated claims with confidence band, never shown as clinical certainty
requirements.evidence: HallucinationDetector.cs tokenises each impression sentence and emits 'ai:unsupported_claim' when token-support fraction against Findings+StudyContext+tenant allow-list is below TenantSettings.HallucinationMinSupport; severity/threshold/allow-list/on-off all tenant-configurable. Merged in ReportingService.ValidateAsync(...,settings,...). Frontend UnsupportedClaimFinding renders the sentence in .ai-mark with a 'Unsupported claim' warning badge (never as certainty). SupportFraction acts as a confidence-style band.
requirements.gap: It is a deterministic lexical-overlap heuristic, not an uncertainty/hedging-language detector; 'uncertain' phrasing per se (e.g. 'possibly', 'cannot exclude') is not classified, and the support fraction is overlap, not a calibrated confidence.

requirements.title: AI-008 — Suggest follow-up language using only tenant-approved phrasebooks
requirements.evidence: ReportingService.SuggestFollowUpAsync generates up to 3 phrases then, when RulebookSpec.StyleSpec.ApprovedFollowups is non-empty, drops any suggestion not on the allow-list and audits a PolicyViolation (hash-only) for each rejected line. ReportValidator.CheckUnauthorizedFollowup ('unauthorized_followup') flags typed recommendations off the list. Endpoint GET /api/reports/{id}/followup-suggestions. Iter32AiCompletenessTests cover positive/negative + audit.
requirements.gap: Allow-list lives only at rulebook style scope (ApprovedFollowups); there is no separate tenant-level phrasebook entity independent of a rulebook, and matching is exact normalized-string equality (no templated/parameterized phrases like 'follow-up in {N} months').

requirements.title: AI-009 — System/specialty/user/case-level prompts with layered overrides
requirements.evidence: ReportingService.BuildPromptForMode Resolve() layers tenant prompt-override (IPromptOverrideStore, approval-gated, only Approved bodies load) over rulebook PromptBlocks over hard-coded clinical default. PromptOverridesController + PromptOverrideStatus{Draft,Approved} + MedicalDirector approve endpoint (Iter32AiCompletenessTests).
requirements.gap: Only two effective layers exist (tenant-override + rulebook) on top of the system default. The PRD's four distinct scopes — specialty-level and per-USER and per-CASE prompt layers — are not modeled; overrides are keyed by (tenant, rulebookId, blockKey) only, with no user-id or case-id dimension.

requirements.title: AI-010 — Model routing by tenant, user role, modality, PHI class, cost ceiling, latency budget
requirements.evidence: EfProviderRouter.ScoreAsync (Repositories.cs) ranks enabled providers by a normalized composite of cost, quality and rolling-P95 latency (weights from TenantSettings.RoutingWeightsJson) with PHI-class eligibility filters (Blocked/PhiApproved/LocalOnly/DeIdentifiedOnly). Routing-preview endpoint GET /api/ai/routing/preview (ItAdmin). Per-tenant.
requirements.gap: Routing keys only on tenant + PHI class + cost/quality/latency scores. There is NO routing by USER ROLE and NO routing by MODALITY (these are not inputs to SelectAsync/ScoreAsync). Cost is a soft score, not a hard cost-CEILING cutoff; latency is a soft score, not a hard latency-BUDGET cutoff. estimatedInput/Output tokens are fixed constants (1000/500) in SelectAsync.

requirements.title: AI-011 — Local model execution for de-identified or PHI-sensitive workflows
requirements.evidence: Local provider adapters OllamaProvider.cs, LlamaCppProvider.cs, VLlmProvider.cs under Infrastructure/Providers/Local with tests (OllamaProviderTests, LlamaCppProviderTests, VLlmProviderTests). ProviderComplianceClass.LocalOnly is treated as PHI-eligible in AiGateway.EnforcePhiPolicy and EfProviderRouter eligibility. PROGRESS.md Iter32 Agent E marks AI-011 ✅.
requirements.gap: None material on the locked stack. Local adapters call a local HTTP endpoint (OpenAI-compatible) rather than in-process inference, which is the expected architecture.

requirements.title: AI-012 — Full traceability of prompt version, rulebook version, model id, provider, input hash, output hash, edits
requirements.evidence: AiGateway.RouteAsync audits AuditAction.AiResponse with provider, adapter, model, promptVersion, inputHash=SHA256(userPrompt), outputHash=SHA256(text), latency, input/output tokens, containsPhi; also writes an AiRequest usage row (IAiUsageStore) for ok/blocked/error (PROGRESS.md notes this closed the AI-012 gap). Report edits are versioned in ReportVersion snapshots (ReportsController.Patch).
requirements.gap: RULEBOOK VERSION is not part of the AI audit payload — the gateway records promptVersion + model but not the resolved rulebook id@version used for that run. Input hash is over UserPrompt only (system prompt and resolved override bodies are not hashed into the trace). Edit lineage (ReportVersion) is stored separately from the AI run, so a given AI output cannot be linked to the subsequent radiologist edits via a single trace id.

requirements.title: AI-013 — Streaming token output to UI with stop/regenerate controls
requirements.evidence: OpenAiChatHelpers.BuildChatBody hardcodes stream=false and SendChatAsync reads the full JSON response (no SSE). AiResult is a single materialized record (Text). Frontend ReportClient.tsx runAi() does a single awaited POST then sets the result; no EventSource/ReadableStream/getReader, no Stop button, no AbortController. api.ts runAi is a plain request.
requirements.gap: Entire feature absent: need a streaming endpoint (SSE/chunked) on the gateway+adapters, AiCompletionRequest streaming flag, a frontend token-stream renderer, a Stop (cancel) control wired to a CancellationToken, and a Regenerate control. 'Regenerate' today is only re-clicking Generate (no seed/variation handling).

requirements.title: AI-014 — Deterministic temperature ceiling for clinical outputs (default 0.2) overridable per rulebook
requirements.evidence: AwsBedrockProvider.cs (line 127) and GoogleVertexAiProvider.cs (line 67) hardcode temperature=0.2. AiCompletionRequest record (Interfaces.cs:35) has NO Temperature field; OpenAiChatHelpers.BuildChatBody sets no temperature at all (relies on provider default); AiAdapters.cs mock sets none.
requirements.gap: No centralized, enforced temperature ceiling: the OpenAI-direct/Azure/OpenAI-compatible/CLI adapters do not set temperature, so the 0.2 ceiling is neither uniform nor enforced. There is no per-rulebook temperature override (RulebookSpec.StyleSpec has no temperature field). Need a Temperature on AiCompletionRequest, a gateway-enforced ceiling, and a rulebook style key.

requirements.title: AI-015 — Structured output enforcement (JSON Schema / FHIR shape) for downstream sections
requirements.evidence: RulebookSpec.OutputSchemaSpec (Type/Required/Properties/Enum) is parsed from YAML (RulebookSpec.cs:53). DictationCleanupService prompts for and parses a fixed section JSON object. FHIR export exists (FhirDiagnosticReportSerializer / export/fhir). AiCompletionRequest has no response_format/json_schema field.
requirements.gap: OutputSchema is parsed but NEVER enforced — grep shows zero usages outside its own declaration; it is not passed to providers as response_format/json_schema, not validated against model output, and not used to constrain generation. Only the dictation-cleanup mode does soft JSON coercion via prompt+try/catch. No provider-side structured-output (json_schema response_format) binding.

requirements.title: AI-016 — Output watermarking (cryptographic provenance, optional perturbation) [EE]
requirements.evidence: No matches for watermark/perturbation/provenance-signing in backend code (only PRD/docs/threat-model). AI output is returned as plain text with no embedded cryptographic provenance token or perturbation step.
requirements.gap: Entire EE feature absent: need a provenance signer (sign output hash with tenant/KMS key — KMS providers exist under Infrastructure/Kms and could be reused) and an optional perturbation pass, plus a verify endpoint.

requirements.title: AI-017 — Adversarial input detection (prompt-injection in clinical text, e.g. 'ignore previous instructions')
requirements.evidence: No prompt-injection / adversarial-input scanner anywhere in backend (grep for 'ignore previous'/'prompt.inject'/'adversarial' hits only PRD + docs/threat-model.md + agent-safety.md). DictationCleanupService and ReportingService pass user dictation straight into the user prompt with no sanitization or detection.
requirements.gap: Entire P0 control absent: need a pre-prompt detector that scans dictation/free-text for injection patterns (override-instruction phrases, role hijacks, fenced system directives) and flags/strips them before the AiGateway call, with an audit event. Currently the only related guard is structural (system prompt says 'do not sign').

requirements.title: AI-018 — Token budget enforcement per request and per user/day
requirements.evidence: PlanQuotaService.CheckAsync enforces tenant AiCalls/Input/Output token limits per MONTH (PlanLimits, BILL-001..006) and AiGateway gates on it. Per-request rate limiting via [EnableRateLimiting("ai")] on AI endpoints. Static max_tokens=1024 cap in provider bodies (OpenAiChatHelpers, Bedrock, Vertex).
requirements.gap: Granularity mismatch with the PRD: enforcement is per TENANT per MONTH, not per-REQUEST token budget nor per-USER-per-DAY. AiRequest rows carry UserId capability but the quota query (GetOkAiUsageAsync) aggregates by tenant+month only. The 1024 max_tokens is a hardcoded ceiling, not a configurable per-request budget. Need per-user/day accounting and a configurable per-request token budget.

requirements.title: AI-019 — Multi-model ensemble for impression generation (majority + diff), opt-in [EE]
requirements.evidence: No ensemble/majority-vote logic in backend (grep 'ensemble'/'majority' hits only PRD/docs). ReportingService.RunAsync calls exactly one provider per request; there is a SandboxCompareController (/api/ai/sandbox/compare) for side-by-side provider comparison but it is a manual A/B preview, not an automatic ensemble that fuses outputs.
requirements.gap: Entire EE feature absent: need an opt-in ensemble runner that fans out to N providers, computes majority/consensus impression and a structured diff. SandboxCompare provides a starting point for the fan-out plumbing but no fusion/voting.

requirements.title: AI-020 — Local PII/PHI scrubbing pass before any cloud egress (Presidio + tenant rules)
requirements.evidence: AiGateway.EnforcePhiPolicy BLOCKS PHI from routing to non-PHI-approved/non-local providers (ProviderPolicyException) and ReportingService.ContainsPhi/ContainsPhiText + DictationCleanupService.LooksLikePhi detect PHI shapes (MRN/DOB/dates/Mr.|Mrs. names). PhiRedactor.cs masks PHI in log lines.
requirements.gap: Architecture is detect-and-BLOCK, not scrub-then-SEND. There is no de-identification pass that strips/pseudonymizes PHI from the prompt before egress to a cloud provider (PhiRedactor is used only for logs/exceptions per SEC-010, not on the outbound AiCompletionRequest). No Presidio-equivalent recognizer set, no tenant-configurable scrub rules, and no re-identification map. So de-identified-cloud workflows (send scrubbed text to a non-PHI provider) are not supported — PHI simply can't go to cloud at all.

requirements.title: AI-021 — 'Quote-it' mode: AI cites the source sentence in findings for each impression bullet
requirements.evidence: No quote-it/source-citation generation in backend (grep 'quote.it'/'cite.source'/'source.span' hits only PRD/docs). The impression prompt does not request per-bullet source spans, and no response shape carries citation links. The frontend UnsupportedClaimFinding quotes a sentence, but that is the validator flagging an UNsupported sentence, not the AI citing the supporting source sentence per bullet.
requirements.gap: Entire feature absent: need an impression-generation variant that emits, per bullet, the supporting Findings span/offset, an AiResult shape that carries citations, and UI to render bullet→source links.

requirements.title: AI-022 — Disagreement detection between two providers when ensemble enabled [EE]
requirements.evidence: No disagreement-detection logic in backend (grep 'disagreement' hits only PRD/docs). Depends on the absent ensemble path (AI-019). SandboxCompareController returns two provider outputs but does not compute a semantic disagreement signal.
requirements.gap: Entire EE feature absent: need, on top of the ensemble runner, a comparison that detects clinically material divergence between two provider outputs and surfaces it. SandboxCompare gives raw outputs but no automated divergence analysis.

requirements.title: AI-023 — Multilingual report generation with translation provenance
requirements.evidence: There is an Iter35LocaleController (UI locale/i18n) but no report-content translation pipeline and no translation-provenance record (grep 'translat' in backend src hits only api.ts/Program comments, not a translation service). ReportingService modes are English-only; no source-language/target-language or translation audit on report text.
requirements.gap: Entire feature absent for report CONTENT: need a translate mode that records source text, target language, model/provider used, and a provenance audit linking the translated report to its source, distinct from UI-locale i18n which is unrelated.

requirements.title: AI-024 — Personal style memory (encrypted, per-user, exportable, deletable, opt-in)
requirements.evidence: No per-user style-memory entity or store in backend (grep 'style.memory'/'StyleMemory' hits only PRD). Prompt layering (IPromptOverrideStore) is keyed by tenant+rulebook, not user; there is no learned/stored personal style profile, no per-user encryption-at-rest for it, and no export/delete (DSAR-style) endpoints for a style profile.
requirements.gap: Entire feature absent: need a per-user StyleMemory entity (opt-in flag), KMS-encrypted storage (KMS providers exist and can be reused), capture of accepted edits to learn style, injection into prompts, and export/delete endpoints.

requirements.title: AI-025 — Speculative completion of structured fields based on study metadata
requirements.evidence: No speculative/auto-prefill of structured fields from study metadata in backend (grep 'speculative' hits only PRD/docs). Report creation (ReportsController.Create) copies modality/bodyPart/indication/comparison from the DTO/StudyContext verbatim; nothing pre-populates technique/findings scaffolds or structured fields from DICOM/study metadata. DicomContext fetch exists but is not used to auto-complete report fields.
requirements.gap: Entire feature absent: need a service that, from study metadata (modality, body part, protocol, DICOM context), speculatively prefills technique/structured fields, clearly marked (.ai-mark) and easily dismissed.

requirements.title: AI-026 — 'Explain like I'm the referring physician' mode
requirements.evidence: ReportingService 'referring_summary' mode and ReportRewriteService.ReportRewriteMode.ReferringSummary produce a one-paragraph summary for the referring clinician highlighting impression + actionable findings. Also 'patient_friendly' mode (8th-grade) exists. Exposed via /api/reports/{id}/ai and the rewrite endpoint; RewriteStylePanel.tsx in the frontend. RewriteModeTests cover modes.
requirements.gap: Tone is fixed in code per mode rather than tunable to the referrer's specialty; output is not separately persisted/labeled as a distinct artifact, only returned for accept/reject. Minor vs the PRD intent.

requirements.title: AI-027 — Length and tone controls per tenant default
requirements.evidence: RulebookSpec.StyleSpec has Tone ('concise_clinical' default) and ImpressionMaxBullets (length); ReportValidator.CheckImpressionBullets enforces the bullet ceiling ('impression_bullet_count'). Rewrite/impression prompts encode concise/formal tones.
requirements.gap: Tone is stored on the rulebook style but is NOT injected into the generation prompt (BuildPromptForMode does not read Style.Tone) — so the tone control is declarative only and not actually applied to AI output. There is no TENANT-DEFAULT length/tone setting (controls live at rulebook scope, and TenantSettings has no tone/length default); preferred_terms mapping shown in the PRD YAML is not modeled in StyleSpec. So length is partly enforced (bullets) but tone is effectively not wired, and the 'per tenant default' dimension is missing.

requirements.title: AI-028 — Hallucination guardrail: every impression claim must trace to a source span; unsupported claims surfaced before export
requirements.evidence: HallucinationDetector flags unsupported impression sentences ('ai:unsupported_claim'); these merge into validation and the UI surfaces them (UnsupportedClaimFinding) before acknowledge/export. ReportsController.Acknowledge blocks acknowledgement when blockers exist (RequireZeroBlockers) and export is gated behind Acknowledged status, so unsupported claims can be made export-blocking when severity=Blocker / WarnAsBlocker.
requirements.gap: It surfaces unsupported claims by token-overlap but does NOT establish a positive source-SPAN trace for each claim (no claim→exact-source-offset mapping; that is AI-021). The guardrail is opt-in per TenantSettings (HallucinationDetectionEnabled) and defaults can leave it off; severity defaults to Warning so it does not hard-block export unless configured. To reach 100% it needs span-level provenance per impression sentence and a default export-blocking posture.

summary: RadioPad ships a real .NET 8 global tool (cli/RadioPad.Cli, command name `radiopad`) that covers the CLI-001..CLI-010 (CE/EE) band well: device-flow login, daemon lifecycle, generate-from-local-input, rulebook/report validation, golden-case rulebook test, template import/export, provider list/test/register, a local PHI policy guard, resumable audit sync, and a global --headless mode. These ten are the ONLY §14.8 items tracked in docs/09-regulatory/traceability-matrix.md (section literally titled "CLI — Command-line tool (10)"), and code confirms them. Beyond CLI-010 there is a steep drop-off: the higher-numbered CLI items (CLI-011 eval-with-metrics, CLI-013 doctor, CLI-014 model pull/list/prune, CLI-015 shell completions, CLI-016 universal JSON output, CLI-017 plugin install, CLI-018 pacs query/fetch, CLI-019 benchmark, CLI-020 migrate) are mostly missing or only stubbed, and the ENTIRE §14.8.3 daemon section (DMN-001..016) is unaddressed AS A DAEMON. Per the locked-stack rule I credited daemon FUNCTIONS that live in the ASP.NET API instead of a separate Rust `radiopadd` binary: local llama.cpp inference (DMN-005 partial), provider-router policy engine (DMN-004), env-var secret resolution (DMN-006 partial), append-only audit log (DMN-009 partial), per-provider/plan quotas (DMN-010 partial), health+ready endpoints (DMN-012 partial), startup auto-migration (CLI-020 function). But there is genuinely no UNIX-socket/named-pipe/mTLS RPC, no gRPC+reflection, no embedded ONNX runtime, no OS-keychain/DPAPI vault, no SQLCipher encrypted cache, no WASM plugin host, no durable offline audit buffer that flushes when the control plane reconnects, no Prometheus metrics endpoint, no signed EdDSA self-update, no hot config reload, no crash-restart backoff supervisor, and no PHI-stripped crash dumps. Key files: cli/RadioPad.Cli/Program.cs, Commands/{DeviceFlow,Daemon,AuditSync,ProviderRegister,PhiGuard,Templates,ValidationPacks,CliRuntime}.cs, PluginVerifier.cs; backend AiGateway.cs, ProviderSecretResolver.cs, LlamaCppProvider.cs, Program.cs (health/ready/migrate), PacsController.cs (server-side QIDO). Note: the daemon/ directory is LEGACY Open Design Node.js, not RadioPad.

requirements.title: CLI-001 — `radiopad login` via OAuth Device Authorization Grant or browser PKCE
requirements.evidence: cli/RadioPad.Cli/Program.cs BuildLoginCommand() wires `--device-flow`; Commands/DeviceFlow.cs implements full RFC 8628 device-authorization grant against POST /api/auth/device/authorize + /api/auth/device/token, polls with authorization_pending/slow_down/access_denied/expired_token handling, persists bearer token to ~/.radiopad/config.json (never logged). Non-device path saves tenant/user/server identity. Confirmed in traceability-matrix.md line 148 (✅).
requirements.gap: Only the Device Authorization Grant is implemented. The PRD's alternative 'browser-based PKCE' (authorization-code + PKCE with a local loopback redirect) is not present. The non-device `login` path is a plain identity-save, not an OAuth flow.

requirements.title: CLI-002 — `radiopad daemon start|stop|status|logs|restart`
requirements.evidence: cli/RadioPad.Cli/Program.cs BuildDaemonCommand() + Commands/Daemon.cs (DaemonControl) implement start (spawns RadioPad.Api as detached process, writes ~/.radiopad/daemon.pid with bind/port), stop (SIGTERM/CloseMainWindow then 5s grace then hard kill), status (pid liveness), and restart. Matrix line 149 (✅).
requirements.gap: The `logs` subcommand from the requirement is NOT implemented (only start/stop/status/restart exist). Process stdout/stderr is not redirected/captured (RedirectStandardOutput=false), so there is no log surface for a `logs` command to read.

requirements.title: CLI-003 — `radiopad generate` from local input files (text/JSON/FHIR)
requirements.evidence: Program.cs BuildGenerateCommand(): when --report is omitted it requires --input, resolves --template (ResolveTemplateAsync), creates a draft via POST /api/reports, PATCHes findings from the file, runs the local PHI guard, then routes through /api/reports/{id}/ai. Supports --mode, --provider, --rulebook, --out/--output (file or '-'), --format json|text. Matrix line 150 (✅).
requirements.gap: Input is read as raw text only (findings) — there is no JSON or FHIR input parsing/mapping as the requirement enumerates (it just dumps the whole file into Findings). Indication/technique/comparison are not populated from structured input. No FHIR bundle ingestion in the generate path.

requirements.title: CLI-004 — `radiopad validate` for rulebook and report validation
requirements.evidence: Program.cs `rulebook validate` (RulebookSpec.FromYaml lint) and `report validate` (POST /api/reports/{id}/validate). Matrix line 151 (✅), CI runs all 17 rulebook validate commands.
requirements.gap: None material. A top-level `radiopad validate` alias does not exist (validation is under `rulebook` and `report` subtrees) but both functions are present.

requirements.title: CLI-005 — `radiopad rulebook test` regression vs golden cases
requirements.evidence: Program.cs BuildRulebookTestCommand(): loads rulebook, iterates --cases *.json, runs ReportValidator.Validate, compares expected vs actual flagged rule ids (missing/unexpected), prints PASS/FAIL and aggregate, non-zero exit on failure. Matrix line 152 (✅).
requirements.gap: PRD example shows `--cases ./golden-cases --report html`; the implemented command has --cases but no `--report html` formatted output (console text only).

requirements.title: CLI-006 — `radiopad templates import|export|list|diff`
requirements.evidence: Program.cs BuildTemplatesCommand() + Commands/Templates.cs implement list, export (JSON/YAML by extension), and import (JSON/YAML upsert via POST /api/templates). Matrix line 153 (✅).
requirements.gap: The `diff` subcommand is NOT implemented. Only list/import/export of the four required verbs exist.

requirements.title: CLI-007 — `radiopad ai providers list|test|set-default`
requirements.evidence: Program.cs BuildProviderCommand() implements `provider list` (GET /api/providers), `provider test` (round-trips a throwaway report through a provider), and `provider register` (Commands/ProviderRegister.cs, POST /api/providers, env: secret refs, compliance forced to Sandbox). Adapter catalog is broad (Azure/Bedrock/Vertex/OpenAI/Anthropic/Ollama/vLLM/llama-cpp/Copilot/Gemini/Codex). Matrix line 154 (✅).
requirements.gap: Command group is `provider` not `ai providers`. The `set-default` verb is NOT implemented (no way to set the default provider from the CLI; default routing is server-side). `register` exists but is not one of the three required verbs.

requirements.title: CLI-008 — Enforce tenant model policies locally before any request leaves the machine
requirements.evidence: Commands/PhiGuard.cs EnsureAllowedAsync() runs client-side before the AI call in generate: fetches report + provider, refuses (exit code 4 ExitPhiPolicyBlocked) when report containsPhi and provider compliance is neither PhiApproved nor LocalOnly. Server remains authoritative via AiGateway.EnforcePhiPolicy (backend AiGateway.cs). Matrix line 155 (✅).
requirements.gap: Guard is defence-in-depth and fail-open when it cannot evaluate (network error returns 0). It only covers the PHI/compliance dimension; broader 'tenant model policies' (e.g. allowed-model lists, region pinning) are enforced only server-side, not pre-flight on the machine.

requirements.title: CLI-009 — `radiopad audit export` sync to control plane or local file
requirements.evidence: Program.cs `audit export` (GET /api/audit), `audit verify` (recompute SHA-256 hash chain locally), and `audit sync` → Commands/AuditSync.cs: resumable pull to NDJSON with a persisted watermark (~/.radiopad/audit-sync.state), --from/--to window, dedupe by createdAt+id. Matrix line 156 (✅).
requirements.gap: PRD example shows `audit export --format ndjson --redact-phi`; the export verb prints raw JSON (no --format / no --redact-phi). Redaction and NDJSON formatting live only in the separate `sync` verb (and sync does not redact). 'Sync to control plane' is realized as local-file NDJSON, not an upstream push to a remote control plane.

requirements.title: CLI-010 — Headless mode for enterprise deployment with config file
requirements.evidence: Program.cs adds global `--headless` option; CliRuntime.Headless + RequireConfig() exit with code 3 (ExitHeadlessUnauthenticated) when config is missing and headless is set. Config persisted at ~/.radiopad/config.json (CliRuntime.LoadConfig/SaveConfig). Matrix line 157 (✅).
requirements.gap: Headless = no-prompt + non-zero-on-missing-config only. There is no dedicated enterprise config-file flag (e.g. --config <path> / env override) beyond the fixed ~/.radiopad/config.json, and no broader fleet/headless deployment config (provider/policy bootstrap from a file).

requirements.title: CLI-011 — `radiopad eval` harness (edit-distance, rule pass-rate, hallucination flags)
requirements.evidence: Program.cs registers top-level `eval <dir>`: enumerates *.json golden cases, POSTs each report to /api/reports/golden-case, counts PASS/FAIL on HTTP success, prints passed/total. No edit-distance/pass-rate/hallucination computation found (grep for edit-distance|hallucination|passRate in cli returned no matches).
requirements.gap: Only a binary HTTP-success pass/fail. None of the three required metrics (edit-distance, rule pass-rate, hallucination flags) are computed or reported. Not in the traceability matrix.

requirements.title: CLI-012 — `radiopad fhir import|export` bulk FHIR operations
requirements.evidence: Program.cs BuildIngestCommand() has `ingest fhir <file>` (POST a single FHIR R4 ServiceRequest/Bundle to /api/ingest/fhir/servicerequest) and `report export --format fhir` (GET /api/reports/{id}/export/fhir) for a single report.
requirements.gap: No dedicated `radiopad fhir` command group and no BULK operations. Single-resource ingest and single-report export exist; bulk import/export (e.g. FHIR $export / batch bundles) is absent.

requirements.title: CLI-013 — `radiopad doctor` system diagnostic
requirements.evidence: No `doctor` command registered in Program.cs; grep for `doctor` across cli/ returns only unrelated matches. Not in cli-guide.md or traceability matrix.
requirements.gap: Entire diagnostic command missing: no network check, model-availability probe, daemon-health check, or latency probes. Building blocks exist (daemon status, provider health POST /api/providers/{id}/health, /api/health/ready) but no aggregating `doctor` command.

requirements.title: CLI-014 — `radiopad model pull|list|prune` (GGUF/ONNX)
requirements.evidence: No `model` command in Program.cs; grep for `model pull`/`gguf` in cli returns no matches. Local model management is not exposed via the CLI.
requirements.gap: No local model lifecycle management at all (pull/list/prune). There is no model store or downloader; the llama.cpp/Ollama adapters assume an already-running external server.

requirements.title: CLI-015 — Shell completions: bash, zsh, fish, PowerShell
requirements.evidence: No completions command registered and no completion script assets (Glob **/*completion* found nothing). cli-guide.md has no completion docs. System.CommandLine beta4 ships dotnet-suggest infra but no first-class `radiopad completions <shell>` generator is wired.
requirements.gap: No way to emit per-shell completion scripts; the four required shells are unsupported as a deliverable.

requirements.title: CLI-016 — JSON output mode for every command (for piping)
requirements.evidence: Some commands emit JSON inherently (provider list, report get, audit export pass through API JSON; generate has --format json|text). But there is no global `--json` flag, and many commands print human-formatted tables/lines (report list, templates list, packs list, rulebook test PASS/FAIL, daemon status). CliRuntime has no JSON-output switch.
requirements.gap: No universal JSON output mode. Table/line-formatted commands cannot be switched to machine-readable JSON, so 'every command' is not satisfied.

requirements.title: CLI-017 — `radiopad plugins install <name>` from registry or local path
requirements.evidence: Program.cs BuildPacsCommand() has `pacs plugins list|verify|enable|disable` (manifest SHA-256 + Ed25519 verification via PluginVerifier.cs) and `plugin verify` for artifacts. There is desktop PACS plugin loading infra (desktop/PLUGIN_TRUST.md) and a server MCP capability registry.
requirements.gap: No `plugins install` verb and no registry client / local-path installer. Only verify/enable/disable of already-present plugins exists; nothing fetches or installs a plugin by name or path.

requirements.title: CLI-018 — `radiopad pacs query|fetch` against a DICOMweb node
requirements.evidence: Server has a QIDO-RS study-search proxy (PacsController.cs query endpoint with accession/patientId/studyDate/modality) and DicomWebClient (backend Services/DicomWebClient.cs). CLI has `dicom fetch <report-id>` → GET /api/reports/{id}/dicom-context.
requirements.gap: The CLI `pacs` group only exposes `plugins` (no `query`/`fetch` verbs). `dicom fetch` resolves a report's DICOM context, not a free-form DICOMweb query/fetch against an arbitrary node. No CLI surface for ad-hoc QIDO/WADO testing.

requirements.title: CLI-019 — `radiopad benchmark` for local performance regressions
requirements.evidence: No `benchmark` command in Program.cs; grep returns no CLI matches. Performance harnessing exists elsewhere (perf/k6 scripts, backend PerfBudgetMiddleware) but not as a CLI command.
requirements.gap: No workstation benchmark command at all.

requirements.title: CLI-020 — `radiopad migrate` for schema migrations on self-hosted deployments
requirements.evidence: The migration FUNCTION exists and runs automatically at API startup: backend Program.cs line 453 `await db.Database.MigrateAsync()` applies pending EF Core migrations before serving. EF migrations live under RadioPad.Infrastructure/Migrations.
requirements.gap: No `radiopad migrate` CLI command — migrations only run implicitly when the API boots. Operators cannot run/inspect/roll migrations from the CLI as the requirement specifies.

requirements.title: DMN-001 — Rust, statically-linked daemon, ≤35 MB binary, ≤30 MB RSS idle
requirements.evidence: Project CLAUDE.md locks the stack to ASP.NET Core 8; there is no separate `radiopadd` Rust binary. The 'daemon' function is the RadioPad.Api process spawned by `radiopad daemon start`. The legacy daemon/ dir is Open Design Node.js, unrelated.
requirements.gap: N/A under the locked stack for the Rust/binary-size/RSS specifics. If a true sidecar daemon were ever required, none exists; the ASP.NET API is heavier than the size/RSS budget but those numbers are stack-specific and out of scope per the lock.

requirements.title: DMN-002 — UNIX socket / named pipe / local TCP+mTLS client RPC
requirements.evidence: `radiopad daemon start` launches the API on local TCP (RADIOPAD_BIND / ASPNETCORE_URLS, default 127.0.0.1:7457, Daemon.cs NormalizeBindUrl). The CLI talks to it over plain HTTP (CliRuntime.NewHttpClient).
requirements.gap: No UNIX-domain-socket or Windows named-pipe transport, and no mTLS on the local TCP channel. Transport is plain loopback HTTP only.

requirements.title: DMN-003 — gRPC API with reflection; OpenAPI proxy for browser clients
requirements.evidence: The API exposes Swagger/OpenAPI (Program.cs UseSwagger/UseSwaggerUI; openapi/openapi.yaml present) which satisfies the 'OpenAPI for browser clients' half.
requirements.gap: No gRPC server and no gRPC reflection anywhere (no Grpc.AspNetCore reference, grep for gRPC in backend src found none). The RPC surface is REST/OpenAPI only.

requirements.title: DMN-004 — Provider router policy engine evaluates per-request before external call
requirements.evidence: backend AiGateway.cs RouteAsync() calls EnforcePhiPolicy(tenant, request) before dispatching to any adapter and audits ProviderBlocked on violation (throws ProviderPolicyException); it is documented as 'the only component allowed to call provider APIs in production'. EfProviderRouter selects providers by compliance/priority/latency (referenced in matrix PROV-003).
requirements.gap: Policy is evaluated in-process inside the API, which is the daemon equivalent under the locked stack — functionally complete. No separate hot-swappable policy file (see DMN-013).

requirements.title: DMN-005 — Embeds llama.cpp + ONNX Runtime; auto-selects backend (CPU/CUDA/Metal/ROCm/Vulkan/DirectML)
requirements.evidence: backend Providers/Local/LlamaCppProvider.cs (HTTP adapter to llama.cpp `/completion`, default 127.0.0.1:8080, LocalOnly compliance) plus OllamaProvider and VLlmProvider give local inference. ProbeAsync hits llama.cpp /health.
requirements.gap: llama.cpp is integrated as an EXTERNAL HTTP server adapter, not embedded in-process. No ONNX Runtime integration at all. No backend auto-selection logic (CUDA/Metal/ROCm/Vulkan/DirectML) — that is delegated entirely to the externally-launched llama-server.

requirements.title: DMN-006 — Secret vault via OS keychain (Keychain/DPAPI/Secret Service/kwallet); age-encrypted fallback
requirements.evidence: Secrets are referenced as `env:NAME` and resolved at use time by ProviderSecretResolver.cs (Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable); keys never appear in JSON/logs/audit. AesGcmColumnEncryptor + IColumnEncryptor protect at-rest DB columns; PacsSecretResolver mirrors the env pattern.
requirements.gap: No OS keychain integration (macOS Keychain, Windows DPAPI/CNG, Linux Secret Service/kwallet) and no age-encrypted file fallback. Secret material lives in environment variables, not an OS-backed vault.

requirements.title: DMN-007 — Encrypted local cache (SQLCipher) with WAL + tenant-keyed encryption
requirements.evidence: grep for SQLCipher across backend returned no matches. Column-level AES-GCM encryption exists (AesGcmColumnEncryptor) and there is per-tenant CMK handling, but the local store is standard EF Core (no SQLCipher page-level DB encryption, no documented WAL-mode local cache).
requirements.gap: No SQLCipher-encrypted local cache with WAL and tenant-keyed page encryption. At-rest protection is column-level only, in the main DB, not a tenant-keyed local cache.

requirements.title: DMN-008 — Plugin host (WASM/WASI preview 2) for connectors/tools, capability-limited
requirements.evidence: There is an MCP plugin/capability subsystem: InMemoryMcpCapabilityRegistry, McpScopePolicy/IMcpScopePolicy, IMcpSandbox, PluginManifestSignatureVerifier, PluginPolicyException; CLI verifies signed plugin manifests (PluginVerifier.cs). Capability-limiting and signature trust exist.
requirements.gap: No WASM/WASI runtime host. Connectors/tools are not executed as capability-sandboxed WASM modules; the model is MCP scope policy + signed manifests, not a WASI preview-2 sandbox.

requirements.title: DMN-009 — Audit buffer: durable local SQLite-WAL queue surviving crashes, flushes to control plane
requirements.evidence: Server-side append-only audit log exists (IAuditLog.AppendAsync, SHA-256 hash chain verified by `radiopad audit verify`). CLI `audit sync` (AuditSync.cs) keeps a resumable watermark and writes durable local NDJSON.
requirements.gap: No durable LOCAL OUTBOUND queue that buffers events during control-plane outages and auto-flushes on reconnect. `audit sync` is a manual pull-to-file, not a crash-surviving store-and-forward buffer to a remote control plane.

requirements.title: DMN-010 — Resource quotas: max RAM/GPU mem/parallel-requests per provider, per tenant
requirements.evidence: PlanQuotaService + IPlanQuotaStore + PlanLimits enforce per-tenant plan quotas inside AiGateway.RouteAsync (audited PolicyViolation on breach). RateLimitMiddleware provides per-IP/per-tenant fixed-window limiting.
requirements.gap: Quotas are usage/plan-based (credits, request counts). There is no enforcement of max RAM, max GPU memory, or max parallel requests per provider — the physical-resource quotas the requirement names are absent.

requirements.title: DMN-011 — Self-update channel separate from Studio (signed, EdDSA)
requirements.evidence: grep for self-update/EdDSA found none in CLI or daemon context. Ed25519 verification exists only for plugin/model artifact signatures (PluginVerifier.cs, BouncyCastle), not for an updater.
requirements.gap: No self-update mechanism at all (no update channel, no signed-release fetch/apply). The CLI is distributed as a dotnet global tool with no built-in updater.

requirements.title: DMN-012 — Health, ready, metrics (Prometheus) endpoints
requirements.evidence: backend Program.cs maps GET /api/health (liveness) and GET /api/health/ready (DB CanConnect → 200/503). PerfBudgetMiddleware records per-route duration histograms internally; observability docs/Grafana dashboards exist under deploy/observability.
requirements.gap: No Prometheus /metrics scrape endpoint is exposed (no prometheus-net/OpenTelemetry exporter mapped; grep found dashboards/docs but no scrape route). Health+ready present; metrics endpoint missing.

requirements.title: DMN-013 — Hot reload of policy and provider config without restart
requirements.evidence: Provider/policy config lives in the DB and is read per-request, but there is no documented hot-reload of a policy/provider CONFIG FILE; no IOptionsMonitor/file-watcher reload path found for policy. The startup encryptor/config is resolved once at boot.
requirements.gap: No file-based policy/provider config with live reload. Changing config requires DB writes (which take effect per-request) or a restart for app settings; no explicit hot-reload subsystem.

requirements.title: DMN-014 — Auto-restart on crash with exponential backoff; max 5 restarts/5 min before alerting
requirements.evidence: DaemonControl.Start (Daemon.cs) spawns the API once and records the PID; restart is manual (`radiopad daemon restart`). No supervisor loop, no backoff, no restart-count window.
requirements.gap: No crash supervisor: the CLI does not monitor the spawned process, does not auto-restart with exponential backoff, and has no 5-in-5-minutes alert threshold.

requirements.title: DMN-015 — Crash dumps PHI-stripped before any optional upload
requirements.evidence: No crash-dump capture/scrubbing/upload pipeline found in CLI or backend. GlobalExceptionMiddleware handles request errors but does not produce sanitized crash dumps.
requirements.gap: No crash-dump generation, no PHI-stripping of dumps, and no opt-in upload path.

requirements.title: DMN-016 — Confidential computing mode (SGX/SEV) for inference tenants
requirements.evidence: No SGX/SEV or confidential-computing references in the codebase.
requirements.gap: Entire confidential-computing inference mode is absent (P2/EE).

area: AI Safety & Quality / Validation Engine (PRD v2 §16)
summary: RadioPad implements a solid deterministic core of §16.1's validation matrix plus the human-in-the-loop primitives that protect signing, but a large fraction of the PRD's checks and safety-process controls are missing or only partially built. The deterministic engine (backend/RadioPad.Api/src/RadioPad.Validation/Engine/ReportValidator.cs) genuinely covers laterality, measurement, required-section, negation (NegEx-style), modality-mismatch, critical-finding language, and follow-up allow-list checks, all driven by YAML rulebooks (RulebookSpec) and exercised by golden-case validation packs (ValidationPackService) — these map well to the locked .NET stack regardless of the PRD's NER/Go/Rust wording. The "unsupported-impression / hallucination" check exists but is a deterministic token-overlap heuristic (HallucinationDetector.cs), NOT an AI-assisted, source-span-attested check as §16.1/§16.5 require. Roughly a third of the §16.1 matrix has NO implementation at all: anatomy-mismatch, sex/age-appropriate language, allergy/contrast safety, dose (DLP/CTDIvol) language, spelling/grammar (Hunspell/LanguageTool), acronym expansion, prior-report inconsistency (semantic diff), quantitative RECIST comparison, and standalone ambiguity detection. §16.2 human-in-the-loop is partial: AI text is visibly marked (.ai-mark) and acknowledged, export is gated behind explicit acknowledgement and blockers, and the system never auto-signs (RIS copy via CopyToRisButton) — but there is NO critical-results routing workflow, NO keystroke-aggregation analytics, NO no-silent-retry operator-visibility guarantee, NO accessibility opt-out for the AI mark, and NO always-accessible mandatory "Why this suggestion?" panel. §16.3 confidence presentation is partial (pass/warn/block + unsupported-statement warnings + quality score) but lacks source-span highlights, a provenance/context-trace graph, a visible model+rulebook version pin in the panel, and a "Needs radiologist review" banner. §16.4 safety-RFC has tooling scaffolding (validation packs with approve/run/audit) but no enforced RFC/CSWG/≥99%-golden-gate/30-day-pre-announce process. §16.5 adversarial robustness is largely absent: no prompt-injection stripping in the dictation/daemon path, no output watermarking, no source-span attestation, only a weak refusal fallback, and no toxic-content-filter toggle. Evidence verified against actual source; PROGRESS.md claims around §16 (e.g. Prompt Studio for §16.4) were cross-checked and do not substitute for the missing checks.

requirements.title: §16.1 Laterality consistency (Deterministic, Blocker, cross-section + impression)
requirements.evidence: ReportValidator.CheckLaterality (backend/.../RadioPad.Validation/Engine/ReportValidator.cs:247) compares asserted left/right between Findings/context and Impression with negation-awareness (HasAssertedSide). Wired via rule id 'laterality_consistency' (line 117); covered by ValidationEngineTests.LateralityConflictDetected.
requirements.gap: Uses regex left/right tokens rather than true NER; spine-level laterality and multi-side organs not modelled. Functionally meets the clause on the locked stack.

requirements.title: §16.1 Measurement consistency (Deterministic regex + unit normalization, Warning)
requirements.evidence: CheckMeasurements + NormalizeMeasurement (ReportValidator.cs:289-311) normalize cm→mm and flag Impression measurements absent from Findings. Rule id 'measurement_consistency' (line 121).
requirements.gap: Only Findings↔Impression; does not reconcile against structured fields across all sections (PRD says 'all sections + structured fields'). Biaxial covered by a separate rule.

requirements.title: §16.1 Required-section check (Deterministic schema, Blocker, per template/rulebook)
requirements.evidence: ValidateCore iterates rulebook.RequiredSections and emits Blocker 'required_section:<name>' when missing/empty (ReportValidator.cs:81-92). RulebookSpec.RequiredSections; ValidationEngineTests.RequiredSectionMissingIsBlocker.
requirements.gap: None material for the clause.

requirements.title: §16.1 Unsupported-impression (AI-assisted + source-span, Blocker — impression traces to findings)
requirements.evidence: HallucinationDetector.Detect (backend/.../Application/Services/HallucinationDetector.cs) flags impression sentences whose content-token overlap with Findings/context falls below a tenant threshold; merged as 'ai:unsupported_claim' in ReportingService.ValidateAsync and rendered with .ai-mark by UnsupportedClaimFinding in ReportClient.tsx.
requirements.gap: It is a deterministic token-overlap heuristic, not AI-assisted with character-level source-span tracing as the PRD demands; severity defaults to tenant-config not Blocker; no span linkage from impression token to supporting Findings span. Needs an AI-assisted span-attested traceability check.

requirements.title: §16.1 Critical-finding language (Deterministic lexicon + AI, Blocker, mandatory tenant phrasebook)
requirements.evidence: CheckCriticalLanguage (ReportValidator.cs:448) detects critical phrasing (BI-RADS 4C/5/6, Lung-RADS 4B/4X, LI-RADS 5/M/TIV, tension pneumothorax, malpositioned tubes, etc.) and requires communication/escalation verbs; rule ids 'critical_result_language'/'critical_finding_language'.
requirements.gap: Lexicon is hard-coded in the validator, not a tenant-configurable mandatory phrasebook; no AI-assisted layer. PRD wants tenant phrasebook + AI.

requirements.title: §16.1 Follow-up recommendation (Deterministic allow-list, Warning, tenant-approved phrases only)
requirements.evidence: CheckUnauthorizedFollowup (ReportValidator.cs:603) gates Recommendations sentences against RulebookSpec.StyleSpec.ApprovedFollowups; also enforced at suggestion time in ReportingService.SuggestFollowUpAsync with hash-only audit of rejected suggestions.
requirements.gap: Allow-list is per-rulebook rather than a tenant-wide approved set, and empty list disables the rule (back-compat). Acceptable but slightly narrower than 'tenant-approved'.

requirements.title: §16.1 Negation conflict (Deterministic NegEx/ConText, Blocker — 'No PE' vs 'small PE')
requirements.evidence: CheckNegationConflict + IsNegatedMention (ReportValidator.cs:367-404) detect concepts denied in Findings but asserted in Impression with negation-window logic; rule id 'negation_conflict'.
requirements.gap: Hand-rolled negation windows rather than a full NegEx/ConText pipeline; scope/family detection limited. Meets the example case.

requirements.title: §16.1 Modality mismatch (Deterministic template + study meta, Warning — CT language in MR report)
requirements.evidence: CheckModalityMismatch (ReportValidator.cs:406) flags cross-modality phrasing relative to report.Study.Modality; rule id 'modality_mismatch'.
requirements.gap: Small fixed pair list (CT/MR/US/XR); does not consult template metadata, only study modality.

requirements.title: §16.1 Anatomy mismatch (Deterministic anatomy NER vs body_part, Warning — wrong body part wording)
requirements.evidence: No anatomy-vs-body_part check exists. CheckRequiredOrganCoverage (ReportValidator.cs:654) only verifies a fixed organ list is mentioned; no rule compares anatomy terms in narrative against report.Study.BodyPart. Grep for anatomy mismatch rule ids found none.
requirements.gap: Implement an anatomy-NER (or lexicon) check that flags narrative anatomy inconsistent with the study body part; add rule id + rulebook wiring.

requirements.title: §16.1 Hallucination risk (AI-assisted + provenance, Warning — claims not in input context)
requirements.evidence: Same HallucinationDetector token-overlap heuristic covers the unsupported-impression and hallucination-risk rows together; no provenance graph is produced.
requirements.gap: No provenance linkage to the input context and no AI-assisted analysis; effectively a duplicate of the deterministic unsupported-claim heuristic.

requirements.title: §16.1 Ambiguity detection (AI-assisted, Info — 'could represent' without follow-up)
requirements.evidence: No ambiguity/hedging-without-follow-up check found in ReportValidator.cs or any service; grep for 'ambiguity'/'could represent' returns only PRD/docs, no code rule.
requirements.gap: Add an (AI-assisted or deterministic) check that flags hedged language lacking an associated follow-up recommendation; emit Info finding.

requirements.title: §16.1 Sex/age-appropriate language (Deterministic, Warning — pediatric language in adult report)
requirements.evidence: No sex/age-appropriateness check; grep for 'pediatric'/'sex'/'age-appropriate' in backend src returns no validation code. Study metadata lacks structured patient sex/age fields used by any check.
requirements.gap: Requires patient sex/age in study context plus a lexicon check; neither the data nor the rule exists.

requirements.title: §16.1 Allergy / contrast safety (Deterministic, Warning — contrast mentioned with allergy flag)
requirements.evidence: No allergy/contrast-safety check; grep for 'allergy'/'contrast safety' in backend src returns nothing. ContrastPhaseRe only checks that a phase is documented (contrast_phase_documented), not allergy cross-checking.
requirements.gap: Needs an allergy flag in study/patient context and a rule that fires when contrast is described against that flag; data + rule missing.

requirements.title: §16.1 Dose language CT/IR (Deterministic, Info — DLP / CTDIvol mention check)
requirements.evidence: No DLP/CTDIvol dose check; grep for 'DLP'/'CTDIvol'/'dose' in backend src returns no validation rule (only billing/dosing-unrelated hits).
requirements.gap: Add a deterministic rule that checks for DLP/CTDIvol presence on CT/IR studies; Info severity. Straightforward regex once wired.

requirements.title: §16.1 Spelling / grammar (Hunspell + LanguageTool, Info — medical dictionaries layered)
requirements.evidence: No spell/grammar engine; grep for 'Hunspell'/'LanguageTool' finds only PRD/docs. The only 'grammar' references are prose inside rulebook prompt_blocks (cleanup instructions), not a validation check.
requirements.gap: Integrate a spell/grammar library (or equivalent .NET package) with layered medical dictionaries and surface Info findings; entirely absent.

requirements.title: §16.1 Acronym expansion (Tenant lexicon, Info — expand on first use)
requirements.evidence: TenantLexicon exists and is used for forbidden-term checks (ReportValidator.cs:50-70), but there is no acronym-expansion-on-first-use rule. Grep for 'acronym'/'expand on first use' returns docs only.
requirements.gap: Add a lexicon-driven acronym rule that flags unexpanded acronyms on first use; reuse TenantLexicon plumbing.

requirements.title: §16.1 Prior-report inconsistency (AI-assisted + semantic diff, Warning — unexpected interval change without note)
requirements.evidence: PriorComparePanel.tsx + /compare-prior produce a section-level string diff (changed/identical) for display; PriorReportSummary feeds the hallucination support text.
requirements.gap: This is a literal text diff for the UI, not an AI-assisted semantic diff that flags unexpected interval change lacking an explanatory note; no validation finding is generated. Needs an AI-assisted comparison emitting a Warning.

requirements.title: §16.1 Quantitative comparison (Deterministic RECIST math, Info — lesion change classification)
requirements.evidence: No RECIST/sum-of-diameters/response-classification logic anywhere; grep for 'RECIST'/'target lesion'/'sum of diameters' in backend returns no files. Measurement rules only check presence/consistency.
requirements.gap: Implement RECIST math (target-lesion sums, %change, PR/SD/PD classification) emitting an Info finding; non-trivial clinical logic.

requirements.title: §16.2.1 AI text visibly marked until reviewed (color + screen-reader annotation + accessibility opt-out)
requirements.evidence: ReportClient.tsx applies .ai-mark to AI-authored sections, stores aiHighlightsJson, shows an 'AI-generated text is highlighted' banner, and clears marks on acknowledge/edit; .ai-mark is the locked purple family per CLAUDE.md.
requirements.gap: No explicit screen-reader-friendly annotation (e.g. aria-label/visually-hidden 'AI generated') on marked regions and no per-user accessibility opt-out toggle for the color mark; color-only signal is an a11y gap vs the clause.

requirements.title: §16.2.2 Final export requires explicit user confirmation (cannot be bypassed by automation)
requirements.evidence: Export buttons in ReportClient.tsx are disabled unless status is Acknowledged/Exported (exportAllowed gate, lines 366-368, 506-510); Acknowledge requires a confirm() and zero blockers (acknowledge() + blockers>0 disabling).
requirements.gap: Confirmation is a single client-side acknowledge step; server also advances status on validate, but export gating is enforced. Minor: a server-side export precondition assertion would harden against API-level automation.

requirements.title: §16.2.3 Critical-finding suggestions require explicit confirmation + critical-results workflow routing
requirements.evidence: CheckCriticalLanguage only emits a Blocker finding requiring communication language in text; there is no critical-results routing entity/workflow (grep for 'CriticalResult'/'critical results workflow' in Domain/Entities and services returns no workflow), and no explicit confirmation step for critical-finding suggestions.
requirements.gap: Build a critical-results acknowledgement/routing workflow (notify, document, track) and require explicit confirmation when a critical finding is suggested; currently only a text-validation nudge.

requirements.title: §16.2.4 Report signing happens in customer RIS/EHR unless RadioPad approved as signing endpoint
requirements.evidence: RadioPad never auto-signs (CLAUDE.md safety boundary); ReportClient.tsx provides explicit Sign as Primary / Co-Signer / Addendum actions, and CopyToRisButton + Export FHIR/HL7 (Hl7OruSerializer, FhirDiagnosticReportSerializer) support pushing to the official system. In-system signing is an explicit, gated action.
requirements.gap: The 'approved-as-signing-endpoint per tenant' toggle is implicit (signing is always available in-app); a tenant policy flag distinguishing RIS-only vs in-system signing would fully match the clause.

requirements.title: §16.2.5 User edits tracked at keystroke-aggregation level for analytics/rulebook eval (raw text stays in tenant)
requirements.evidence: ReportClient.tsx saves full section text on blur (update/patch); no aggregation of edit deltas/keystroke metrics is captured. AnalyticsService and AnomalyDetector do not record edit-distance/acceptance analytics. No edit-aggregation entity found.
requirements.gap: Add edit-aggregation telemetry (e.g. AI-acceptance %, edit distance) emitted without raw text leaving tenant; entirely absent.

requirements.title: §16.2.6 No silent retries on PHI paths — operator visibility mandatory for every retry
requirements.evidence: AiGateway.RouteAsync (AiGateway.cs) performs a single attempt and audits AiResponse/ProviderBlocked/PolicyViolation; there is no retry loop, but equally no explicit no-silent-retry guarantee or operator-visible retry surfacing. Provider adapters were not found to implement audited retries.
requirements.gap: The clause is effectively unmet as a stated control: there is no retry-with-visibility mechanism nor an explicit no-retry assertion documented/enforced on PHI paths. Add audited, operator-visible retry handling (or an explicit policy + test).

requirements.title: §16.2.7 'Why this suggestion?' panel mandatory and always accessible
requirements.evidence: No 'Why this suggestion' panel in ReportClient.tsx or report routes; grep matches for 'Why this' resolve to docs/PRD only, not a UI component. There is a separate read-only /prompts Prompt Studio (PROGRESS §16.4) but it is not an always-accessible per-suggestion rationale panel.
requirements.gap: Build an always-accessible per-suggestion explanation panel (model, prompt/rulebook version, supporting findings/spans, rationale) on the report editor; currently absent.

requirements.title: §16.3 Confidence presentation — rule status (pass/warn/block)
requirements.evidence: ReportClient.tsx Validation panel groups findings by Blocker/Warning/Info with badges and a Quality score; validation/page.tsx aggregates across drafts. Severity mapping (Blocker→red, Warning→amber, Info→blue) per CLAUDE.md.
requirements.gap: None for this sub-item.

requirements.title: §16.3 Confidence presentation — unsupported-statement warnings with source-span highlights
requirements.evidence: UnsupportedClaimFinding (ReportClient.tsx:829) shows the offending impression sentence in .ai-mark with an 'Edit Findings' jump.
requirements.gap: It quotes the sentence but does not highlight the supporting/contradicting SOURCE spans in Findings; no character-span linkage exists (ValidationFinding only carries Section + Snippet).

requirements.title: §16.3 Confidence presentation — missing-input warnings
requirements.evidence: Required-section blockers and prior_comparison_required cover some missing inputs; HallucinationDetector treats sparse support as unsupported.
requirements.gap: No explicit 'missing input' category surfaced to the panel distinct from required-section blockers (e.g. missing prior, missing technique metadata) per the clause.

requirements.title: §16.3 Confidence presentation — contradiction warnings
requirements.evidence: Deterministic negation_conflict, laterality_consistency, and level_consistency surface contradictions in the panel.
requirements.gap: Contradiction coverage is limited to a few hand-coded patterns; no general AI-assisted contradiction detector across findings/impression/prior.

requirements.title: §16.3 Confidence presentation — source/context trace (provenance graph)
requirements.evidence: No provenance graph data structure or UI; AiGateway audits input/output SHA-256 hashes only (AiGateway.cs:125-144), not a traceable source→claim graph. No provenance component in report routes.
requirements.gap: Build a provenance/context-trace model linking output claims to source spans and render it; absent.

requirements.title: §16.3 Confidence presentation — model/rulebook version pin
requirements.evidence: PromptVersion is recorded in AiRequest/audit (ReportingService.PromptVersion, AiGateway), and rulebook version is selectable in the editor; provider/model is stored per provider.
requirements.gap: The Validation panel does NOT display the pinned model + rulebook version next to the result; the pin exists in audit/usage data but is not surfaced as the clause requires.

requirements.title: §16.3 Confidence presentation — 'Needs radiologist review' banner
requirements.evidence: ReportClient.tsx shows an 'AI-generated text is highlighted below — review every section before acknowledging' banner when AI highlights exist.
requirements.gap: This is an AI-highlight notice, not the specific persistent 'Needs radiologist review' status banner tied to validation/confidence state; wording and trigger differ from the clause.

requirements.title: §16.4 Safety RFC process — RFC doc, golden-case regression gate (>=99% blockers / >=95% warnings on >=3 rulebooks), CSWG review, 30-day pre-announce
requirements.evidence: ValidationPackService supports import/create/approve/deprecate/run with pass/fail summaries and append-only audit (ValidationPackApproved/Run); on-disk golden cases exist under rulebooks/_tests/. RulebookGovernanceException enforces Approved-only rulebooks in production AI runs.
requirements.gap: No enforced RFC artifact requirement, no threshold gate (>=99%/>=95% across >=3 reference rulebooks) blocking a change, no Clinical Safety Working Group sign-off step, and no 30-day tenant pre-announcement mechanism. The tooling exists but the gated process does not.

requirements.title: §16.5 Adversarial robustness — prompt-injection stripping in dictation before model invocation
requirements.evidence: DictationCleanupService.CleanupAsync (Application/Services/DictationCleanupService.cs) passes raw dictation straight into the user prompt with no directive stripping/sanitization; the only 'strip'/'injection' hits in desktop/daemon are unrelated (legacy daemon, plugin sandbox, CSP). No red-team corpus or directive filter.
requirements.gap: Add a pre-model sanitizer that detects/strips suspected injected directives in dictation and a red-team test corpus; absent on the active stack.

requirements.title: §16.5 Adversarial robustness — output watermarking (EE: cryptographic provenance + optional perturbation)
