requirements.evidence: No watermarking; grep for 'watermark' returns docs/PRD only. AiGateway records output hashes for audit but does not embed cryptographic provenance or perturbation watermark in emitted text.
requirements.gap: Implement EE output watermarking (cryptographic provenance signature, optional perturbation); not present.

requirements.title: §16.5 Adversarial robustness — source-span attestation (model outputs include char spans; UI verifies spans exist)
requirements.evidence: AiResult (Records.cs:19) carries only Text/Provider/Model/tokens — no span fields; no prompt instructs the model to emit input character spans and no UI verification of spans. ValidationFinding has no span offsets.
requirements.gap: Add span-attested output contract (model returns char spans into input), and UI/server verification that spans exist; entirely missing.

requirements.title: §16.5 Adversarial robustness — refusal handling (fall back to deterministic template; never silently emit empty content)
requirements.evidence: DictationCleanupService.ParseSections falls back to surfacing raw text as Findings when the model returns non-JSON (DictationCleanupService.cs:152-158), avoiding silent empties in that path; AiGateway throws/audits on provider errors rather than emitting empty content.
requirements.gap: No explicit refusal detector with a deterministic template fallback for impression/draft generation; an empty/refused model response in RunAsync is not specifically detected and replaced with a deterministic template. Partial coverage only in dictation cleanup.

requirements.title: §16.5 Adversarial robustness — toxic-content filter off by default, configurable for patient-facing modes
requirements.evidence: No toxic-content filter or configuration toggle found; patient_friendly rewrite mode exists (ReportingService SupportedModes) but no associated content-safety filter setting in TenantSettings or gateway.
requirements.gap: Add a configurable toxic-content filter (default off, enableable for patient-facing modes); absent.

area: Desktop App / RadioPad Studio (PRD v2 §14.7, DESK-001 … DESK-025)
summary: RadioPad Studio is a Tauri 2 shell (desktop/src-tauri) that loads the static Next.js export and supervises the bundled .NET API sidecar. The security/data spine of §14.7 is genuinely built and reasonably solid: AES-256-GCM-encrypted offline drafts + local cache keyed off the OS keyring, a sha256+ed25519 plugin/model verifier with OS-level sandbox launch, a PHI-redacting tracing layer, a global-hotkey set, secure-clipboard with TTL + clear-on-blur, RFC-8628 device pairing (backend DeviceAuthController + /pair UI + keyring token), a signed PACS-plugin manifest loader, and ed25519 auto-update + per-OS code-signing documented in runbooks. About 11 of the 25 line items are implemented or close; ~4 are partial; ~10 are entirely missing. The biggest gaps are the day-to-day UX surfaces a reporting-room hero is judged on: there is NO tray/menu-bar daemon control + provider switcher (DESK-014, P0), NO OS-native notifications (DESK-013), NO multi-monitor floating overlay or 380x600 mini-panel (DESK-011/DESK-024), NO crash reporter (DESK-017), NO accessibility/AT compatibility work (DESK-018, P0), and NO power-saver, Wacom/pen, foot-pedal, locale hot-reload, per-user themes/high-contrast, or fleet management. Several "implemented" items are also functionally narrower than the clause (last-write-wins instead of full conflict resolution; regex redactor instead of Presidio + log rotation; SQLite-store encryption instead of SQLCipher; no proven ARM64 build matrix). Where the PRD named a specific OSS library that conflicts with the locked stack (e.g. SQLCipher, Presidio, Sentry-OSS), I judged by function on the Tauri/.NET stack per CLAUDE.md, but I still mark items partial/missing when the FUNCTION (rotation, true conflict resolution, crash capture) is absent — not merely the named library.

requirements.title: DESK-001 — Windows (x64+ARM64), macOS (Intel+Apple Silicon), Linux (x64+ARM64)
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json bundle.targets = [msi, dmg, deb, appimage, rpm] covers all three OSes; macOS minimumSystemVersion 11.0 and hardenedRuntime set; UPDATE_SIGNING.md lists windows-x86_64, darwin-x86_64, darwin-aarch64, linux-x86_64 update platforms. Tauri inherently supports Apple Silicon + Intel.
requirements.gap: No build evidence for ARM64 on Windows or Linux: the update manifest in UPDATE_SIGNING.md omits windows-aarch64 and linux-aarch64, and there is no CI matrix file in-repo proving an ARM64 cross-compile. macOS Apple Silicon is covered (darwin-aarch64). Need ARM64 build/release matrix for Windows + Linux to reach 100%.

requirements.title: DESK-002 — Auto-start and manage radiopadd local daemon
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/sidecar_manager.rs spawns the bundled `radiopad-api` sidecar via app.shell().sidecar(), supervises it with up to MAX_RESTARTS=3 restarts, exponential-ish restart_delay, a health-check loop (backend_health::check_ready_async) and emits radiopad://backend-status. Wired in main.rs setup() via sidecar_manager::start(). capabilities/default.json scopes shell:allow-spawn to the radiopad-api sidecar only.
requirements.gap: The local daemon managed is the .NET API sidecar (radiopad-api), which satisfies the locked-stack equivalent of `radiopadd`. Minor: restart cap of 3 then gives up with a `failed` state; no user-facing restart control (that overlaps the missing tray DESK-014). Functionally complete for auto-start/manage.

requirements.title: DESK-003 — Global hotkeys (dictation, generate impression, rewrite, copy, paste, voice command toggle)
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/main.rs registers Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+{R,N,I,W,D,C}: D=dictate, I=generate-impression, W=rewrite, C=secure-copy-section, N=new-report, R=restore-window. Events forwarded as radiopad://* and translated in frontend/app/ShellBridge.tsx into navigation/DOM actions.
requirements.gap: Two of the six named actions are missing: there is no PASTE hotkey and no VOICE-COMMAND-TOGGLE hotkey (voiceCommands.ts exists in frontend but is not bound to a global shortcut). 'Copy' is implemented as secure-copy-section. Add paste + voice-command-toggle shortcuts and wire them in ShellBridge.

requirements.title: DESK-004 — Secure clipboard mode with automatic timeout (configurable, default 30s)
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/main.rs secure_copy(text, ttl_ms) writes to clipboard, remembers ownership, and clears after ttl_ms only if RadioPad still owns the value; emits radiopad://clipboard-cleared. clear_on_blur_enabled reads secureClipboard.clearOnBlur from the store and clears on focus-loss (window Focused(false) handler).
requirements.gap: The 30s default is NOT enforced in Rust — the TTL is supplied entirely by the frontend caller; no default applied if omitted, and no documented/UI-exposed 'configurable' setting binding the default. Add a Rust-side default (30_000ms) when ttl_ms is unset/zero and expose a settings control for the timeout value.

requirements.title: DESK-005 — Local encrypted cache (SQLCipher) for drafts and temporary inputs
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/local_cache.rs implements per-scope AES-256-GCM encrypted cache with per-entry TTL (default 3600s) and lazy eviction; key from crypto_keyring::master_key() (32-byte key generated on first run, stored hex in OS keyring, never on disk in plaintext). Backed by tauri-plugin-store JSON files (local-cache.<scope>.enc.json).
requirements.gap: Implementation is AES-256-GCM over a JSON store, NOT SQLCipher. Per CLAUDE.md stack-lock the encryption FUNCTION is satisfied (at-rest AES-256, OS-keyring key). However it is a key/value store, not a SQL cache, and the at-rest values are individually encrypted within an otherwise-plaintext JSON envelope (keys/structure visible). Acceptable functionally; mark partial because it diverges from a true encrypted-database cache and store-file metadata is not encrypted.

requirements.title: DESK-006 — Offline draft editing with full conflict resolution on reconnect
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/offline_drafts.rs persists encrypted drafts (AES-256-GCM) with an append-only audit log (offline_drafts_audit.log). frontend/lib/offlineDrafts.ts buffers drafts and replays them on `online`/Capacitor networkStatusChange via api.reports.create + patch; ShellBridge.tsx calls startAutoSync().
requirements.gap: Conflict resolution is last-write-wins only (offlineDrafts.ts: `rev` is a 'monotonic edit counter for last-write-wins'; sync blindly PATCHes server). 'Full conflict resolution' (server-vs-local diff/merge, ETag/version check, user-facing conflict UI) is absent — there is no compare of server rev against local rev before overwrite, so concurrent server edits are silently clobbered. Needs version/ETag negotiation + merge or conflict-resolution UI.

requirements.title: DESK-007 — Local PACS/RIS bridge plugin host
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/pacs_plugins.rs is a plugin host: enumerates signed manifests under the OS plugins dir, verifies sha256+ed25519 via sandbox.rs, returns verified/unverified PluginRecords, and toggles a local .enabled flag without mutating the signed manifest. Commands pacs_plugins_list/verify/set_enabled registered in main.rs. SDK + example at desktop/plugin-sdk/ (manifest.schema.json, example-sectra-plugin).
requirements.gap: It hosts/verifies/enables plugin manifests but does not actually RUN a PACS/RIS bridge: no connector process launch from this path, no DICOM/HL7/RIS transport, no data exchange with a third-party reporting tool. It is a manifest registry + trust gate, not a working bridge. Needs an actual plugin execution/IPC channel that brokers PACS/RIS data.

requirements.title: DESK-008 — Device authorization and tenant pairing (QR code or device-flow OAuth)
requirements.evidence: backend AuthFlowsController.cs DeviceAuthController implements RFC 8628 fully (authorize/approve/deny/token with slow_down, authorization_pending, expired_token, access_denied, consumed claim, device fingerprint, audited UserLogin). frontend/app/pair/page.tsx drives the device side, fetches device_fingerprint (Tauri), polls, and stores the bearer via device_pairing_token_set into the OS keyring (device_pairing.rs + crypto_keyring.rs).
requirements.gap: Two gaps: (1) NO QR-code option — only the manual user_code entry path exists (PRD allows 'QR code OR device-flow', so device-flow alone is acceptable, but QR is not implemented). (2) The approval surface is broken: authorize returns verificationUri='/devices' and pair/page tells the operator to approve at /devices, but NO /devices route exists in frontend/app (only /pair). An operator cannot approve a user_code through the UI; approval currently only works via the raw API/dev headers. Add the /devices approval page (and optionally QR).

requirements.title: DESK-009 — Local model/plugin execution where enabled
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/sandbox.rs verify_plugin() does constant-time sha256 check + optional ed25519 detached-signature verification (pubkey from RADIOPAD_PLUGIN_PUBKEY, PEM or hex), with a fail-closed release policy (unsigned refused in release, pubkey-set => signature mandatory). launch_sandboxed() runs verified binaries under macOS sandbox-exec (.sb profile), Linux bwrap/unshare, Windows AppContainer/WDAC, or noop fallback, in a per-plugin workdir. Exposed via verify_plugin command in main.rs; whisper.md documents a local-only Whisper model sidecar path. Unit tests for hash-mismatch + ct_eq.
requirements.gap: Verification + sandboxed launch are real and tested. Minor: the local Whisper model execution (desktop/whisper.md) is documented but not wired in code (no cpal capture / whisper sidecar binding present), and the Windows path is a documented stub (spawns directly with an AppContainer env flag rather than creating the AppContainer in-process). Core requirement met.

requirements.title: DESK-010 — Local logs with PHI redaction (Presidio + tenant rules) and rotation
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/log_redactor.rs installs a RedactingWriter MakeWriter over the global tracing subscriber (init() called first in main.rs) that regex-redacts name tokens, DOB/date-near-name, and 6-12 digit MRN-like runs to <redacted:phi>; passes ICD-10 codes and short numbers. Unit tests verify MRN/name redaction and ICD-10/short-number pass-through.
requirements.gap: Two gaps vs clause: (1) redaction is a small hand-rolled regex set, NOT Presidio and NOT tenant-rule-driven (no per-tenant pattern config). (2) NO log rotation — the writer forwards to stderr; there is no rolling file appender / size/time-based rotation (tracing-appender not used; rolling cfg absent). Add file sink + rotation and pluggable tenant redaction rules (Presidio-equivalent NER optional).

requirements.title: DESK-011 — Multi-monitor support; floating mini overlay above third-party reporting tools
requirements.evidence: No match found: grep for monitor/multi-monitor/available_monitors/set_position across desktop/ returns nothing; tauri.conf.json defines a single 'main' window only, no always-on-top/overlay window, no monitor enumeration. No floating/always-on-top window is created in main.rs.
requirements.gap: Entire feature absent: need monitor enumeration (available_monitors), an always-on-top, frameless, click-through-capable overlay window that floats above third-party PACS apps, and per-monitor placement/persistence.

requirements.title: DESK-012 — Battery-aware power-saver mode
requirements.evidence: No match found: no battery/power/AC-status detection anywhere in desktop/ (no battery crate, no power-source query, no throttling logic in main.rs or sidecar_manager.rs). Health loop polls at a fixed 500ms/5s cadence regardless of power state.
requirements.gap: Entire feature absent: need OS power-source/battery detection and a power-saver mode that reduces polling/animation/background work on battery.

requirements.title: DESK-013 — OS-native notifications (critical results, peer-review assignments)
requirements.evidence: No match found: grep for notification/Notification across desktop/ returns nothing; Cargo.toml does not depend on tauri-plugin-notification; tauri.conf.json plugins list (shell, dialog, clipboard-manager, store, updater) has no notification plugin; no notify permission in capabilities/default.json.
requirements.gap: Entire feature absent: add tauri-plugin-notification (+ permission), and wire backend events for critical results / peer-review assignments to OS-native toasts.

requirements.title: DESK-014 — Tray/menu bar daemon control + provider switcher
requirements.evidence: No match found: grep for tray/TrayIcon/menu_bar/SystemTray across desktop/ returns nothing. main.rs builds no TrayIconBuilder and no menu; Cargo.toml tauri features = [] (no 'tray-icon' feature). A provider switcher exists only as web pages (/providers, /admin/providers) — not as a tray control.
requirements.gap: Entire P0 feature absent: need a system-tray/menu-bar icon with daemon (sidecar) start/stop/restart controls and an in-tray provider switcher. This is a P0 line item and a defining 'unobtrusive reporting-room' surface.

requirements.title: DESK-015 — Auto-update with EdDSA-signed bundles and rollback if first boot fails
requirements.evidence: tauri-plugin-updater 2 in Cargo.toml; tauri.conf.json plugins.updater configured (channel endpoint template, createUpdaterArtifacts 'v1Compatible', dialog false). desktop/UPDATE_SIGNING.md documents ed25519 (EdDSA) signing, three channels with separate keypairs, KMS/SSM key custody, staged rollout manifest, key rotation, and that the updater rejects version<=installed and binds the digest into the signature.
requirements.gap: Two gaps: (1) plugins.updater.pubkey is empty ('') in the committed config — updates are unverifiable until CI injects TAURI_SIGNING_PUBLIC_KEY (documented, but no default/dev key, so update verification is non-functional in the repo state). (2) 'rollback if first boot fails' is NOT implemented in code — no first-boot health gate / watchdog that reverts to the previous version on a failed launch; it is not described in UPDATE_SIGNING.md either. Add a first-boot success sentinel + automatic rollback.

requirements.title: DESK-016 — Code-signed installers; macOS notarized; Windows EV cert
requirements.evidence: desktop/INSTALLER_HARDENING.md fully documents Windows EV cert on FIPS HSM (thumbprint injected via RADIOPAD_WIN_CERT_THUMBPRINT, SHA-256 digest, timestamp fallback chain, dual-sign, WDAC policy at desktop/wdac/RadioPad.xml), macOS Developer ID signing + notarytool notarization + stapling + Gatekeeper verify, and Linux dpkg-sig/rpmsign/AppImage GPG+ed25519. tauri.conf.json wires digestAlgorithm sha256, timestampUrl, entitlements.plist, hardenedRuntime true. Secret inventory + CI verify workflow referenced (desktop-installer-verify.yml).
requirements.gap: All signing is documentation + CI-time injection: tauri.conf.json ships certificateThumbprint=null and macOS signingIdentity=null, and the referenced workflows (.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml, desktop-installer-verify.yml) were not verified present in this audit. No actual signed artifact or live cert is in the repo (correctly, for security). Mark partial until the CI workflows + secrets exist and a signed/notarized build is produced.

requirements.title: DESK-017 — Crash reporter (opt-in, PHI-stripped) — Sentry self-hosted or Bugsnag-OSS
requirements.evidence: No match found: no sentry/bugsnag/panic-hook crash-reporting dependency in desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml; no std::panic::set_hook or minidump capture in main.rs; the only 'crash' references are doc filenames. No opt-in crash-report consent UI in frontend.
requirements.gap: Entire feature absent: add a panic/crash capture hook with PHI stripping (can reuse log_redactor::redact), an opt-in consent gate, and a self-hosted Sentry/Bugsnag-OSS-equivalent transport.

requirements.title: DESK-018 — Accessibility: NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, Orca compatibility
requirements.evidence: No match found in desktop/ for NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver/Orca or any AT-specific handling. The shell is a Tauri WebView rendering the Next.js app; docs/ui-ux-audit/07-accessibility-audit.md (PROGRESS.md Iter-43) reports 13 a11y findings, missing skip link, missing focus traps, EmptyState without aria-live — i.e. the web UI itself is not yet AT-clean, and no desktop-specific screen-reader verification exists.
requirements.gap: P0 with no dedicated work: WebView a11y inherits the documented frontend a11y gaps, and there is no evidence of testing/fixing against NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver/Orca, nor native accessibility-tree exposure verification. Needs an a11y pass on the web UI plus per-AT desktop verification.

requirements.title: DESK-019 — Wacom / pen / touch input for finding annotations and structured drawing
requirements.evidence: No match found: no pointer/pen/stylus/Wacom/PointerEvent pressure handling or drawing-canvas component in desktop/ or frontend annotation surfaces (grep hits on 'pen' were substring matches inside unrelated words). No structured-drawing/annotation feature exists.
requirements.gap: Entire P2 feature absent: need a pressure-aware pen/touch annotation canvas (PointerEvents w/ pressure/tilt) for finding markup and structured drawing.

requirements.title: DESK-020 — Foot-pedal support (USB HID) for dictation start/stop and jog
requirements.evidence: No match found: no HID/hidapi/usb dependency in Cargo.toml and no foot-pedal handling in desktop/ (grep 'HID'/'foot-pedal' produced only substring matches). Dictation start/stop is only via global hotkey and UI button.
requirements.gap: Entire feature absent: add USB-HID device enumeration (e.g. hidapi crate) and map foot-pedal events to dictation start/stop and jog.

requirements.title: DESK-021 — Multi-language UI with hot reload of locale
requirements.evidence: frontend/lib/i18n.ts supports 6 locales (en/es/de/fr/pt/hi) with a full negotiation chain (query > cookie > Accept-Language > tenant default > en) and per-user PUT /api/users/me/locale; frontend/components/LocalePicker.tsx changes locale; PROGRESS.md Iter-35/40 confirm i18n keys across all 6 locales.
requirements.gap: NOT hot reload: LocalePicker.onChange explicitly calls window.location.reload() ('Hard reload so server + client message bundles realign'), and docs/ui-ux-audit notes 'LocalePicker does full page reload'. Hot reload (swap message bundle in place without a page reload, e.g. via IntlBoundary state) is missing. Multi-language UI itself is implemented.

requirements.title: DESK-022 — Per-user color themes and high-contrast modes
requirements.evidence: No match found: grep for high-contrast/prefers-contrast/data-theme/forced-colors across frontend/app returns nothing. CLAUDE.md explicitly LOCKS tokens and forbids dark mode/additional accent colours; globals.css defines a single fixed palette. No theme-switcher component or high-contrast stylesheet exists.
requirements.gap: Feature absent and partly in tension with the design lock (no dark mode / no new accent tokens). A compliant path is a high-contrast/forced-colors accessibility variant (does not add brand accents) plus a per-user theme preference store. Needs a high-contrast token set + @media (forced-colors) handling + per-user persistence, coordinated with the design-lock docs.

requirements.title: DESK-023 — Local-first plugin sandbox (WASM, capability-limited)
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/sandbox.rs provides capability-limited OS-process sandboxing: macOS sandbox-exec .sb profile (desktop/src-tauri/macos-plugin-sandbox.sb), Linux bwrap --unshare-all --die-with-parent / unshare fallback, Windows AppContainer/WDAC (desktop/wdac/RadioPad.xml), per-plugin temp workdir, plus sha256+ed25519 verification gate. Manifest capabilities[] field exists (pacs_plugins.rs).
requirements.gap: Sandbox is OS-process isolation, NOT a WASM runtime (no wasmtime/wasmer). The manifest 'capabilities' list is surfaced but not enforced as a capability boundary at runtime (it is metadata; no per-capability allow/deny mapping). Per stack-lock, OS sandboxing is a reasonable functional substitute for WASM, but capability-limiting is only partially realized. To fully meet: either a WASM host or enforce declared capabilities against the OS sandbox profile.

requirements.title: DESK-024 — Quick mini panel hotkey opens a 380x600 overlay for dictation + impression without leaving PACS
requirements.evidence: No match found: grep for mini-panel/overlay/380/quick-panel returns nothing in desktop/ (the 380x600 mini-panel is described in PRD/legacy docs only). main.rs registers no mini-panel hotkey and creates no second 380x600 window; tauri.conf.json defines only the main 1280x820 window.
requirements.gap: Entire P1 feature absent: add a dedicated global hotkey that opens a 380x600 always-on-top frameless overlay window hosting dictation + impression generation, independent of the main window so the user never leaves PACS. Depends on the same overlay infrastructure missing in DESK-011.

requirements.title: DESK-025 — Centralized fleet management hook (MDM, Intune, Jamf, Ansible)
requirements.evidence: No match found: no MDM/Intune/Jamf/Ansible managed-config support in desktop/ (grep returned only substring matches). No managed-policy reader (e.g. Windows registry HKLM policy keys, macOS managed preferences/profiles, or a config-file drop-in), no Ansible role/playbook, no MSI ADMX templates.
requirements.gap: Entire EE feature absent: add managed-configuration ingestion (Windows ADMX/registry policies for Intune, macOS configuration profile/managed prefs for Jamf, a deploy config file for Ansible) so fleet tools can pre-provision endpoint, channel, allowlists, etc.

area: Auth, Tenancy & Access Control (PRD v2 §14.1, AUTH-001 … AUTH-015)
summary: RadioPad has a strong, well-tested authentication core on the locked .NET 8 / Next.js 16 stack, but the §14.1 access-control surface is roughly half-complete. SOLID/implemented: SAML 2.0 SP with signed-assertion validation (SamlController), OIDC Authorization Code + PKCE (AuthController + OidcBearerMiddleware), passwordless magic-link (MagicLinkController) with chained per-email/per-IP rate limiting, TOTP MFA (MfaController, RFC 6238) and WebAuthn/FIDO2 passkeys (WebAuthnController, hand-rolled attestation parser), RFC 8628 device flow (DeviceAuthController), full SCIM 2.0 Users+Groups+role projection (ScimController), account lockout + global session revocation via SessionEpoch (LockoutPolicy + UsersController), and per-tenant + global IP allowlist (IpAllowlistMiddleware). Stateless HMAC bearer tokens with a 12h lifetime (RadioPadBearerTokens), surfaced through AuthSession inventory rows. The frontend has /login, /admin/sso, and /admin/security pages backing these. GAPS: email/PASSWORD sign-in is not wired (PasswordHash field exists but no verify endpoint — only dev-header signin + passwordless flows); RBAC defines only 6 of the 11 PRD roles (missing Resident, Fellow, Subspecialist, Researcher, Auditor/read-only); tenant isolation is row-level TenantId only (no per-tenant DB schema / storage prefix / cache namespace / queue subject); MFA enforcement is a global OIDC env flag rather than a per-tenant policy; and several enterprise controls are entirely absent in code (step-up auth AUTH-008, per-role/per-tenant session limits & idle timeouts AUTH-009, break-glass AUTH-010, service-to-service short-lived JWT + mTLS AUTH-011, tenant-facing API-key lifecycle AUTH-012, geo-restrictions AUTH-014, support impersonation AUTH-015), device trust AUTH-007 captures only an optional fingerprint with no signed cert / posture check. Several gaps are documented in docs/ and ADR-0004 but not built. Effort is judged on the LOCKED stack (function, not the PRD's named OSS library).

requirements.title: AUTH-001 — Email/password, magic link, OIDC, SAML 2.0
requirements.evidence: Magic link: backend/RadioPad.Api/src/RadioPad.Api/Controllers/AuthFlowsController.cs (MagicLinkController, SHA-256 hashed single-use token, rate-limited). OIDC Authorization Code + PKCE + nonce + JWKS validation: AuthController.BeginOidc/OidcCallback + OidcBearerMiddleware.cs. SAML 2.0 SP metadata + signed-assertion ACS with replay/audience/time-window checks: Controllers/SamlController.cs. WebAuthn passkeys: Controllers/WebAuthnController.cs. User.PasswordHash field exists (Entities.cs:47, 'ASP.NET Identity password hasher (dev only path)').
requirements.gap: No email/PASSWORD sign-in endpoint: AuthController.SignIn is dev-header-only ('there is no password'); grep for PasswordHash/VerifyHashedPassword across Controllers returns no verification path. Password as an interactive credential is unimplemented despite the field existing. To reach 100%: add a password verify endpoint (hash compare + lockout integration) or formally drop password from scope.

requirements.title: AUTH-002 — RBAC roles (Radiologist, Resident, Fellow, Subspecialist, Admin, Medical Director, Compliance Reviewer, IT Admin, Billing Admin, Researcher, Auditor read-only)
requirements.evidence: Permission-based RBAC engine is solid: Domain/Enums/Enums.cs UserRole enum + RbacPermission enum; Application/Security/RolePermissionMap.cs maps roles→permission sets; RolePermissionService + TenantedController.RequirePermission/RequireRole (ReportsController.cs); EndpointPermissionMatrix.cs documents gated endpoints; EndpointPermissionRbacTests cover enforcement.
requirements.gap: Only 6 of 11 PRD roles exist: Radiologist, ReportingAdmin, MedicalDirector, ComplianceReviewer, ItAdmin, BillingAdmin. MISSING: Resident, Fellow, Subspecialist, Researcher, and Auditor (read-only). 'Admin' maps to ReportingAdmin. Add the 5 missing UserRole enum members + RolePermissionMap entries (Auditor = read-only audit/reports), migration, and SCIM/UI role options.

requirements.title: AUTH-003 — Tenant isolation at app, DB schema, storage prefix, cache namespace, audit log, queue subject
requirements.evidence: App-level + audit-log isolation enforced: TenantedController.ResolveContextAsync (ReportsController.cs:19) resolves tenant/user from verified identity; every entity carries TenantId and queries filter on it (e.g. ScimController, WebAuthnController, RetentionWorker 'join through Reports to enforce tenant isolation'); IAuditLog rows are tenant-stamped.
requirements.gap: Isolation is single-DB ROW-LEVEL (TenantId column) only. No per-tenant DB schema, no storage prefix, no cache namespace, no queue subject partitioning (grep for StoragePrefix/CacheNamespace/QueueSubject = no matches; no global EF query filter, isolation is manual per-query). Full clause requires layered isolation across storage/cache/queue. To close: add per-tenant storage prefixing in blob layer, cache key namespacing, queue subject scoping, and ideally a global query filter as defence-in-depth.

requirements.title: AUTH-004 — MFA enforcement by tenant policy; WebAuthn/FIDO2 preferred, TOTP fallback
requirements.evidence: WebAuthn/FIDO2 (preferred) fully implemented incl. attestation verification + counter-regression detection: WebAuthnController.cs. TOTP (RFC 6238, 160-bit base32, ±1 step skew) fallback: MfaController in AuthFlowsController.cs. OIDC path can require MFA via amr claim: AuthController.cs:276 + OidcProfiles.cs DefaultRequireMfa.
requirements.gap: MFA enforcement is NOT a per-tenant policy: it is a single global env flag RADIOPAD_OIDC_REQUIRE_MFA applied to the OIDC path only; native magic-link/WebAuthn/device flows are not gated by a stored tenant MFA-required policy. No TenantSettings.MfaRequired field (grep confirms only MfaSecret/MfaEnabled on User). Add a per-tenant MFA policy column + enforcement at session mint across all flows.

requirements.title: AUTH-005 — SCIM 2.0 provisioning / deprovisioning
requirements.evidence: Controllers/ScimController.cs implements RFC 7644 Users + Groups (list/get/create/replace/patch/delete), tenant-scoped constant-time bearer auth, soft-delete deprovision (IsActive=false preserves audit chain), group→role projection via ScimGroupRoleMapJson, ServiceProviderConfig + ResourceTypes discovery. Audited via AuditAction.ScimGroupChanged. Tests: Iter35 + integration.
requirements.gap: Functionally complete for Okta/Azure AD provisioning. Minor: filter support is limited to userName/displayName eq; bulk operations advertised as unsupported. None block the requirement.

requirements.title: AUTH-006 — Emergency account lockout and global session revocation in ≤30 s
requirements.evidence: Sliding-window lockout (5 failures/15min → LockedUntil): Auth/LockoutPolicy.cs, accrued on TOTP/WebAuthn/magic-link failures. Admin lockout flips User.IsActive: UsersController.Lockout/Unlock (Iter31Controllers.cs:292). Global session revocation: UsersController.RevokeSessions bumps User.SessionEpoch which is folded into the HMAC token seed (RadioPadBearerTokens.Mint), instantly invalidating all outstanding bearers; per-session revoke via AuthController.RevokeSession/Logout. Tests: Iter32AuthTests RevokedSessionEpoch_InvalidatesExistingBearer.
requirements.gap: Revocation is effectively immediate (next request fails validation) since tokens are stateless HMAC keyed by epoch — there is no propagation lag to measure. The explicit ≤30s SLA is met by construction but is not asserted/monitored. Optional: add a latency assertion/metric to formally evidence the SLA.

requirements.title: AUTH-007 — Device trust policies for Studio and daemon (signed device cert + posture check)
requirements.evidence: RFC 8628 device authorization grant captures an optional DeviceFingerprint and stores AuthSession.DeviceFingerprintHash: DeviceAuthController in AuthFlowsController.cs (authorize/approve/deny/token poll).
requirements.gap: No signed device CERTIFICATE issuance/validation and no POSTURE check (OS/patch/disk-encryption attestation). Device identity is a self-asserted fingerprint string only, not a trust policy. To close: issue/verify per-device certs (mTLS or signed enrollment), add a posture-claim schema + tenant device-trust policy, and gate session mint on it.

requirements.title: AUTH-008 — Step-up auth for high-risk actions (rulebook promotion, billing changes, mass export)
requirements.evidence: High-risk endpoints are gated by static RBAC permissions only (e.g. RulebooksApprove, BillingManage, ReportsExport in RolePermissionMap.cs / EndpointPermissionMatrix.cs). 'high-risk' appears only as a docstring in EndpointPermissionMatrix.cs; grep for step-up/reauth/elevated/recent-auth in backend code = no functional mechanism.
requirements.gap: No step-up (fresh re-auth / recent-MFA / WebAuthn challenge) before sensitive operations. Requires: a 'recent auth' / acr-style claim or short-lived elevation token, a [RequireStepUp] gate on rulebook promotion, billing mutation, and mass-export endpoints, plus UI re-prompt.

requirements.title: AUTH-009 — Session limits per role and idle timeouts configurable per tenant
requirements.evidence: Token lifetime is a single fixed 12h constant: RadioPadBearerTokens.DefaultLifetime (RadioPadBearerTokens.cs:11). AuthSession rows record IssuedAt/ExpiresAt but there is no idle-timeout or per-role/per-tenant cap.
requirements.gap: No configurable idle timeout, no per-role session-limit (max concurrent sessions), no per-tenant session policy. grep for IdleTimeout/SessionLimit/SessionTimeout = no matches. Requires TenantSettings policy fields + enforcement (sliding idle expiry, concurrent-session cap eviction) in session mint/validate.

requirements.title: AUTH-010 — Break-glass access with mandatory justification, dual approval, and audit alert
requirements.evidence: Concept referenced only in docs (docs/08-user-docs/admin-guide.md, docs/03-architecture/auth-architecture.md). grep for break.?glass across backend src = no code matches.
requirements.gap: Entirely unimplemented in code: no break-glass request entity, no mandatory justification capture, no dual-approval workflow, no SecurityAlert on activation/expiry. Requires a full request/approve/time-boxed-grant feature with audit + alert wiring.

requirements.title: AUTH-011 — Service-to-service auth via short-lived JWTs (≤15 min) with mTLS at the gateway
requirements.evidence: User session tokens are HMAC bearers with a 12h lifetime (RadioPadBearerTokens). IOAuthTokenIssuer exists only for outbound provider OAuth refresh (Application/Abstractions/IOAuthTokenIssuer.cs, OAuthRefreshVault). grep for mTLS/ClientCertificate/service-to-service across backend src = no functional gateway code.
requirements.gap: No internal service-to-service identity: no short-lived (≤15min) service JWT issuer/audience model and no mTLS / client-certificate enforcement at the gateway/Kestrel. Requires a service-principal token issuer with ≤15min TTL + mTLS client-cert validation policy.

requirements.title: AUTH-012 — API key lifecycle: scoped, rotatable, revocable, hashed at rest
requirements.evidence: Tenant-scoped bearer secrets exist for specific integrations and are hashed/constant-time compared (TenantSettings.ScimBearerSecret, IngestBearerSecret, FhirWebhookSecret in Entities.cs); provider 'ApiKey' references are AI-provider credentials (ApiKeySecretRef), not customer API keys.
requirements.gap: No general tenant-facing API KEY product with scopes, rotation, revocation, and creation/listing UI+API. Existing secrets are single-purpose, manually configured, and not lifecycle-managed. Requires an ApiKey entity (hashed, scoped, prefix display, last-used), CRUD/rotate/revoke endpoints, and admin UI.

requirements.title: AUTH-013 — IP allowlist per tenant and per role
requirements.evidence: Per-tenant allowlist (TenantSettings.IpAllowlistJson, legacy IpAllowlistCidr) AND-ed with global RADIOPAD_IP_ALLOWLIST, fail-closed on invalid/lookup-failure, loopback exempt, X-Forwarded-For only when trusted: Middleware/IpAllowlistMiddleware.cs. Admin UI: frontend/app/admin/security/page.tsx ('Allowed networks'). Tested.
requirements.gap: PER-ROLE granularity is absent — allowlist is tenant-wide (and global) only; there is no role-scoped allowlist. Add a role-dimension to the allowlist model + middleware (resolve role before gating, or post-identity enforcement).

requirements.title: AUTH-014 — Geo-restrictions (block sign-ins from countries outside tenant policy)
requirements.evidence: grep for geo/country/geoip across backend src = no matches. No GeoIP resolution, no country policy field on TenantSettings.
requirements.gap: Entirely unimplemented: no IP→country lookup, no per-tenant allowed/blocked-country policy, no enforcement at sign-in. Requires a GeoIP provider integration + TenantSettings country policy + sign-in gate with audit.

requirements.title: AUTH-015 — Impersonation by support requires dual approval and is fully audited
requirements.evidence: grep for impersonat across backend src = no auth feature (matches are only in copilot/PRD/docs contexts). No 'act-as'/'on-behalf-of' token shape in RadioPadBearerTokens; no support-impersonation endpoint.
requirements.gap: No support impersonation capability at all: no dual-approval request flow, no time-boxed act-as session, no impersonation banner/marking, and no dedicated audit trail distinguishing impersonated actions. Requires a full impersonation feature (request → dual approve → scoped act-as token → audit every action with actor+subject).

summary: The RadioPad codebase implements a substantial, functional reporting workspace on the locked stack (Next.js 16 + ASP.NET Core 8 + Tauri 2). The fixed six-section editor, AI impression/draft/cleanup, multi-mode rewrite, "rewrite in my style", AI-mark visual marking, prior-report compare, copy-to-RIS, acknowledgement-gated export (text/PDF/DOCX/JSON/FHIR R4/HL7 ORU), full multi-radiologist signing + addenda, per-report versioning, and dictation (Web Speech + voice command mode) are all present and wired end-to-end. The biggest gaps versus §14.2 are: NO command palette (RPT-018, P0), NO real-time CRDT collaboration (RPT-015), NO comments/@-mentions (RPT-016), NO macros/autotext or snippet library (RPT-021/RPT-022, RPT-021 is P0), NO worklist sidebar with prioritization (RPT-029, P0), NO multi-study reporting view (RPT-030), NO citation insertion (RPT-023), NO bilingual side-by-side rendering (RPT-025), NO "Why this suggestion?" provenance panel (RPT-026, P0), NO peer-review/anonymization action (RPT-028), NO QR-provenance print PDF (RPT-027). Several "implemented" items are partial: autosave is on-blur PATCH + offline-draft buffer, not the 2s WAL-style encrypted autosave RPT-013 specifies; export lacks PDF/A-2u conformance, FHIR R5, and DICOM SR (RPT-011); change history has no undo/redo UI (RPT-014); AI lacks streaming/stop/regenerate (relevant to RPT-017 expectations from AI-013); structured/table/numeric input is template-declared but not rendered as real field/table widgets (RPT-003); conditional-by-modality sections are not engine-evaluated (RPT-002). Of 30 requirements: ~10 implemented, ~10 partial, ~10 missing. Several P0 items (RPT-015 is P1 but RPT-018, RPT-021, RPT-024 partial, RPT-026, RPT-029 are P0) remain missing.

requirements.title: RPT-001 Editor with sections (Indication, Technique, Comparison, Findings, Impression, Recommendations), configurable per template
requirements.evidence: frontend/app/reports/[id]/ReportClient.tsx defines SECTIONS array (lines 22-29) rendering all six sections as textareas; backend Report entity + ReportsController.Patch persists all six fields. applyTemplate() (ReportClient.tsx ~169-192) maps template sections into the report and pins templateId. templates/chest-ct.json etc. declare sections.
requirements.gap: The six sections are HARD-CODED in the frontend SECTIONS const, not derived from the template's sectionsJson. Templates can only seed placeholder text for the fixed six keys; they cannot add/remove/reorder sections or define custom-named sections. 'Configurable per template' is therefore only partially honored (scaffolding text, not layout).

requirements.title: RPT-002 Tenant-specific section layouts with conditional sections by modality
requirements.evidence: templates/*.json carry a top-level "modality"/"bodyPart" field and per-section "required" flags, but no conditional/when logic exists. No code evaluates conditional section visibility by modality. The editor always renders the same six sections regardless of tenant/modality.
requirements.gap: No conditional-section engine. Need template schema for conditional sections (when modality==X show section Y), tenant-scoped layout selection, and a frontend renderer that honors it. Currently zero implementation.

requirements.title: RPT-003 Free text, template-based, structured fields, table input, numeric measurement input
requirements.evidence: Free text: all sections are textareas (ReportClient.tsx ~533). Template-based: applyTemplate() seeds text. Numeric measurement EXTRACTION exists (MeasurementExtractionService, GET /api/reports/{id}/measurements, api.reports.measurements). templates/chest-ct.json declares "structured":[...] and "format":"bullets".
requirements.gap: Only free-text + template scaffolding are real input modes. There are NO structured-field widgets, NO table input UI, and NO numeric measurement INPUT controls — measurements are only parsed out of free text, not entered via structured fields. The 'structured' template metadata is not rendered as inputs.

requirements.title: RPT-004 Generate Draft Report from dictation + notes + measurements + study metadata
requirements.evidence: ReportingService.BuildPromptForMode 'draft' case (ReportingService.cs ~145-148) builds a structured draft prompt from modality/body part/indication/comparison + dictation (report.Findings). Exposed via POST /api/reports/{id}/ai mode='draft' (ReportsController.RunAi) and api.reports.runAi supports 'draft'. Routes through AiGateway (PHI policy + audit).
requirements.gap: Functionally present. Minor: the editor toolbar surfaces 'Generate impression' but not a dedicated 'Generate Draft' button (draft mode is reachable via API/voice but not a primary UI button); measurements are not explicitly injected into the draft prompt context.

requirements.title: RPT-005 Generate Impression from Findings
requirements.evidence: ReportingService.GenerateImpressionAsync + 'impression' prompt (ReportingService.cs 60-62, 139-141) 'Generate a concise impression (max 5 bullets) faithful to the Findings section.' Wired to toolbar 'Generate impression' button and runAi('impression') in ReportClient.tsx (lines 115-130, 437-439). Result marked with ai-mark and persisted via PATCH.
requirements.gap: None material.

requirements.title: RPT-006 Rewrite in my style (personal style memory, per-user, opt-in, encrypted)
requirements.evidence: frontend RewriteStylePanel.tsx lets user paste 2-5 prior reports to learn a style fingerprint; api.reports.rewriteInMyStyle POSTs /api/reports/{id}/rewrite?mode=in_my_style. Produces a rewrite with a styleFingerprint.
requirements.gap: Style is learned ad-hoc from pasted samples per request — there is NO persisted, per-user, encrypted, opt-in/deletable/exportable 'style memory' store (that is AI-024's intent and is not built). No backend persistence of the fingerprint between sessions. So the rewrite function works but the durable encrypted personal style memory the clause implies is absent.

requirements.title: RPT-007 Modes: concise, formal, patient-friendly, referring-physician summary, bilingual, structured
requirements.evidence: ReportsLifecycleController.Rewrite + ReportRewriteService support concise, formal, patient_friendly, referring_summary (ReportRewriteService.cs 32-38, BuildPrompt 84-100). Frontend REWRITE_MODES exposes those four (ReportClient.tsx 31-36). RewriteModeTests cover them.
requirements.gap: Only 4 of 6 listed modes exist. 'bilingual' and 'structured' rewrite modes are NOT implemented (TryParseMode rejects them, ReportsLifecycleController.cs 350-362).

requirements.title: RPT-008 AI-generated text visibly marked (color + screen-reader-friendly) until reviewed/edited
requirements.evidence: aiHighlights state + .ai-mark (purple) wrapper around AI-written sections (ReportClient.tsx 532-549); editing a section removes the highlight (onChange deletes the key); acknowledge() clears all highlights; aiHighlightsJson persisted on the report. UnsupportedClaimFinding also uses .ai-mark. CLAUDE.md design lock mandates .ai-mark.
requirements.gap: Color marking + clear-on-edit is solid, but there is NO explicit screen-reader annotation (no aria-label/visually-hidden 'AI generated' text) on the .ai-mark regions, which the clause explicitly requires ('screen-reader-friendly annotation'). The UI-UX audit (PROGRESS Iteration 43) flagged a11y gaps generally.

requirements.title: RPT-009 Side-by-side prior report comparison with semantic diff
requirements.evidence: PriorComparePanel.tsx renders a two-column current-vs-prior grid; backend GET /api/reports/{id}/compare-prior (ReportsController.cs 341-373) matches prior by accession-stem; GET /prior matches by body part. Uses .rp-diff-add/.rp-diff-remove classes for changed sections.
requirements.gap: Diff is COARSE (whole-section 'changed' boolean, not a semantic/word-level diff). No clinical-semantic diff (e.g., laterality/measurement deltas). Prior matching is heuristic (accession stem or body part), not a true patient-MRN linkage. 'Semantic diff' is not really met — it is a section-equality flag.

requirements.title: RPT-010 One-click copy to RIS/PACS via secure clipboard or PACS bridge
requirements.evidence: CopyToRisButton.tsx copies sanitized plain text (export/text?preview=true) to clipboard; under Tauri it calls secure_copy (auto-clear TTL 30s) and falls back to navigator.clipboard with delayed wipe. PACS vendor adapters exist (SectraIds7Adapter, Visage7Adapter, CarestreamVueAdapter, PacsVendorRouter) for a bridge path.
requirements.gap: Secure clipboard path is complete. The 'PACS bridge' write-back path (push report text into the PACS reporting system) is not clearly wired from this button — adapters exist mainly for study/worklist fetch; an explicit copy-to-PACS-report bridge action is not surfaced. Clipboard path fully satisfies the primary clause.

requirements.title: RPT-011 Export to plain text, PDF/A-2u, DOCX, JSON, FHIR R4/R5 DiagnosticReport, HL7 v2 ORU, DICOM SR
requirements.evidence: ReportsController export endpoints + serializers: text (BuildNarrative), JSON, PDF (QuestPDF, ReportDocumentRenderer.RenderPdf), DOCX (OpenXML, RenderDocx), FHIR R4 (FhirDiagnosticReportSerializer — resourceType DiagnosticReport, R4 profile), HL7 v2.5 ORU (Hl7OruSerializer). Frontend buttons for text/JSON/FHIR/PDF/DOCX in ReportClient.tsx toolbar.
requirements.gap: Three sub-formats are MISSING/incomplete: (1) PDF is plain Letter PDF, NOT PDF/A-2u conformant (QuestPDF default, no PDF/A flag). (2) FHIR is R4 ONLY — no R5 serializer (FhirDiagnosticReportSerializer.cs header says 'R4'). (3) NO DICOM SR export FOR REPORTS — DICOM SR converters exist only in the HL7<->DICOM SR ingest bridge (Hl7ToDicomSrConverter / OrthancBridge), not as a report export endpoint. No export/dicom-sr route or frontend button.

requirements.title: RPT-012 Radiologist acknowledgement required before final export/signing
requirements.evidence: ReportsController.RequireAcknowledgedForExport gates ALL export endpoints (returns 409 unless Status>=Acknowledged); Acknowledge endpoint blocks when validation blockers present unless tenant RequireZeroBlockers=false. Frontend export buttons disabled unless status Acknowledged/Exported (ReportClient.tsx exportAllowed ~367). Acknowledge confirms with the radiologist before marking reviewed.
requirements.gap: None material; matches safety-boundary 'AI never auto-signs'.

requirements.title: RPT-013 Autosave to encrypted local cache every 2s with WAL-style durability
requirements.evidence: Desktop Tauri encrypted stores exist: offline_drafts.rs (AES-256-GCM, OS-keyring key, append-only offline_drafts_audit.log) and local_cache.rs; frontend offlineDrafts.ts buffers drafts and syncs on 'online'/network reconnect. Editor PATCH (ReportsController.Patch) writes a ReportVersion snapshot per save (server-side durability).
requirements.gap: This is NOT the specified behavior. There is NO 2-second autosave timer — the web editor saves only onBlur (ReportClient.tsx textarea onBlur PATCH). The encrypted offline store is reconnect-driven sync, not a continuous 2s WAL. No write-ahead-log semantics. The 2s cadence + WAL durability clause is unmet.

requirements.title: RPT-014 Per-report change history (full undo/redo across sections, with edit attribution)
requirements.evidence: Backend records a ReportVersion on every PATCH with Sequence, AuthorUserId (attribution), Action='edit', and a SnapshotJson of all sections (ReportsController.Patch ~248-265). GET /api/reports/{id}/versions returns the last 50 versions. Addenda create IsAddendum versions.
requirements.gap: History is recorded with attribution, but there is NO undo/redo UI and no restore-version endpoint. The frontend never lists versions or lets the radiologist undo/redo across sections. 'Full undo/redo' interaction is absent — only an append-only snapshot log exists.

requirements.title: RPT-015 Collaboration: real-time multi-user editing via CRDT (Yjs)
requirements.evidence: Grep for Yjs/CRDT/y-websocket/Awareness across frontend+backend returns only PRD/docs/design-system files — no application code. No websocket collab server, no CRDT document model. Editing is single-user PATCH with last-write-wins on the offline buffer.
requirements.gap: Entirely unimplemented. Would require a CRDT document layer (Yjs or equivalent) + a realtime transport on the locked stack (e.g. ASP.NET SignalR or a websocket hub) + editor binding. None exists.

requirements.title: RPT-016 Comments and @-mentions tied to text ranges; resolvable
requirements.evidence: Grep for comment/mention entities/components: backend hits are only PermissionCatalog/validator/RetentionWorker incidental matches; no Comment/ReportComment entity, no DbSet, no comments controller, no frontend comments UI. No text-range anchoring model.
requirements.gap: No comment system at all. Need a comment entity (with text-range anchors), @-mention resolution against tenant users, resolve/unresolve state, and editor UI. Zero implementation.

requirements.title: RPT-017 Per-section AI actions with section-scoped prompts
requirements.evidence: Rewrite supports a section whitelist: api.reports.rewrite/rewriteInMyStyle take sections[] and ReportRewriteService.BuildPrompt honors the include set (ReportRewriteService.cs 102-121); the Rewrite popover lets the user pick a section (ReportClient.tsx 452-477) and RewriteStylePanel targets findings/impression/recommendations. Impression generation is inherently section-scoped (Findings->Impression).
requirements.gap: Section scoping exists for rewrite/style, but there is no general per-section action menu with arbitrary section-scoped prompts (e.g., inline 'AI on this section' affordance on each section block). Impression/draft/cleanup are fixed-mode, not freeform section-scoped prompts. Partial coverage of the clause's intent.

requirements.title: RPT-018 Command palette (Ctrl/Cmd-K) with fuzzy actions and rulebook-aware suggestions
requirements.evidence: Grep for command palette / cmdk / Cmd-K / fuzzy across frontend code returns only docs/design-system/i18n files; no CommandPalette component, no global keydown handler for Cmd/Ctrl-K, no fuzzy-action registry in the app.
requirements.gap: Completely missing — and this is a P0. Need a command palette component (keyboard-triggered), an action registry, fuzzy search, and rulebook-aware suggestion sourcing. None present.

requirements.title: RPT-019 Dictation: continuous, push-to-talk, hands-free voice commands
requirements.evidence: DictateButton.tsx uses Web Speech API with continuous=true (continuous dictation) and toggle start/stop; desktop uses Whisper-local via Tauri sidecar per comment. Voice command mode: voiceCommands.ts + ReportClient.tsx handleVoiceCommandTranscript executes 'generate impression'/'make concise'/'validate'/'cleanup dictation' hands-free, with auto-dismiss pills.
requirements.gap: Continuous + a hands-free voice-command set exist. But there is NO explicit push-to-talk (hold-to-talk) mode — only a click toggle. lang is hard-coded 'en-US' (flagged in UI-UX audit), limiting multilingual dictation. So 2 of the 3 named dictation modes are met; push-to-talk is missing.

requirements.title: RPT-020 Auto-formatting (bullet impression, sentence case, measurement normalization)
requirements.evidence: Impression prompt instructs 'max 5 bullets' (ReportingService.cs 140); templates declare "format":"bullets","maxBullets":5 (chest-ct.json). MeasurementExtractionService extracts/normalizes measurements (GET /measurements). Cleanup mode preserves numbers/laterality.
requirements.gap: Auto-formatting is achieved only indirectly via the AI prompt and is not a deterministic client-side formatter. There is NO explicit sentence-case transformer, NO automatic bullet reformatting of typed text, and measurement NORMALIZATION is read-only extraction, not an in-place auto-format applied to the editor text. The deterministic auto-format toolbar/feature is absent.

requirements.title: RPT-021 Macros / autotext (user, tenant, subspecialty scopes)
requirements.evidence: Grep for Macro/AutoText entities/DbSet/controllers in backend returns no matches; the only frontend 'snippet' reference is ValidationFinding.snippet (api.ts:462), unrelated. No macro expansion in the editor.
requirements.gap: No macros/autotext at all — and this is P0. Need a Macro entity with user/tenant/subspecialty scoping, CRUD endpoints, and editor-side trigger/expansion. Zero implementation.

requirements.title: RPT-022 Snippet library with versioning
requirements.evidence: No Snippet entity, DbSet, controller, or frontend snippet library. (Template versioning/approval exists for report TEMPLATES, but those are full report scaffolds, not reusable text snippets.)
requirements.gap: No snippet library and no snippet versioning. Distinct from templates. Zero implementation.

requirements.title: RPT-023 Citation insertion (linked to evidence module)
requirements.evidence: Grep for citation across frontend+backend application code returns no matches; no evidence module, no citation entity, no insert-citation editor action. (AI-021 'Quote-it' / 'follow-up phrasebook' is a different, separate feature.)
requirements.gap: No citation insertion and no evidence module to link to. Zero implementation.

requirements.title: RPT-024 Keyboard-first navigation and full screen-reader support
requirements.evidence: Some ARIA present: rewrite menu uses aria-haspopup/aria-expanded/role=menu (ReportClient.tsx 445-452), CopyToRisButton has aria-label + role=status/alert, voice toggle aria-pressed. Shell has documented a11y components.
requirements.gap: Not 'keyboard-first' or 'full' screen-reader support. The UI-UX audit (PROGRESS Iter 43) explicitly found: no skip link, missing focus traps, EmptyState lacks aria-live, inconsistent role semantics, AI-mark lacks SR annotation (see RPT-008). No global keyboard navigation scheme (also no command palette per RPT-018). Substantial a11y work remains for a P0 'full' claim.

requirements.title: RPT-025 Bilingual side-by-side rendering (e.g., AR + EN)
requirements.evidence: App has next-intl multi-locale UI chrome (LocalePicker, 6 locales) but NO bilingual side-by-side REPORT rendering. No bilingual rewrite mode (RPT-007 gap), no dual-column report language view, no translation-provenance rendering in the editor.
requirements.gap: No side-by-side bilingual report content rendering. Locale switching changes UI chrome only, not report body. Zero implementation of the clause.

requirements.title: RPT-026 Why this suggestion? panel (rulebook, prompt, model, input excerpt)
requirements.evidence: AI responses DO return provenance fields (provider, model, latencyMs, promptVersion, routedBy, selectedProviderId) from ReportsController.RunAi, and AiGateway audits prompt/model/hashes — but the frontend DISCARDS these (runAi only stores out.text). Grep for 'why this'/whyThis/WhyThisSuggestion panel returns no matches.
requirements.gap: No 'Why this suggestion?' UI panel exposing rulebook + prompt + model + input excerpt to the radiologist. The data is largely available server-side; it is simply not surfaced. P0. Needs a frontend panel + endpoint to fetch the input excerpt/rulebook/prompt for a given suggestion.

requirements.title: RPT-027 Print-optimized PDF with QR code linking to provenance hash (audit)
requirements.evidence: ReportDocumentRenderer.RenderPdf produces a basic Letter PDF (header/narrative/footer) with NO QR code and NO provenance-hash link. No QR library referenced. Audit chain/hashes exist server-side but are not embedded in the PDF.
requirements.gap: No QR code generation, no provenance-hash embedding, no print-optimized layout variant. EE-tier feature entirely unimplemented.

requirements.title: RPT-028 Send to peer review action with anonymization toggle
requirements.evidence: Grep for peer.review/peerReview/anonymiz across frontend+backend application code returns only PRD/docs/roadmap files. No peer-review entity, endpoint, controller, or UI action; no anonymization toggle.
requirements.gap: No peer-review workflow and no anonymization toggle. Zero implementation.

requirements.title: RPT-029 Worklist sidebar with prioritization (STAT/urgent/routine/screening)
requirements.evidence: Reports list page (frontend/app/reports/page.tsx) is a flat table sorted by UpdatedAt, with NO priority column, NO STAT/urgent/routine/screening classification, and NO worklist sidebar in the editor. ReportsController.List filters by modality/status/q only — no priority field on the Report entity. (PACS adapters mention 'worklist' fetch but no in-app prioritized worklist sidebar exists.)
requirements.gap: No priority field/enum on reports, no worklist sidebar component, no prioritization sort/filter. P0 and entirely missing as a workspace feature.

requirements.title: RPT-030 Multi-study reporting view (combined exam reads, e.g., CT chest+abdomen+pelvis)
requirements.evidence: Report is one-study-per-report (single StudyContext). The editor (ReportClient.tsx) and ReportsController operate on a single report/study. No combined multi-study read view, no linking of multiple studies into one reporting surface.
requirements.gap: No multi-study/combined-exam reporting model or view. Zero implementation.

area: PRD v2 §14.11 Subspecialty Modules + §14.12 Structured Reporting & RADS Engine
summary: RadioPad implements a PARTIAL, text-centric version of these two sections. What exists: (1) a flat library of ~20 approved subspecialty rulebooks in rulebooks/ (chest_ct, chest_xray, lung_screening/lung_lungrads, abdomen_ct/us, liver_lirads/liver_mri, brain_mri, head_ct_trauma, mammo_birads/mammography, prostate_pirads/prostate_mri, thyroid_us, knee/shoulder/pelvis/spine MRI, musculoskeletal_xr, cardiac_mri, paediatric_chest_xray) each with style/rules/prompt_blocks/output_schema; (2) a curated RADS terminology lookup (rulebooks/_terminology/rads.yaml) exposing BI/LI/PI/Lung/TI/C-RADS category codes via RadsService + /api/terminology/rads + a Terminology UI page; (3) regex-based RADS validation rules in ReportValidator (require a category in the impression, require it on its own line, enforce critical-result language for high categories, enforce follow-up language for TI-RADS>=3); (4) a RadLex sublexicon and FHIR CodeSystem export; (5) ValidationPack (golden-case) infrastructure with ~2 cases per rulebook. CRITICAL GAPS vs the PRD: the entire engine is FREE-TEXT, not structured. The Report entity has only narrative sections (Indication/Technique/Comparison/Findings/Impression/Recommendations) with NO structured RADS fields, NO key-image refs, NO RADS category column. Therefore the headline RADS-engine clauses are essentially unbuilt: RADS-001 auto-derivation from structured fields (NONE — RadsService is a passive lookup, validator only checks a category is *mentioned*), RADS-002 forbid contradicting manual category (NONE — no structured field to contradict), RADS-004 Fleischner calculator (no match anywhere), RADS-005 RECIST/iRECIST/mRECIST/Lugano/Cheson lesion tracker (no match anywhere), RADS-006 structured key images (none), RADS-007 bidirectional structured↔narrative sync (none — there is only narrative), RADS-008 RADS analytics/category churn/inter-reader variance (only a generic quality-score dashboard exists), RADS-009 TIRADS/O-RADS scoring forms (no scoring forms; O-RADS not even in terminology), RADS-011 NI-RADS (absent), RADS-012 "Why this category?" justification panel (none). O-RADS, CAD-RADS, NI-RADS are entirely absent from rads.yaml/rulebooks despite RADS-001 naming them. For §14.11, the six per-module deliverables are largely unmet: rulebooks fall far short of ≥5 per module, templates far short of ≥10 (only 5 templates total exist), golden cases far short of ≥50 (≈2 each), and there is NO module-bundle/manifest concept, NO RadElement CDE bindings, NO society-guideline link metadata beyond a single public ACR URL per RADS system. Roughly: §14.12 is ~25% complete (RADS-003/010 partial via terminology+rulebook checks; the structured-engine core is missing); §14.11 is ~30% complete (rulebook breadth exists but as flat text rulebooks, not versioned modules meeting the quantitative bars).

requirements.title: §14.11 SUB-* — Subspecialty module catalogue (15 named modules: Chest, Abdomen, Neuro, MSK, Breast, Cardiac, Pediatric, IR, Nuclear, Emergency, OB/Gyn, General US, Dental, Vascular, Head&Neck)
requirements.evidence: rulebooks/ holds ~20 approved YAML rulebooks spanning chest (chest_ct_v1, chest_xray_v1, lung_screening_ct_v1/lung_lungrads_v1), abdomen/body (abdomen_ct_v1, abdomen_us_v1, liver_lirads_v1, liver_mri_v1, pelvis_mri_v1), neuro (brain_mri_v1, head_ct_trauma_v1, spine_mri_v1), MSK (knee_mri_v1, shoulder_mri_v1, musculoskeletal_xr_v1), breast (mammo_birads_v1, mammography_v1), cardiac (cardiac_mri_v1), pediatric (paediatric_chest_xray_v1), prostate (prostate_pirads_v1, prostate_mri_v1), thyroid US (thyroid_us_v1). These cover roughly half the named subspecialties at a rudimentary level.
requirements.gap: No coverage rulebooks at all for several named modules: Nuclear/Molecular (SUB-NM), Dental/Maxillofacial (SUB-DT), Vascular (SUB-VS CTA/MRA/aneurysm), Head & Neck (SUB-HN), Interventional/procedure notes (SUB-IR), Obstetric fetal US/anatomy survey (SUB-OB), and a dedicated Emergency STAT pack (SUB-ER). Existing coverage is also thin per module.

requirements.title: §14.11 — Module = versioned BUNDLE of rulebooks+templates+terminology+checks+evidence+golden cases
requirements.evidence: No module/bundle/manifest entity or file exists. Glob for **/*module* returns only node_modules noise. Rulebooks are individual flat YAML files; ValidationPackService groups golden cases per single rulebook_id only, not per subspecialty module. No backend Module entity in Entities.cs.
requirements.gap: Build a Subspecialty Module concept: a versioned manifest that aggregates its rulebooks, templates, terminology bindings, validation checks, evidence links, and golden-case packs, with install/version/activation per tenant.

requirements.title: §14.11 — ≥5 subspecialty rulebooks per module
requirements.evidence: Each subspecialty currently has 1-2 rulebooks (e.g., breast = mammo_birads_v1 + mammography_v1; liver = liver_lirads_v1 + liver_mri_v1; most have one). Total rulebook count ~20 across ~10 subspecialties.
requirements.gap: Far below the ≥5-per-module bar. Need to author roughly 4+ additional rulebooks per subspecialty module.

requirements.title: §14.11 — ≥10 subspecialty templates per module
requirements.evidence: templates/ contains only 5 JSON report templates total (chest-ct, brain-mri, abdomen-us, lumbar-spine-mri, knee-xray) plus deck-framework.html. That is ~0.3 templates per subspecialty, not ≥10 per module.
requirements.gap: Author ~10 templates for each of the 15 modules (≈150 templates). Essentially unbuilt.

requirements.title: §14.11 — Terminology bindings (RadLex sublexicon + RadElement CDEs)
requirements.evidence: RadLex sublexicon exists: rulebooks/_terminology/radlex_subset.yaml, served via RadLexService + /api/terminology/radlex/search and a FHIR CodeSystem export (TerminologyController). Rulebooks carry terminology_refs (e.g., liver_lirads_v1 references system: li_rads).
requirements.gap: No RadElement Common Data Element (CDE) bindings anywhere (grep for RadElement/CDE finds only docs/PRD, no code or data). RadLex subset is generic, not partitioned into per-subspecialty sublexicons.

requirements.title: §14.11 — Society guideline links (ACR/RSNA/ESR endorsements)
requirements.evidence: rads.yaml carries one publicGuidanceUrl per RADS system (ACR landing pages) surfaced through RadsService/TerminologyController. Rulebooks reference society systems in prose.
requirements.gap: No structured per-module society-guideline/evidence-link metadata, no ACR/RSNA/ESR endorsement registry, no per-rule evidence citation field. Only top-level ACR URLs for the 6 RADS systems.

requirements.title: §14.11 — ≥50 golden cases per module
requirements.evidence: rulebooks/_tests/<id>/ holds golden-case JSON fixtures, but only ~2-5 per rulebook (e.g., liver_lirads_v1 has 01-normal..04; thyroid_us_v1 has case-1/case-2). PROGRESS.md confirms '≥2 golden cases each'. ValidationPackService runs them via ReportValidator.
requirements.gap: Orders of magnitude short of ≥50 per module; need ~50 synthetic golden cases for each of 15 modules.

requirements.title: §14.11 — Subspecialty validation checks (e.g., LI-RADS category-derivation rules)
requirements.evidence: ReportValidator implements subspecialty-flavoured rule ids: lirads_category_required, lirads_observation_size_required, birads_assessment_in_impression, lungrads_category_required/mandatory, pirads_category_required/mandatory, tirads_category_mandatory, index_lesion_localized, figo_staging_when_oncologic, acl_pcl_status_documented, rotator_cuff_thickness_described, labrum_described, etc.
requirements.gap: Checks are text/regex presence checks (does the impression mention a category?), NOT derivation rules. The PRD's example 'LI-RADS category derivation rules' (compute category from imaging features) does not exist.

requirements.title: RADS-001 — Auto-derive RADS category from structured fields (BI-RADS 0-6, LI-RADS LR-1..LR-M, PI-RADS 1-5, Lung-RADS 1-4X, TI-RADS 1-5, O-RADS US/MRI, NI-RADS, CAD-RADS)
requirements.evidence: RadsService (Application/Services/RadsService.cs) is a passive YAML lookup adapter only (Lookup/ListSystems/GetSystem) — it never derives a category. The Report entity (Entities.cs) has no structured fields, only free-text sections. ReportValidator only checks a category is textually present. No derivation logic anywhere.
requirements.gap: No structured field model and no derivation engine. Also O-RADS, NI-RADS, CAD-RADS are entirely absent from rads.yaml (only bi/li/pi/lung/tirads/c_rads defined). Must add structured input fields per system and compute the category.

requirements.title: RADS-002 — Forbid manually-entered category that contradicts structured fields
requirements.evidence: No structured RADS field exists to contradict (Report has only narrative text). ReportValidator's conflict checks (CheckNegationConflict, laterality/spine-level conflict at lines ~258-344) compare text sections only, not a structured category vs a derived category.
requirements.gap: Depends on RADS-001 (structured fields + derivation). Then add a guard rule that blocks a manual category disagreeing with the derived one.

requirements.title: RADS-003 — Embed RADS explanation snippet (society-approved wording)
requirements.evidence: rads.yaml stores shortLabel per category (e.g., 'BI-RADS 4A: Low suspicion for malignancy') with a publicGuidanceUrl, served via /api/terminology/rads and the Terminology page (frontend/app/terminology/page.tsx).
requirements.gap: Snippets are short non-proprietary labels, not full society-approved explanation prose, and are not auto-embedded into the report at the point of category selection; the user must look them up manually in the terminology page.

requirements.title: RADS-004 — Calculate Fleischner Society follow-up recommendation for lung nodules
requirements.evidence: Grep for 'Fleischner' across backend returns no match; it appears only in the PRD file. lung_lungrads_v1 / lung_screening_ct_v1 rulebooks enforce a Lung-RADS category and nodule dimensions textually but contain no Fleischner follow-up computation.
requirements.gap: Build a Fleischner calculator (nodule size/type/solid-vs-subsolid + risk → follow-up interval) and surface its recommendation.

requirements.title: RADS-005 — RECIST 1.1 / iRECIST / mRECIST / Lugano / Cheson lesion tracker across studies
requirements.evidence: Grep for RECIST/iRECIST/mRECIST/Lugano/Cheson/Deauville/PERCIST across backend returns no match (PRD-only). No lesion entity, no cross-study tracker, no target/non-target lesion model.
requirements.gap: Entirely unbuilt: needs a longitudinal lesion-measurement model, per-criteria response computation, and cross-study comparison UI.

requirements.title: RADS-006 — Structured key images & key-finding placeholders (text-only MVP; image refs in beta)
requirements.evidence: Report entity has no key-image/key-finding fields; grep for KeyImage finds only PRD/docs. No placeholder model in templates (template JSON is section-based prose).
requirements.gap: Even the text-only MVP form (structured key-finding placeholders) is absent. Add a structured key-finding list to the report model and templates.

requirements.title: RADS-007 — Bidirectional sync of structured fields ↔ narrative text
requirements.evidence: There are no structured fields to sync; the report is narrative-only (Entities.cs Report). AI prompt_blocks do findings→impression rewriting but that is text→text, not structured↔narrative.
requirements.gap: Requires the structured-field model first, then two-way binding so editing a field updates the narrative and vice-versa.

requirements.title: RADS-008 — RADS analytics: distribution per radiologist, category churn draft→final, inter-reader variance
requirements.evidence: frontend/app/analytics/quality/page.tsx is a generic heuristic quality-score dashboard (avg score, blockers, by-radiologist, by-rulebook) driven by ValidationResult.QualityScore. It has no RADS category distribution, no draft→final category churn, no inter-reader variance.
requirements.gap: Build RADS-specific analytics: requires persisted RADS categories per report version (currently none) plus distribution/churn/variance computations and views.

requirements.title: RADS-009 — TIRADS, O-RADS-US/MRI scoring forms
requirements.evidence: No scoring forms exist; the Terminology page only lists static category codes and lets you pick a system to view labels. ReportValidator has a TI-RADS presence/follow-up check (text), but no interactive feature-points scoring form. O-RADS is listed in the frontend RADS_SYSTEMS dropdown but is NOT defined in rads.yaml, so /api/terminology/rads?system=O-RADS returns empty/404.
requirements.gap: Build interactive scoring forms (ACR TI-RADS feature points → TR category; O-RADS US/MRI inputs → category) and add O-RADS data.

requirements.title: RADS-010 — C-RADS scoring for CT colonography
requirements.evidence: rads.yaml defines the c_rads system with C0-C4 categories and an ACR guidance URL, queryable via /api/terminology/rads?system=c_rads.
requirements.gap: Only the category lookup exists. No C-RADS scoring form, no CT-colonography rulebook/template, no derivation of the C/E category from structured polyp/extracolonic inputs.

requirements.title: RADS-011 — NI-RADS for treated head & neck cancer
requirements.evidence: Grep for NI-RADS/ni_rads in rulebooks and backend returns no match; NI-RADS is not in rads.yaml (only bi/li/pi/lung/tirads/c_rads) and there is no head & neck rulebook.
requirements.gap: Add NI-RADS category data, a treated-H&N rulebook/template, and scoring; fully absent.

requirements.title: RADS-012 — 'Why this category?' justification panel
requirements.evidence: Grep for justification/why-category finds matches only in PRD/docs and unrelated 'justification' strings; no UI panel and no derivation rationale to display (since RADS-001 derivation does not exist).
requirements.gap: Depends on RADS-001/RADS-002; build a panel showing which structured features drove the derived category with society references.

requirements.title: Cross-cutting — Structured report data model (prerequisite for RADS-001/002/006/007/008/012)
requirements.evidence: Report entity (backend/.../Domain/Entities/Entities.cs, lines ~425-441) is purely narrative: Study context + Indication/Technique/Comparison/Findings/Impression/Recommendations strings. Rulebook output_schema declares a category enum (e.g., liver_lirads_v1 lirads_category) but it is not persisted as a structured DB field.
requirements.gap: Introduce a structured findings/RADS field model on Report (or a related entity) so categories, key findings, and measurements are first-class data — the foundation the entire §14.12 engine is built on.

requirements.title: Cross-cutting — DICOM SR export of structured/RADS content
requirements.evidence: Grep for DICOM SR / DicomSr returns no match in code. Existing structured output is FHIR DiagnosticReport ingest/export (BidiFhir), not DICOM SR.
requirements.gap: If DICOM SR is required for structured-reporting interop, it is unbuilt (note: image module is out of scope per PRD MVP, so this is lower priority).

summary: RadioPad implements the AI Provider Abstraction (§14.9) and MCP/Tool Integration Layer (§14.10) substantially on the locked stack (ASP.NET Core 8 + EF Core backend, Next.js frontend, .NET CLI). The provider-abstraction core is strong: a per-tenant ProviderConfig registry, IAiProviderAdapter interface with ~13 wired adapters, a single AiGateway choke-point that hard-fails PHI-policy violations before inference, encrypted env:/KMS API-key vaulting, AES-256-GCM OAuth refresh-token vault with rotation, per-provider cost/latency/token/availability telemetry (IAiUsageStore), a composite cost/quality/latency router, sandbox model comparison, retention labels, and a background model-drift watch. The MCP layer is also well-built: a tenant tool registry with admin Submit→Approve→Block lifecycle, least-privilege scopes, default-deny of shell:/fs:/net: scopes, per-call append-only audit with input/output hashes, allowlist connectors, Ed25519 signed-manifest verification with a deny-by-default capability registry, and a sandboxed test runner. Gaps that block 100%: PROV-004 (no explicit equal-or-higher compliance fallback chain — routing just filters eligibility), PROV-012 (no provider conformance test suite), PROV-013 (only a MONTHLY plan quota exists; the per-tenant per-DAY USD cost ceiling with hard cutoff + 80% warning is absent), PROV-014/015 (latency-budget and cheapest-compliant are weighted factors, not the specified hard-budget/mode switches), PROV-016 (no structured BAA/DPA/ZDR/training-on-data compliance matrix — only a single compliance class + free-text retention label), PROV-018 (per-modality default override not implemented; modality param is accepted but unused), PROV-019 (BYO-OAuth-app not surfaced), PROV-020 (no confidential-computing attestation). On MCP: MCP-006 sandbox is in-process only, NOT WASM/WASI (explicitly deferred in code), MCP-008 (no tool conformance suite), MCP-009 (no tool usage quotas), MCP-011 (built-in safe tools — PubMed/ACR/ICD-CPT/Fleischner/RECIST — are manifest-only declarations with no implemented connector binding classes), MCP-012 (no explicit prompt-injection-resistant prompts/output validation layer for tools).

requirements.title: PROV-001 Tenant-level model/provider registry
requirements.evidence: ProviderConfig entity (TenantId-scoped) in backend/RadioPad.Api/src/RadioPad.Domain/Entities/Entities.cs:167; CRUD via ProvidersController (api/providers) in Controllers/OtherControllers.cs:266; frontend admin UI frontend/app/providers/page.tsx; adapters resolved by AiGateway via IAiProviderAdapter registry.
requirements.gap: None material. Fully tenant-scoped registry with list/save/health endpoints.

requirements.title: PROV-002 Mark providers PHI-approved / de-identified-only / blocked
requirements.evidence: ProviderComplianceClass enum (Blocked/Sandbox/DeIdentifiedOnly/PhiApproved/LocalOnly) in Domain/Enums/Enums.cs:8; persisted on ProviderConfig.Compliance; editable in providers/page.tsx compliance selector.
requirements.gap: None. All three classes (plus Sandbox/LocalOnly) present and editable.

requirements.title: PROV-003 Per-provider cost / latency / token / availability telemetry
requirements.evidence: IAiUsageStore.RecordAsync writes one AiRequest per gateway call with InputTokens/OutputTokens/LatencyMs/Status (AiGateway.cs:145); UsageSummary.ByProvider rollup (Interfaces.cs:245) with cost pricing; EfProviderRouter computes per-provider P95-24h latency from the ledger (Repositories.cs:244); availability via POST /api/providers/{id}/health + Iter35AvailabilityMonitor.
requirements.gap: None material; cost/latency/token/availability all captured per provider.

requirements.title: PROV-004 Fallback routing only between providers of equal-or-higher compliance class
requirements.evidence: EfProviderRouter.ScoreAsync (Repositories.cs:255-266) filters eligibility by compliance (Blocked excluded; PHI requires PhiApproved/LocalOnly; tenant-requires-PHI-grade applies a floor for non-PHI). AiGateway.EnforcePhiPolicy also blocks downgrade at call time.
requirements.gap: There is no explicit multi-provider fallback CHAIN that, on a primary failure, re-routes only to an equal-or-higher compliance-class provider. AiGateway invokes a single selected adapter and throws on failure (no automatic failover). Eligibility filtering exists, but the directed 'fallback only to >= compliance' behavior on transport failure is not implemented.

requirements.title: PROV-005 Model comparison in sandbox evaluation mode
requirements.evidence: SandboxCompareController (api/ai/sandbox/compare) runs same prompt across up to 4 Sandbox-class providers, still through AiGateway/EnforcePhiPolicy (Controllers/SandboxCompareController.cs); SandboxComparePanel UI in frontend/app/providers/page.tsx:355.
requirements.gap: None material. Side-by-side compare with per-run latency/tokens shipped.

requirements.title: PROV-006 API key vaulting and rotation
requirements.evidence: ApiKeySecretRef must be env:<NAME> (inline secrets rejected, OtherControllers.cs:306); ProviderSecretResolver (env:/aws:/azkv:/gcp: schemes) in Infrastructure/Providers/ProviderSecretResolver.cs; KMS-backed at-rest column encryption (AesGcmColumnEncryptor + IKmsProvider chain in Program.cs:230-234). Secrets never returned to clients.
requirements.gap: Key rotation for static API keys is operator-driven via secret store; no automated rotation scheduler for API keys (OAuth refresh tokens do have automated rotation). Minor.

requirements.title: PROV-007 OAuth token storage only in encrypted local/tenant vaults
