requirements.evidence: OAuthRefreshVault (AES-256-GCM per-token DEK wrapped by tenant KMS KEK) in Application/Services/OAuthRefreshVault.cs; ciphertext columns on ProviderConfig (Entities.cs:205-220); never returns token bytes; audit OAuthRefreshRotated=41; rotation policy + ShouldRotate background logic; Iter35OAuthVaultTests (8/8).
requirements.gap: None material; only operator-side real-IdP wiring (IOAuthTokenIssuer is Noop by default) remains, which is out of scope of the locked-stack code.

requirements.title: PROV-008 Provider policy enforcement before inference (hard fail on violation)
requirements.evidence: AiGateway.RouteAsync calls EnforcePhiPolicy BEFORE adapter dispatch and throws ProviderPolicyException on disabled/Blocked/PHI-to-non-approved (AiGateway.cs:48,218-242); ProviderBlocked audit + usage row written; AiGatewayPolicyTests / AiPolicyHttpTests.
requirements.gap: None. Hard fail-closed before inference is the single choke-point.

requirements.title: PROV-009 Data retention labeling per provider and endpoint
requirements.evidence: ProviderConfig.RetentionLabel free-text field (Entities.cs:203), surfaced in list/save API and providers/page.tsx retention-label input; migration 20260504110000_Iter34ProviderRetention; Iter34ProviderRetentionTests.
requirements.gap: Label is a single free-text string per provider, not a structured per-endpoint retention record, and it is informational only (never enforced). PRD says 'per provider AND endpoint'; per-endpoint granularity and any policy effect are absent.

requirements.title: PROV-010 Block PHI to providers without approved compliance configuration
requirements.evidence: EnforcePhiPolicy: when ContainsPhi, only PhiApproved or LocalOnly allowed, else ProviderPolicyException (AiGateway.cs:226-232); router excludes phi_not_allowed candidates (Repositories.cs:262).
requirements.gap: None. Core safety boundary, fully enforced and tested.

requirements.title: PROV-011 First-class provider adapters (OpenAI, Azure, Anthropic, Vertex, Bedrock, Cohere, Mistral, Groq, Together, Fireworks, Replicate + local OSS llama.cpp/Ollama/vLLM/LocalAI/TGI/Tabby)
requirements.evidence: Wired adapters (Program.cs:87-112): mock, anthropic, ollama (legacy), azure-openai, aws-bedrock, google-vertex, openai (direct), openai-compatible, github-copilot-sdk, ollama-chat, vllm, llama-cpp, gemini-cli/codex-cli, ubag. Catalog (docs/03-architecture/provider-catalog.md) states openai-compatible covers OpenRouter/Groq/Together/Mistral/NVIDIA NIM/vLLM.
requirements.gap: Dedicated first-class adapters for Cohere, Mistral La Plateforme, Groq, Together, Fireworks, Replicate are NOT individually implemented (only reachable generically via openai-compatible). Local OSS LocalAI, TGI, and Tabby have no dedicated adapter (also only via openai-compatible). Generic reachability partially satisfies the function but not the 'first-class adapter' clause per named provider.

requirements.title: PROV-012 Provider conformance test suite
requirements.evidence: No conformance harness in code; grep for 'conformance' matches only PRD + docs (model-abstraction.md, ai-quality-rubric.md), no executable suite. Per-adapter unit tests exist (e.g. OpenAiDirectProviderTests) but are not a reusable certify-any-provider conformance suite.
requirements.gap: Build a runnable, provider-agnostic conformance test suite (golden prompt/response contract checks) any new adapter can be run against to certify RadioPad compatibility.

requirements.title: PROV-013 Cost ceiling per tenant per day with hard cutoff; warning at 80%
requirements.evidence: Only a MONTHLY plan quota exists: PlanQuotaService.CheckAsync compares month-to-date AiCalls/InputTokens/OutputTokens against PlanLimits and throws QuotaExceededException (Application/Services/PlanQuotaService.cs). ProviderConfig.MaxCostPerCallUsd is a per-call cap, not a per-tenant per-day USD ceiling. No 80% warning logic anywhere.
requirements.gap: Implement a per-tenant per-DAY USD spend ceiling that hard-cuts at 100% and warns at 80%. Current quota is monthly call/token-based, not a daily dollar ceiling, and has no warning threshold.

requirements.title: PROV-014 Latency budget routing (pick fastest compliant provider under N ms)
requirements.evidence: EfProviderRouter uses P95-24h latency as a weighted LatencyScore in the composite (Repositories.cs:281-297); RoutingWeights tunable per tenant; tie-break prefers lower P95.
requirements.gap: There is no hard latency-BUDGET filter (a configurable N-ms ceiling that excludes slower providers and then picks the fastest compliant one). Latency only influences a soft weighted score, not a budget cutoff mode.

requirements.title: PROV-015 'Cheapest-compliant' routing mode for non-clinical drafts
requirements.evidence: CostScore is a weighted factor in the composite router and operators can set RoutingWeightsJson to weight cost highly (defaults cost 0.5). EfProviderRouter.SelectAsync picks highest composite among eligible.
requirements.gap: No explicit selectable 'cheapest-compliant' MODE (a switch that, for non-clinical drafts, ignores quality/latency and deterministically picks the lowest-cost compliant provider). Achievable only by manually re-weighting; not a first-class mode bound to non-clinical drafts.

requirements.title: PROV-016 Provider compliance matrix view for admins (BAA, DPA, ZDR endpoints, training-on-data)
requirements.evidence: Providers list/governance dashboard (frontend/app/admin/governance/page.tsx model-inventory panel) shows compliance class + retention label per provider; providers/page.tsx shows compliance + retention.
requirements.gap: No structured compliance-matrix data model or view with discrete BAA-status, DPA-status, ZDR-endpoint, and training-on-data columns. These are collapsed into one ProviderComplianceClass enum plus a free-text retention label — admins cannot see the four distinct attributes the PRD names.

requirements.title: PROV-017 Provider drift watch — daily probe with golden cases
requirements.evidence: ModelDriftDetectionService BackgroundService runs golden-case regression against sandbox providers on an interval (default 6h, configurable), raises SystemAlert on quality-delta breach (Api/Services/ModelDriftDetectionService.cs); DriftController GET /api/admin/drift/status + manual trigger; DriftBaselines migration; DriftDetectionTests.
requirements.gap: Interval defaults to 6h not strictly 'daily' (configurable, so acceptable). Tests sandbox-class providers only to avoid cost; function meets intent.

requirements.title: PROV-018 Per-modality default provider override
requirements.evidence: IRoutingPreviewService.PreviewAsync accepts a 'modality' parameter but EfProviderRouter never uses it for selection (Repositories.cs:387-393 ignore modality). No ModalityProviderOverride entity/field anywhere (grep found no matches outside PRD).
requirements.gap: Implement a per-modality default/override mapping (e.g. mammo→on-prem Llama only) consulted by the router. The plumbing accepts modality but it has zero effect on routing today.

requirements.title: PROV-019 Bring-your-own-OAuth-app for subscription-backed providers (non-PHI by default)
requirements.evidence: CopilotIntegrationSettings supports BYO accounts / OAuth client id + secret ref + AllowByoAccounts (Entities.cs:229-258); IOAuthTokenIssuer + OAuthRefreshVault provide the storage/rotation substrate; CopilotMode.BringYourOwnAccount exists.
requirements.gap: BYO-OAuth-app is only realized for the GitHub Copilot integration, not a general 'bring-your-own-OAuth-app' for arbitrary subscription-backed AI providers. No generic provider-level BYO-OAuth-app registration UI/endpoint with the non-PHI-by-default guard; concrete IOAuthTokenIssuer is Noop.

requirements.title: PROV-020 Confidential-computing inference attestation surfaced to admins
requirements.evidence: No attestation code for inference enclaves; 'attestation' matches only WebAuthn FIDO attestation (AttestationParser.cs) and TLS, unrelated to confidential-computing inference. No admin surface for enclave/CC attestation.
requirements.gap: Implement capture and admin display of confidential-computing (TEE/enclave) attestation evidence per provider/inference. Entirely absent.

requirements.title: MCP-001 Tool registry for approved internal tools (MCP-style)
requirements.evidence: McpToolRegistryController (api/mcp/tools) with McpTool entity, list/get/register/approve/block/delete; persisted via McpRegistry migration; admin UI frontend/app/admin/mcp/page.tsx; connector manifests in mcp-connectors/*.json.
requirements.gap: None material; full registry lifecycle present.

requirements.title: MCP-002 Each tool requires explicit admin approval per tenant
requirements.evidence: Tools register in Status=Submitted/Approved=false; only MedicalDirector/ItAdmin can Approve (McpToolRegistryController.cs:161-183); Invoke rejects any non-Approved tool with 403 mcp_blocked (line 289); audit McpToolApproved=26. Tenant-scoped throughout.
requirements.gap: None. Default-deny until per-tenant admin approval.

requirements.title: MCP-003 Least-privilege tool scopes (read-only, write to specific endpoints, etc.)
requirements.evidence: McpToolScope enum (ReadOnly/ReadWrite/External) + free-text ScopeString tokens; IMcpScopePolicy.Evaluate gates dangerous prefixes; AllowedConnectorPaths per tool; IMcpCapabilityRegistry deny-by-default (pluginId,capability) tuples (IMcpCapabilityRegistry.cs).
requirements.gap: None material; scope + capability + connector-path least-privilege all present.

requirements.title: MCP-004 Every tool call logged with user, study/patient context, tool, input hash, output hash, timestamp
requirements.evidence: McpInvocationService.RunAsync writes one McpToolCall (TenantId, UserId, ToolId, ReportId, ToolName, ScopeString, InputHash, OutputHash, LatencyMs, Status) + append-only AuditEvent McpToolCalled=28; bodies SHA-256 hashed, never stored raw (Api/Services/McpInvocationService.cs).
requirements.gap: None. Exhaustive append-only per-call ledger with hashes.

requirements.title: MCP-005 Shell/file/network tools blocked by default in clinical production
requirements.evidence: McpScopePolicy DangerousPrefixes = shell:/fs:/net: default-deny; only allowed when BOTH RADIOPAD_MCP_ALLOW_DANGEROUS=1 env AND tenant AllowDangerousMcp (Application/Services/Mcp/McpScopePolicy.cs); enforced in Invoke + Test paths; UI flags 'powerful access' in red.
requirements.gap: None. Compile-time fixed dangerous set, double-gated override.

requirements.title: MCP-006 Sandboxed execution for tool tests (WASM with WASI capability allowlist)
requirements.evidence: InProcessMcpSandbox + SandboxRunner enforce 5s wall-clock timeout, soft 256MiB memory cap, BelowNormal priority, and connector-allowlist regex gate (Application/Services/Mcp/InProcessMcpSandbox.cs; SandboxRunner in McpToolRegistryController.cs:404). Per-OS IPluginSandbox (Windows AppContainer / Linux unshare / macOS noop) for desktop plugins.
requirements.gap: Execution is IN-PROCESS, NOT WASM/WASI with a WASI capability allowlist — explicitly documented as deferred ('no hard OS-level isolation here — that's the next iteration's job, likely WASM via Wasmtime'). No memory-safe WASM isolation or WASI capability model for tool tests.

requirements.title: MCP-007 Allowlist-based connectors for PACS/RIS/EHR (no wildcard egress)
requirements.evidence: AllowedConnectorPaths per tool + InProcessMcpSandbox.CheckConnector regex-allowlist gate on outbound calls; connector manifests mcp-connectors/dicomweb-qido.json, fhir-servicerequest.json, pacs-recent-studies.json scoped to net:dicomweb etc.; IMcpCapabilityRegistry deny-by-default.
requirements.gap: Allowlist mechanism is solid (no wildcard egress). Note connector RUNTIME bindings are not implemented (see MCP-011), but the allowlist gating itself for PACS/RIS/EHR connectors is in place.

requirements.title: MCP-008 Tool conformance test suite (third parties can certify)
requirements.evidence: No tool conformance harness; PROGRESS.md and traceability list manifest signing/scope tests but no reusable certify-any-tool suite. Iter31McpTests/Iter32McpRegistryTests cover registry behavior, not a third-party conformance contract.
requirements.gap: Provide a published tool conformance suite (manifest schema + scope + sandbox + I/O contract checks) third parties run to certify a tool.

requirements.title: MCP-009 Tool usage quotas (per user, per tenant)
requirements.evidence: No per-user/per-tenant MCP tool quota logic; grep for McpQuota/ToolQuota/UsageQuota found no matches. McpToolCall ledger records calls but nothing enforces a cap; PlanQuotaService governs AI calls only, not tool calls.
requirements.gap: Implement per-user and per-tenant tool-invocation quotas with enforcement at McpInvocationService/Invoke, mirroring the AI plan-quota gate.

requirements.title: MCP-010 Signed tool capability registration; daemon refuses unsigned tools in production tenants
requirements.evidence: McpManifestVerifier verifies Ed25519 detached signature over manifest bytes vs RADIOPAD_MCP_RELEASE_PUBKEY_B64 (Application/Services/Mcp/McpManifestVerifier.cs); PluginManifestSignatureVerifier + TrustedPluginPublisher table + migration 20260504100000_TrustedPluginPublishers; ManifestSig stored, ManifestSigned surfaced as 'trusted' badge; desktop PLUGIN_TRUST.md; PluginManifestSignatureTests.
requirements.gap: Signature verification and trusted-publisher model are implemented; production-tenant 'refuse unsigned' is enforced for desktop plugins via capability registry/verifier. Server-side McpToolRegistryController.Register currently accepts an empty signature (stores unsigned, Submitted) — hard refusal of unsigned tools specifically for production tenants at the registry boundary is not strictly enforced, only flagged. Minor hardening gap.

requirements.title: MCP-011 Built-in safe tools: PubMed, ACR Appropriateness, ICD/CPT, Fleischner/Lung-RADS, RECIST helper
requirements.evidence: Connector manifests reference binding classes (e.g. RadioPad.Application.Services.Mcp.Connectors.DicomWebQido) but NO such Connectors namespace classes exist in backend source (no files under Services/Mcp/Connectors). Fleischner/Lung-RADS/RECIST appear only in rulebooks/validation (rulebooks/*.yaml, ReportValidator), not as invokable MCP tools. No PubMed/ACR/ICD/CPT tool implementations found.
requirements.gap: Implement the named built-in safe MCP tools as runnable connector binding classes (PubMed search, ACR Appropriateness lookup, ICD/CPT lookup, Fleischner/Lung-RADS calculator, RECIST helper). Today only DICOMweb/FHIR/PACS connectors are declared as manifests with no executable binding, and the clinical calculators are not exposed as tools.

requirements.title: MCP-012 Prompt-injection-resistant tool prompts and output validation
requirements.evidence: No tool-prompt hardening or output-validation layer for MCP tools; grep for PromptInjection/injection-resist found no matches. Tool outputs are hashed/audited (McpInvocationService) but not validated/sanitized against injection; tool prompts have no documented anti-injection structure.
requirements.gap: Add prompt-injection-resistant tool prompt templates and structured output validation/sanitization for tool results before they re-enter the model/report context. Currently absent for the MCP tool path.

summary: RadioPad has a genuinely functional observability *metrics* spine and a SLO/burn-rate stack on the locked .NET stack, plus a real synthetic availability probe and extensive operational documentation — but tracing, continuous profiling, a status page, and most DR/HA machinery are absent or doc-only. Concrete code: OpenTelemetry metrics (PerfBudgets.cs — 5 histograms; OTLP exporter behind RADIOPAD_OTEL_OTLP_ENDPOINT in Program.cs ~L334-354), per-route PerfBudgetMiddleware, AvailabilityMonitorService (synthetic health probe + burn-rate audit), ObservabilityController (Alertmanager webhook + availability snapshot, RBAC-gated), PhiRedactingLoggerProvider (log PHI redaction), RequestCorrelationMiddleware (X-RadioPad-RequestId), deploy/observability/{slo-recording-rules.yaml,grafana-radiopad-slo.json} (multi-window 2x/5x burn-rate rules + dashboard), perf/k6 scripts + .github/workflows/perf-smoke.yml. Strong doc layer: slo-sla.md, disaster-recovery.md, business-continuity.md, incident-response.md, postmortem-template.md, status-page-policy.md, backup-restore.md, capacity-planning.md, runbook.md, observability.md. KEY GAPS vs PRD v2: (1) NO distributed tracing at all — only request-id correlation; no ActivitySource/spans, no W3C trace context propagation browser→gateway→service→daemon, no radiopad.* span namespace, no high-cardinality attribute hashing (§26.3 missing). (2) NO continuous profiling (§26.4 missing). (3) NO status page implementation, policy doc only (§26.7 missing). (4) SLO instrument set covers validate/sign/AI-draft/QIDO + per-route HTTP but does NOT match the PRD §25.1 surface table (no AI-gateway routing≤80ms, FHIR≤500ms, daemon RPC≤20ms/99.99%, critical-result E2E≤2s, audit P99≤500ms SLO instruments). (5) Capacity targets, scalability strategy (Postgres sharding, NATS JetStream, OpenSearch ILM, PgBouncer, edge caching), and DR architecture (active-active, logical+physical replication, pgBackRest PITR, CRR, DNS failover, restore drills) are documentation/Phase-3 only — actual deploy runs SQLite or single-region Postgres+Caddy. (6) Synthetic monitoring probes only health endpoints, not the golden paths (login/open-study/generate/export), critical-result delivery, or DICOMweb/FHIR per-integration probes. (7) Runbooks are general, not per-service runbooks/<service>.md, and no chaos drills exist. NOTE on stack-swap rule: items judged on FUNCTION not OSS-library identity — e.g. .NET OTel SDK satisfies "OpenTelemetry"; absence of Loki/NATS/Pyroscope only counts where the *function* (tracing, profiling, tiered log storage, event replay) is genuinely absent, which it is for several clauses.

requirements.title: §25.1 SLOs and error budgets (per-surface availability + latency SLOs, 2x/5x multi-window burn-rate alerts)
requirements.evidence: Real burn-rate machinery: deploy/observability/slo-recording-rules.yaml computes P50/P95/P99 recording rules over 5m/30m/1h and multi-window multi-burn-rate budget-burn alerts (RadioPadValidate/Sign/AiDraft/DicomQido P95BudgetBurn) with fast/slow window pairs; deploy/observability/grafana-radiopad-slo.json renders burn-rate panels. Backing histograms in backend/.../Services/PerfBudgets.cs; ObservabilityController exposes RBAC-gated POST /api/admin/observability/slo-alerts (PerfBudgetTests asserts it appends a SystemAlert audit row). docs/07-devops/slo-sla.md documents error budgets + 50%/week change-hold.
requirements.gap: Instrumented SLOs (validate 250ms, sign 500ms, AI draft 4s, QIDO 600ms) do NOT match the PRD §25.1 surface table: missing dedicated SLO instruments/budgets for AI-gateway routing P95≤80ms, FHIR/HL7 endpoints P95≤500ms, Daemon API local 99.99% daily / RPC P95≤20ms, critical-result notifications E2E≤2s (7d), and audit writes P99≤500ms (1d). Availability SLOs (99.9%/99.99%) are documented but not computed as error-budget series. Burn alert thresholds (2%/5% of budget) approximate but are not the PRD's 2x/5x burn-rate multipliers against per-surface budgets. slo-sla.md targets (99.5%, read<300ms) also diverge from PRD numbers.

requirements.title: §25.2 Capacity targets (≥250k reports/mo/node, ≥200 concurrent reporters, ≥10k audit/s/shard, ≥500 AI req/s/pod, ≥8 daemon concurrent, ≤1MB draft)
requirements.evidence: docs/07-devops/capacity-planning.md gives per-tenant sizing (small/medium/large), API replica/Postgres sizing, and scaling signals (CPU>70%, pool>70%, audit append P95>200ms). docs/00-product/nfr.md gives 50 concurrent radiologists / 100 tenants × 50 baselines. perf/k6 + perf-smoke.yml exercise throughput at low VUs.
requirements.gap: None of the specific PRD capacity numbers (250k/mo, 200 concurrent, 10k audit/s/shard, 500 AI req/s/pod, 8 daemon concurrent, ≤1MB CRDT draft) are codified, load-tested, or enforced; capacity-planning.md uses different, lower, draft figures and the k6 smoke runs at 3-10 VUs. No CRDT draft-size guard. No per-shard audit throughput test.

requirements.title: §25.3 Scalability strategy (stateless horizontal scaling, Postgres tenant sharding, logical read replicas, NATS JetStream clustering, OpenSearch ILM, edge caching, PgBouncer)
requirements.evidence: docs/07-devops/infra-architecture.md documents stateless autoscaled API tier, managed Postgres + replicas, egress allowlist, single-region→multi-region Phase-3 plan; capacity-planning.md notes tenant sharding as Phase 3. Backend is stateless ASP.NET Core; deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml uses postgres:16-alpine + Caddy.
requirements.gap: Functionally most strategy is doc/Phase-3 only. No Postgres sharding, no logical replica routing, no event-bus clustering (NATS JetStream function = an event stream with replication/replay; not present — events are in-DB), no OpenSearch/audit hot-warm-cold ILM, no PgBouncer/transaction-mode pooling, no edge/Varnish caching layer wired. Production VPS even runs SQLite (deploy/vps + PROGRESS Iter42). Horizontal-stateless is the only clause materially satisfied.

requirements.title: §25.4 Resource budgets / stretch (Studio idle RAM ≤90MB, daemon idle ≤22MB, CLI startup ≤25ms, cold local 7B Q4 gen <3.5s P95)
requirements.evidence: docs/06-testing/performance-test-plan.md defines a desktop measurement protocol (cold/warm start, idle CPU/memory, sidecar readiness, bundle size) and mandates pre-release desktop measurement; desktop sidecar health check is deliberately low-frequency (5s) to stay near-idle (desktop-runbook.md).
requirements.gap: The specific stretch thresholds (90MB / 22MB / 25ms / 3.5s P95) are not measured, asserted, or gated anywhere; protocol records 'baseline, no regression >20%' rather than the absolute v2.0-LTS targets. No automated CLI-startup or local-model-latency benchmark in CI.

requirements.title: §26.1 Instrumentation — OpenTelemetry end-to-end (traces+metrics+logs), per-tenant + per-modality Grafana dashboards, SLO dashboards with burn alerts
requirements.evidence: OpenTelemetry metrics are real: Program.cs registers AddOpenTelemetry().WithMetrics(AddMeter(PerfBudgets.MeterName)) with OTLP HTTP exporter gated on RADIOPAD_OTEL_OTLP_ENDPOINT; histograms tagged route/tenant/status. SLO dashboard (grafana-radiopad-slo.json) with error-budget burn panels + fast/slow burn alerts exists. docs/06-operations/observability.md documents the meter + alert webhook.
requirements.gap: Only the metrics signal is wired; no traces and (by §26.2) no JSON/OTel log pipeline. Dashboards are a single global SLO board — no per-tenant templated-variable dashboards and no per-modality clinical dashboards. 'End-to-end (traces, metrics, logs)' and browser→gateway→service→daemon→provider correlation are not met.

requirements.title: §26.2 Logs — structured JSON only, PHI redaction layer in logger, CI PHI-template scan, sampling + tiered Loki storage
requirements.evidence: PHI redaction layer is implemented in code: Program.cs (~L395-419) wraps every ILoggerProvider with RadioPad.Api.Logging.PhiRedactingLoggerProvider so PHI-shaped substrings are scrubbed before reaching console/file/SIEM; docs/03-architecture/logging.md documents redaction rules + per-request fields (no PHI/secrets/full sections). SIEM push sink exists (SiemPushService, SyslogUdpSink; SiemSinkTests).
requirements.gap: Logging uses Microsoft.Extensions.Logging AddSimpleConsole (single-line text), not structured-JSON-only — logging.md and monitoring.md both list Serilog/JSON as Phase 2. No CI scan that rejects strings resembling PHI templates. No log sampling for high-volume info, no warning+ full-retention policy enforcement, no Loki/tiered hot-warm-cold storage (Phase 3 in docs).

requirements.title: §26.3 Tracing — W3C Trace Context everywhere, OTel semantic conventions, radiopad.* clinical span namespace, hash high-cardinality (study_id/tenant_id) trace attributes
requirements.evidence: Only request-id correlation exists: RequestCorrelationMiddleware sets X-RadioPad-RequestId (falls back to Activity.Current?.Id or a GUID) and adds it to the log scope. No tracing pipeline. Grep for ActivitySource/StartActivity/AddSource/WithTracing/AddAspNetCoreInstrumentation returned NO matches in source.
requirements.gap: No distributed tracing at all: no ActivitySource/spans, no OTel WithTracing()/exporter, no W3C traceparent propagation across browser→gateway→service→daemon→provider, no radiopad.* span attribute namespace, no OTel semantic-convention attributes, and consequently no high-cardinality (study_id/tenant_id) trace-attribute hashing. This is the single largest §26 gap.

requirements.title: §26.4 Continuous profiling — CPU+heap from all services and daemon (Pyroscope/Phlare), profiles in post-incident runbook
requirements.evidence: Only docs/06-testing/performance-test-plan.md mentions dotnet-counters/dotnet-trace as planned ad-hoc tooling. No continuous-profiling agent, no Pyroscope/Phlare config, no profiling export wired in Program.cs or any service/daemon.
requirements.gap: No continuous profiling capability of any kind (function absent, not just the named OSS tool). Profiles are not part of any runbook step.

requirements.title: §26.5 Runbooks — per-service runbooks/<service>.md with on-call playbook, dashboards, alerts, escalation matrix, dependency map; tested in chaos drills
requirements.evidence: Operational runbooks exist with detection→containment→diagnosis→resolution→comms structure: docs/07-devops/runbook.md (DB outage, audit-chain mismatch, provider outage, PHI leak, login down), docs/04-security/siem-runbook.md, docs/06-operations/desktop-runbook.md. Incident roles + escalation in incident-response.md and business-continuity.md.
requirements.gap: Runbooks are organized by failure mode, not as the PRD-mandated per-service runbooks/<service>.md committed at repo root (there is NO top-level runbooks/ directory). No explicit per-runbook dashboards/escalation-matrix/dependency-map sections for each service. No chaos drills (grep chaos/gameday/toxiproxy = PRD only) testing the runbooks.

requirements.title: §26.6 Synthetic monitoring — multi-region golden-path probes (login/open study/generate impression/export), critical-result delivery probe, DICOMweb/FHIR per-integration probes
requirements.evidence: AvailabilityMonitorService (backend/.../Services/AvailabilityMonitorService.cs) is a real BackgroundService that probes configurable relative paths (default /api/health/ready) on a cadence, maintains a 5-min rolling failure window, emits radiopad.availability.probe.* histogram+counter, and appends a SystemAlert burn-rate audit row when the threshold is exceeded; snapshot exposed via GET /api/admin/observability/availability (RBAC-gated, rendered on /admin/security). Iter35AvailabilityMonitorTests cover it.
requirements.gap: By design the monitor probes ONLY platform health endpoints (PHI-policy forbids report routes), so it does NOT execute the golden user paths (login, open study, generate impression, export), the critical-result delivery E2E probe, or per-integration DICOMweb/FHIR probes. Single local instance — not multi-region. So it is a health-availability probe, not the PRD's golden-path synthetic suite.

requirements.title: §26.7 Status page — public per-component status + planned-maintenance schedule (Cachet/Statuspage OSS equivalent)
requirements.evidence: docs/08-user-docs/status-page-policy.md defines components, states (Operational/Degraded/Partial/Major/Maintenance), SEV update cadence, content rules, subscriptions, internal mirror.
requirements.gap: Implementation is entirely absent — only a policy document. No status-page service/component (no Cachet/Statuspage deploy, no public per-component page, no maintenance-schedule surface). status-page-policy.md itself marks it 'launching with hosted SKU'.

requirements.title: §27.1 RTO/RPO targets (per class: drafting RTO 0/RPO 0 local WAL; control plane 15min/1min; audit RPO 1min; FHIR 30min/5min; marketplace 2h/15min; FL 24h/1h)
requirements.evidence: Drafting RTO≈0 is genuinely realized: Tauri offline_drafts.rs persists AES-256-GCM encrypted drafts with an append-only audit log so Studio continues offline (DESK-006, desktop-runbook.md). docs/04-security/disaster-recovery.md states RTO/RPO targets.
requirements.gap: disaster-recovery.md gives a single coarse target (hosted RTO 4h / RPO 15min) that does NOT match the PRD per-class table (control plane 15min/1min, audit 1min, FHIR 30min/5min, marketplace 2h/15min, FL 24h/1h). No per-class measurement or enforcement. Local-WAL RPO 0 for drafting is approximated by the encrypted local store, not a true WAL.

requirements.title: §27.2 DR architecture (active-active EE+ / active-warm Pro, logical+physical replication, pgBackRest PITR 15min, NATS mirror streams, object-storage CRR, DNS health-checked failover, quarterly restore drills)
requirements.evidence: docs/04-security/disaster-recovery.md (continuous WAL archiving + daily base backup, restore steps, quarterly DR test) and docs/07-devops/backup-restore.md (PITR procedure, audit-verify after restore) and infra-architecture.md (single-region→multi-region Phase-3, DNS failover). business-continuity.md adds cold-spare template + tabletop drills.
requirements.gap: Almost entirely doc/Phase-3: no active-active or warm-standby deployment, no implemented logical+physical replication, no pgBackRest/PITR automation, no event-bus mirror streams, no object-storage CRR, no DNS health-checked failover. Actual production runs single-node SQLite/Postgres+Caddy (PROGRESS Iter42, deploy/vps). Restore drills are documented cadence, not evidenced as executed.

requirements.title: §27.3 Degradation modes (tested, not aspirational): cloud-unreachable, all-AI-down→local, daemon-down banner, Postgres failover, NATS partition replay, OIDC-down cached sessions, DICOMweb-down manual entry
requirements.evidence: Several modes are real on the locked stack: daemon-down → Tauri sidecar_manager.rs emits radiopad://backend-status (starting/ready/degraded/restarting/failed) surfaced as UI banners (desktop-runbook.md); cloud-unreachable → encrypted offline drafts continue (offline_drafts.rs). OIDC-down: docs (business-continuity.md, runbook 'Login down') state existing sessions retained / no forced re-auth — and tokens are signed/expiring (Iter47). DICOMweb-down manual metadata entry path exists. Provider-down behavior is implemented as explicit 502 + user switch (no silent fallback) per business-continuity.md.
requirements.gap: Not 'tested, not aspirational': no automated chaos/fault-injection tests prove these modes. 'All cloud AI providers down → daemon routes to local model with failover notice' is explicitly NOT implemented as auto-failover (design is no silent fallback; user must switch) — diverges from PRD wording. Postgres-primary-loss auto-failover + CRDT/WAL replay and NATS-partition buffer/replay are N/A to the current single-node/no-event-bus deployment (architectural, not built).

requirements.title: §27.4 Backups (daily full + WAL archiving, tested PITR, cross-region replication, tenant-scoped key encryption, weekly random-restore integrity check)
requirements.evidence: docs/07-devops/backup-restore.md specifies daily base + continuous WAL, 35-day retention, encryption at rest, PITR restore procedure, quarterly DR drill + nightly backup-validation (planned); tenant-scoped key encryption is real in code (Infrastructure/Kms/TenantDekCache.cs, column encryption for secrets per Iter49). 'radiopad audit verify' validates audit chain after restore.
requirements.gap: Backup automation itself is not implemented in-repo (it relies on managed-DB features / planned jobs): no scheduled full+WAL job, no cross-region replication wired, no weekly random-restore integrity-check job (marked 'planned'), tenant export CLI is Phase 3. So encryption-with-tenant-keys is the only clause materially built; the rest is documented architecture.

requirements.title: §27.5 Incident response (Sev1–Sev4 with response times; Sev1 ack ≤5min, comms ≤15min; postmortems Sev1/Sev2 within 72h, external for tenant-affecting)
requirements.evidence: docs/04-security/incident-response.md defines SEV-1/2/3 with definitions, roles (IC/Ops/Comms/Scribe), detection/containment/eradication/recovery/comms and postmortem timelines; docs/07-devops/postmortem-template.md is a complete blameless template; status-page-policy.md sets SEV-1 first-update ≤15min / 30-min cadence. business-continuity.md adds on-call rotation + drills.
requirements.gap: Process is documented (not code), but specifics diverge from PRD: only SEV-1..3 defined (no SEV-4); SEV-1 ack documented as 30 min (PRD requires ≤5 min) and comms ≤15 min only via the status-page policy; postmortem deadline is 5 business days (PRD: internal within 72h for Sev1/Sev2). No tooling integration (paging/on-call) — 'Prometheus + on-call paging' is marked planned.
