area: PRD v2 §14.13 Peer Review & Quality, §14.14 Teaching File & Education, §14.15 Critical Results Workflow
summary: Across all 28 requirements in these three modules, only the Critical Results "detector" function has any real code: a rule-based regex detector (CheckCriticalLanguage in backend/RadioPad.Api/src/RadioPad.Validation/Engine/ReportValidator.cs) wired to `critical_result_language` / `critical_finding_language` rules in 12 rulebooks. That detector flags a critical finding that lacks communication/escalation phrasing, giving PARTIAL coverage of CR-001 (rule-only, no AI ensemble) and CR-005 (flags-but-does-not-insert). Everything else in §14.15 (criticality classes/SLAs, communication-tracking record, auto-page/SMS, audit-grade critical-result events, WebAuthn ack, read-back script, escalation, Joint-Commission reports) is MISSING. The entire Peer Review module (§14.13, PR-001..010) and the entire Teaching File module (§14.14, TF-001..008) are MISSING: there are no domain entities (Enums.cs/Entities.cs contain none), no RBAC permissions (PermissionCatalog.cs), no AuditAction kinds, no controllers, and no frontend pages for any of them. Two adjacent-but-distinct assets exist and were verified NOT to satisfy the requirements: frontend/app/analytics/quality/page.tsx is a validation-quality-score dashboard (heuristic QualityScore trends), not a RADPEER concordance/discrepancy dashboard; and PhiRedactor (backend .../Api/Logging/PhiRedactingLoggerProvider.cs) scrubs MRN/DOB/SSN/name but only in log output, not as teaching-file de-identification. WebAuthn (WebAuthnController.cs) is auth/sign only and is not bound to any critical-result acknowledgment. PROGRESS.md and docs/09-regulatory/traceability-matrix.md contain no entries for these modules. Note: per CLAUDE.md the locked stack (ASP.NET/Next) governs, so absence of the PRD's OSS libraries is not itself a gap — but here the FUNCTIONS themselves are absent, not merely a different library.

requirements.title: PR-001 — Random and rule-based selection of cases for peer review
requirements.evidence: No selection/sampling logic anywhere. No PeerReview entity in backend/RadioPad.Api/src/RadioPad.Domain/Entities/Entities.cs or Enums.cs; no controller in Controllers/*.cs; no scheduler/worker. Grep for peer.?review|radpeer returned only docs/PRD/roadmap matches, zero code.
requirements.gap: Build PeerReviewCase entity + random sampler (e.g. configurable %) and rule-based selectors (by modality/RADS/STAT), a background job to enqueue cases, and storage of selection provenance.

requirements.title: PR-002 — Anonymized assignment to peer reviewer
requirements.evidence: No reviewer-assignment model, no anonymization-for-review pipeline. PhiRedactor exists (Api/Logging/PhiRedactingLoggerProvider.cs) but is log-only and not used for review payloads. No assignment endpoint in any controller.
requirements.gap: Add reviewer assignment (round-robin/load-aware), strip author/patient identifiers from the review view, and enforce reviewer != author.

requirements.title: PR-003 — Reviewer scoring (concordance / minor / major discrepancy) with structured rationale
requirements.evidence: No scoring enum (Enums.cs has none), no PeerReviewScore entity, no scoring endpoint or UI. No 'concordance'/'discrepancy' code matches.
requirements.gap: Add RADPEER-style score enum + structured rationale entity, scoring API, and a reviewer UI inside AppShell.

requirements.title: PR-004 — Learning loop: discrepancies inform rulebook improvement suggestions
requirements.evidence: No discrepancy data captured (PR-003 missing) and no analytics feeding rulebook suggestions. Rulebook tooling (RulebooksController) has no peer-review feedback input.
requirements.gap: Aggregate discrepancy patterns and surface rulebook-edit suggestions; depends on PR-003 data first.

requirements.title: PR-005 — Quality dashboard for medical director
requirements.evidence: frontend/app/analytics/quality/page.tsx exists but is a validation QualityScore trend dashboard (avgScore/blockerCount by day/radiologist/rulebook via api.analytics.qualityTrends), explicitly NOT peer-review concordance/discrepancy metrics. No peer-review data source exists.
requirements.gap: Build a director dashboard over peer-review outcomes (concordance rate, major/minor discrepancy rates by reader/subspecialty). Could extend the existing quality page shell but needs new data.

requirements.title: PR-006 — Second-read workflow for STAT/high-risk findings
requirements.evidence: No second-read routing. Report lifecycle (ReportsLifecycleController.cs) supports sign/cosign/addendum but no STAT-triggered mandatory second read. No 'second.?read' code match.
requirements.gap: Detect STAT/high-risk (could reuse critical-language detector) and force a second reviewer before finalization, with state tracking.

requirements.title: PR-007 — Mobile review approvals for time-sensitive cases
requirements.evidence: Mobile app (frontend/app/mobile/*) has dictate/edit/sign flows only; no peer-review approval screen. No mobile review endpoint.
requirements.gap: Add a mobile peer-review/approval surface bound to PR-002/PR-003 backend; depends on those existing first.

requirements.title: PR-008 — Educational mode for residents (attending feedback, structured comments, scoring of resident drafts)
requirements.evidence: No resident/attending role distinction beyond UserRole enum (Radiologist/ReportingAdmin/MedicalDirector/etc. — no Resident/Attending). No feedback/comment entity on drafts. No education code matches.
requirements.gap: Add resident/attending relationship, structured attending feedback + scoring on resident drafts, and an educational review UI.

requirements.title: PR-009 — Discrepancy analytics by subspecialty, modality, reader, and rulebook
requirements.evidence: AnalyticsService/AnalyticsController compute report/quality/usage metrics but no discrepancy dimension (no peer-review data captured). No subspecialty field surfaced for this.
requirements.gap: Capture discrepancy events (PR-003) and add analytics slices by subspecialty/modality/reader/rulebook.

requirements.title: PR-010 — Export-only mode for external peer-review services
requirements.evidence: No peer-review export. Export tooling exists for reports/audit (ReportsController export, AuditController export) but nothing emits anonymized cases for external peer review.
requirements.gap: Add an anonymized case-bundle export targeted at external peer-review vendors.

requirements.title: TF-001 — One-click 'Add to teaching file' with mandatory de-identification
requirements.evidence: No TeachingFile entity/controller/permission/page anywhere. No 'add to teaching file' action in ReportClient.tsx or ReportsController. Grep teaching.?file returned only docs/PRD.
requirements.gap: Add TeachingFileCase entity + 'Add to teaching file' action that runs mandatory de-identification before persisting.

requirements.title: TF-002 — Auto-anonymization of report text, dates, and study identifiers
requirements.evidence: A reusable de-id primitive exists: PhiRedactor (backend/RadioPad.Api/src/RadioPad.Api/Logging/PhiRedactingLoggerProvider.cs) scrubs MRN/DOB/ISO-date/SSN/patient-name (verified by Iter31SecurityTests). BUT it is wired only into log redaction; there is no teaching-file anonymization pipeline that consumes it for report text/dates/study IDs.
requirements.gap: Repurpose/extend PhiRedactor into a teaching-file anonymizer covering accession/study UID/dates and confirm completeness; wire it into the TF-001 add action. Primitive exists, feature does not.

requirements.title: TF-003 — DICOM image attachment with pixel-level de-identification
requirements.evidence: DICOMweb context fetch exists (PacsController, OrthancBridgeController, AuditAction.DicomContextFetched) but no image attachment to a teaching file and no pixel-level (burned-in annotation) de-identification.
requirements.gap: Add image attachment + pixel/header de-id; gated on image module. Largest sub-item.

requirements.title: TF-004 — Case categorization by modality, diagnosis, RADS, teaching pearls
requirements.evidence: No teaching-file entity to categorize. RADS values are recognized by the validator regex but not stored as a teaching taxonomy. No categorization UI.
requirements.gap: Add categorization fields (modality/diagnosis/RADS/pearls) + tagging UI on teaching cases.

requirements.title: TF-005 — Quiz / question generator from teaching file metadata
requirements.evidence: No quiz generator, no Quiz entity, no AI prompt for question generation. No 'quiz' code matches.
requirements.gap: Generate quiz questions from teaching-file metadata (likely via AiGateway); depends on TF-004 metadata.

requirements.title: TF-006 — Export bundle compatible with MIRC-style teaching file formats
requirements.evidence: No MIRC/teaching export. No 'MIRC' match in repo code.
requirements.gap: Implement MIRC-compatible export bundle (manifest + de-identified content).

requirements.title: TF-007 — Per-tenant private teaching library; opt-in public sharing with attribution
requirements.evidence: No teaching library at all; tenant isolation exists generally (TenantedController) but there is no teaching collection to scope, and no public-share/attribution mechanism (marketplace is for rulebooks/templates, not teaching cases).
requirements.gap: Add per-tenant teaching library with opt-in public sharing + attribution metadata.

requirements.title: TF-008 — Resident learning analytics (cases viewed, quiz performance)
requirements.evidence: No view tracking or quiz scoring (TF-005 missing) and no resident role. AnalyticsService has no education metrics.
requirements.gap: Track case views + quiz scores per resident and surface analytics; depends on TF-005/PR-008.

requirements.title: CR-001 — Critical-result language detector (rule + AI ensemble)
requirements.evidence: Rule half implemented: ReportValidator.CheckCriticalLanguage (backend/.../RadioPad.Validation/Engine/ReportValidator.cs:448) detects critical phrasing (tension pneumothorax, free air, malpositioned tubes, BI-RADS 4C/5/6, Lung-RADS 4B/4X, LI-RADS 5/M/TIV, PI-RADS 5 with EPE/SVI) via regex, invoked by critical_result_language/critical_finding_language rules across 12 rulebooks; covered by rulebooks/_tests/chest_xray_v1/03-critical-language.json and mammo 05-birads-critical.json.
requirements.gap: Add the AI half of the ensemble (model-based critical-finding classification) and combine with the rule output for higher recall; today it is rule-only.

requirements.title: CR-002 — Tenant-defined criticality classes (red/orange/yellow) with required communication SLAs
requirements.evidence: No criticality-class config. Validator emits a single Blocker/severity-from-rule, with no red/orange/yellow taxonomy and no SLA timers. TenantSettingsController has no criticality config. No SLA fields in Entities.cs.
requirements.gap: Add tenant-configurable criticality classes + per-class communication SLA, and map detector hits to a class.

requirements.title: CR-003 — Communication workflow: who/when/by what means + acknowledgement timestamp
requirements.evidence: No communication record entity. The validator only checks that communication *language* is present in the impression/recommendations text (regex communicationPattern); it does not capture who/when/means or an acknowledgement timestamp. ReportStatus.Acknowledged is the radiologist acknowledging an AI draft, a different concept.
requirements.gap: Add a CriticalResultCommunication record (notifier, recipient, channel, timestamp, ack timestamp) with create/ack endpoints and UI.

requirements.title: CR-004 — Auto-page / SMS / app notification to ordering physician (via integration)
requirements.evidence: Push infra exists for the radiologist's own devices (PushController, AuditAction.PushDeviceRegistered/Tested) but nothing notifies an ordering physician, and no SMS/pager integration. No ordering-physician contact model.
requirements.gap: Integrate paging/SMS/app push to the ordering physician triggered by a confirmed critical result; reuse push plumbing but new recipient model + channels.

requirements.title: CR-005 — Mandatory text block insertion for confirmed critical findings
requirements.evidence: ReportValidator.CheckCriticalLanguage flags 'Critical finding lacks approved escalation/communication language.' as a Blocker when critical phrasing is present without communication phrasing (ReportValidator.cs:457-463). This enforces presence of communication wording at validation time.
requirements.gap: It only flags absence; it does not *insert* a mandatory structured communication/text block, nor enforce a specific tenant-defined block. Add auto-insertion + required structured block (ties to CR-002/CR-003).

requirements.title: CR-006 — Audit-grade record of every critical-result event
requirements.evidence: AuditAction enum (Enums.cs:87-192) has NO critical-result event kind; audit log (IAuditLog/AuditController) records AI/report/security events only. No critical-result event is appended.
requirements.gap: Define critical-result AuditAction kinds and append append-only records for detection/communication/ack/escalation events.

requirements.title: CR-007 — Mobile companion acknowledgement with WebAuthn
requirements.evidence: WebAuthn implemented for auth/sign-in (WebAuthnController.cs, AUTH-001) and used in mobile sign flow (MobileSignClient.tsx), but it is NOT bound to any critical-result acknowledgment because no critical-result event entity exists to acknowledge.
requirements.gap: Add a mobile critical-result ack screen requiring a WebAuthn assertion, writing the ack timestamp to the CR-003 record. WebAuthn primitive reusable; feature absent.

requirements.title: CR-008 — Configurable callback / read-back script
requirements.evidence: No read-back script config in TenantSettingsController or anywhere; no 'read-back'/'callback script' code.
requirements.gap: Add tenant-configurable read-back script surfaced during the communication step.

requirements.title: CR-009 — Escalation policy if no acknowledgement within N minutes
requirements.evidence: No escalation timer/worker. No 'escalation' code match in backend services. No SLA/ack data to time against (CR-002/CR-003 missing).
requirements.gap: Add a background escalation worker that re-notifies/escalates when ack is overdue per the CR-002 SLA.

requirements.title: CR-010 — Compliance reports for The Joint Commission / external accreditation
requirements.evidence: No critical-result compliance report. Export tooling exists for audit/usage but nothing reports critical-result timeliness/communication compliance.
requirements.gap: Build a compliance report over CR-006 events (timeliness, ack rates, escalations) suitable for accreditation; depends on the event record existing.

area: PRD v2 §23 Integration Requirements + §24 Technical Architecture
summary: RadioPad has implemented a large, genuine slice of §23/§24 on the locked ASP.NET Core 8 + Next.js 16 + Tauri 2 + Capacitor 6 stack — judged on function, not on the OSS libraries the PRD named. Strongly covered: DICOMweb QIDO/WADO/STOW client + tenant-scoped proxy (DicomWebClient.cs, PacsController.cs); FHIR R4 DiagnosticReport export + bidirectional ServiceRequest/DiagnosticReport ingest (FhirDiagnosticReportSerializer.cs, IngestController.cs); HL7 v2 inbound MLLP listener + ORU export + DICOM SR→ORU conversion (Hl7MllpListener.cs, OrthancBridgeController.cs, Hl7OruSerializer.cs); clipboard/desktop bridge (Tauri secure_copy); vendor connector adapters + signed-plugin SDK (Sectra/Visage/Carestream + PacsVendorRouter + desktop/plugin-sdk); SSO/OIDC + SAML 2.0 + SCIM 2.0 (OidcBearerMiddleware, SamlController, ScimController); Stripe billing; SIEM continuous push + snapshot (SiemPushService, Splunk/Sentinel/Elastic/Syslog sinks); REST + OpenAPI (4281-line spec, ~191 operations); Postgres+SQLite data layer; local llama.cpp/Ollama inference; Tauri installers (msi/dmg/deb/appimage/rpm) + signed auto-updater; Docker Compose deployment. Notable GAPS where the FUNCTION (not just the OSS name) is absent: FHIR R5 / Bulk FHIR $export; HL7 ADT message type + in-app MLLP/TLS + outbound ORU delivery (outbox is in-memory only, no MLLP sender); report→DICOM SR export; standalone DICOM UPS-RS worklist/prioritization service; 8 of 11 named enterprise connectors; Lago/Killbill billing alternatives; Slack/Teams/Webhook critical-results FANOUT (only inbound webhooks + push test exist); gRPC, GraphQL, AsyncAPI, and SSE/WebSocket streaming/real-time collaboration in the backend (no .proto, no text/event-stream — SSE exists only in the legacy Node daemon); published TS/Python/Go/Rust SDKs (documented as Planned only); IHE XDS-I.b/SWF.b/AIW-I; MDM (Intune/Jamf/WS ONE); analytics/audit ClickHouse, Redis, NATS, OpenSearch, pgvector/Qdrant, MinIO/S3 (substituted by Postgres/local); vLLM/TGI hosted inference, speculative decoding, NPU acceleration; Helm/k3s/Nix deployment artifacts; frontend module federation, PWA service worker, web workers; desktop gRPC-over-UNIX-socket daemon bridge (uses HTTP sidecar health instead). PROGRESS.md claims (iter-14/15/16/19/20/31/32/33) were verified against real code and are largely accurate, though it counts several rows as fully closed that are actually partial against the PRD clause (e.g. SIEM sink set, vendor connector breadth, HL7 directionality).

requirements.title: §23.1 DICOMweb for study metadata + worklist (QIDO/WADO/STOW)
requirements.evidence: backend/RadioPad.Api/src/RadioPad.Api/Services/DicomWebClient.cs implements QIDO-RS FetchStudyAsync/SearchStudiesAsync, WADO-RS RetrieveInstanceMetadataAsync, STOW-RS StoreInstancesAsync, HealthAsync; backend/RadioPad.Api/src/RadioPad.Api/Controllers/PacsController.cs exposes GET/POST /api/pacs/studies + /api/pacs/health, vendor-neutral, PHI-minimised audit. Tests: DicomWebClientUnitTests.cs, DicomInstanceMetadataTests.cs.
requirements.gap: DICOMweb metadata/WADO/STOW are complete. The 'worklist (UPS-RS)' half is NOT met by this client — see UPS-RS row.

requirements.title: §23.1 / §23.2 DICOM UPS-RS worklist + worklist service/prioritization (§24.1 worklist module)
requirements.evidence: No UPS-RS endpoints in DicomWebClient.cs or controllers (grep for UPS-RS/UpsRs matches only PRD/docs). Worklist function exists only as vendor-adapter FetchWorklistAsync in backend/.../Services/Pacs/IPacsVendorAdapter.cs + Sectra/Visage/Carestream adapters; there is no standalone DICOM UPS-RS ingest or prioritization service.
requirements.gap: Implement UPS-RS (workitem subscribe/retrieve/change-state) and a worklist/prioritization service that aggregates DICOM UPS + HL7 ingest into a prioritized worklist; currently inbound work arrives only as Draft reports via HL7/FHIR/Orthanc hooks.

requirements.title: §23.1 / §23.2 FHIR R4 DiagnosticReport export
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Application/Services/FhirDiagnosticReportSerializer.cs serializes Report→R4 DiagnosticReport (identifier, status, code, subject, conclusion, presentedForm); exposed at GET /api/reports/{id}/export/fhir (ReportsController.cs L477-490, application/fhir+json, RPT-012 acknowledged gating).
requirements.gap: None for R4 DiagnosticReport export. Output is a single resource (no Bundle/Provenance) — acceptable for the clause.

requirements.title: §23.1 FHIR R4 ImagingStudy exchange
requirements.evidence: No ImagingStudy serializer/parser found; FHIR work is limited to ServiceRequest + DiagnosticReport (IngestController.cs, FhirDiagnosticReportSerializer.cs). grep 'ImagingStudy' hits only PRD/docs.
requirements.gap: Add FHIR ImagingStudy read/emit so study-level imaging metadata can be exchanged with the EHR (PRD lists ImagingStudy alongside DiagnosticReport).

requirements.title: §23.2 FHIR R5 + Bulk FHIR ($export)
requirements.evidence: All FHIR code is R4 only (Consumes application/fhir+json, R4 ServiceRequest/DiagnosticReport parsing in IngestController.cs). No $export / NDJSON bulk operation; grep 'Bulk FHIR'/'$export'/'R5' matches only PRD/docs.
requirements.gap: Add R5 resource variants and a Bulk Data $export (group/system-level async NDJSON) endpoint. PRD targets this for Clinical Beta/GA.

requirements.title: §23.1 / §23.2 HL7 v2 ORU (results) + ORM (orders) over MLLP
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Infrastructure/Integration/Hl7MllpListener.cs (BackgroundService, MLLP framing via MllpFramer.cs) + Hl7MessageHandler.cs ingests ORU^R01/ORM^O01 → Draft Report with AA/AE/AR acks; GET /api/reports/{id}/export/hl7 emits ORU^R01 (ReportsController.cs L582). Tests: Hl7MllpListenerTests.cs.
requirements.gap: (a) Inbound handler explicitly rejects everything except ORU/ORM (no ADT — see ADT row). (b) Outbound result delivery: ORU export is download-only; the Orthanc-derived ORU goes to InMemoryHl7Outbox (no DB persistence, no MLLP client that connects to a RIS and sends). Add a persistent outbound MLLP sender.

requirements.title: §23.1 HL7 v2 ADT (admit/discharge)
requirements.evidence: Hl7MessageHandler.cs L52-54 short-circuits: 'if (typeRoot is not ("ORU" or "ORM")) return ...AR Unsupported message type'. No ADT (A01/A03/A08) parsing or patient-context handling.
requirements.gap: Add ADT message handling (patient demographics/encounter context) to the MLLP handler and parser.

requirements.title: §23.1 HL7 MLLP transport secured with TLS
requirements.evidence: Hl7MllpListener.cs opens a raw TcpListener; binds 127.0.0.1 by default (RADIOPAD_HL7_MLLP_BIND). No SslStream / certificate handling in-process; PRD specifies 'MLLP+TLS'.
requirements.gap: Either terminate TLS in-app (SslStream + client-cert/mTLS) or document+enforce a TLS-terminating reverse proxy; today remote exposure would be cleartext MLLP.

requirements.title: §23.1 DICOM SR for measurement-bearing reports
requirements.evidence: Inbound only: DicomSrToHl7Converter.cs converts a stored DICOM SR (Orthanc sr-stored hook, OrthancBridgeController.cs) into ORU^R01; Hl7ToDicomSrConverter.cs + Hl7DicomSrRoundTripTests.cs exist. No report→DICOM SR EXPORT path.
requirements.gap: Add report→DICOM SR encoding/STOW so measurement-bearing reports can be written back to PACS as SR objects (PRD frames SR as a report exchange format, not just an inbound bridge).

requirements.title: §23.1 Clipboard / desktop bridge (universal fallback)
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/main.rs secure_copy command (clipboard write + TTL auto-clear + clear-on-blur, PRD DESK-004); RIS copy/paste bridge row marked across MVP in PRD integration matrix; frontend api.pacs/IPC surface.
requirements.gap: None material.

requirements.title: §23.1 Custom enterprise connectors (plugin) + open connector SDK
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Infrastructure/Pacs/{SectraIds7Adapter,Visage7Adapter,CarestreamVueAdapter}.cs behind IPacsVendorAdapter + PacsVendorRouter.cs (keyed DI for sectra|visage|carestream). Open connector SDK shipped: desktop/plugin-sdk/ (manifest.schema.json, example-sectra-plugin) with Ed25519/SHA-256 signed-plugin verification (desktop/src-tauri/src/pacs_plugins.rs); CLI 'radiopad pacs plugins'.
requirements.gap: Only 3 of the 11 named vendors implemented (Epic Aura, Cerner, GE Centricity, Philips IntelliSpace, Merge, Change Healthcare, Fujifilm Synapse, Intelerad absent). SDK exists but vendor breadth is far from the PRD list. Each remaining adapter is incremental.

requirements.title: §23.2 SSO / OIDC
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Api/Middleware/OidcBearerMiddleware.cs + Auth/OidcProfiles.cs (JWKS bearer validation, profiles); frontend/app/admin/sso/page.tsx lists OIDC profiles. AuthController magic-link/device-flow.
requirements.gap: None material for the clause.

requirements.title: §23.2 SAML 2.0
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Api/Controllers/SamlController.cs — GET /saml/metadata + POST /saml/acs with SignedXml verification against RADIOPAD_SAML_IDP_CERT_PEM; audits UserLogin{method:saml}; live-IdP test gated as a skipped integration suite (PROGRESS.md).
requirements.gap: Single-IdP, hand-rolled SignedXml (no multi-IdP federation, no SP-initiated AuthnRequest/SLO). Adequate for the matrix row; extend if multi-IdP needed.

requirements.title: §23.2 SCIM 2.0 provisioning
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Api/Controllers/ScimController.cs — RFC7644 Users list/get/create/replace/patch(active)/delete, tenant-scoped bearer, constant-time compare, soft-delete preserves audit chain. Iter-20 in PROGRESS.md.
requirements.gap: User resource covered; Group provisioning (schema declared but) not fully implemented. Minor vs GA target.

requirements.title: §23.2 Stripe / Lago / Killbill billing
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Api/Controllers/BillingController.cs is Stripe-only (Stripe + Stripe.Checkout SDK, checkout/portal/webhook/invoices/refund/status); SubscriptionLifecycleService.cs, idempotent on Stripe Event.Id. No Lago/Killbill provider abstraction.
requirements.gap: Stripe satisfies the primary path. Lago/Killbill (self-hosted billing alternatives) absent — would need an IBillingProvider abstraction. Likely fine for GA if Stripe is the supported provider.

requirements.title: §23.2 SIEM export (Splunk, Sentinel, Elastic, Chronicle, Loki)
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Api/Services/SiemPushService.cs (BackgroundService) drains audit log to ISiemSink set in Sinks.cs: Splunk HEC, Sentinel Log Analytics, Elastic _bulk, Syslog UDP; plus snapshot GET /api/audit/siem?format=json|cef (OtherControllers.cs). Tests: SiemSinkTests.cs. PHI-minimised (no DetailsJson).
requirements.gap: 3 of 5 named SIEMs as native sinks (Splunk/Sentinel/Elastic); Chronicle and Loki not as dedicated sinks (Syslog/CEF can partially cover Loki/QRadar). Add Chronicle + Loki push sinks for full parity.

requirements.title: §23.2 Slack / Teams / Webhook for critical results
requirements.evidence: No outbound Slack/Teams/critical-result fanout in backend (grep slack|teams across backend/*.cs only hits a test comment; 'critical' only hits validation severity + AnomalyDetector). Inbound order webhooks exist (IngestController.cs) and mobile push test (PushController.cs), but no critical-finding notification dispatch to Slack/Teams/webhook.
requirements.gap: Build a critical-results notification service: detect critical/Blocker findings, fan out to configurable Slack/Teams/generic webhook channels with acknowledgement tracking. PRD marks this MVP-β.

requirements.title: §23.2 IHE XDS-I.b, SWF.b, AIW-I
requirements.evidence: No IHE profile code (grep XDS-I/SWF/AIW only in PRD/docs). GA-only rows in the matrix.
requirements.gap: Implement IHE XDS-I.b (imaging doc sharing), SWF.b (scheduled workflow), AIW-I (AI workflow) actors. Large, standards-heavy; GA target.

requirements.title: §23.2 MDM (Intune, Jamf, Workspace ONE)
requirements.evidence: No MDM enrollment/compliance integration found in mobile/ or desktop/. GA-only row.
requirements.gap: Add MDM compliance attestation / managed-config consumption for mobile + desktop. GA target.

requirements.title: §23.3 Public API — REST + OpenAPI for all CRUD
requirements.evidence: openapi/openapi.yaml (4281 lines, ~191 documented operations); ASP.NET controllers across Reports/Rulebook/Template/Auth/Billing/Pacs/Ingest/Scim/etc.; docs/03-architecture/api-reference.md.
requirements.gap: None material; ensure all controllers stay reflected in the spec.

requirements.title: §23.3 WebSocket / SSE for streaming AI + real-time collaboration
requirements.evidence: No text/event-stream or WebSocket in backend controllers (grep returns only StreamReader/MemoryStream usage; CopilotController.cs has no streaming). AI draft endpoints return buffered JSON. SSE exists only in legacy Node daemon (daemon/json-event-stream.js) which is read-only reference. No CRDT relay / live collaboration.
requirements.gap: Add SSE or WebSocket token streaming for AI generation and a real-time collaboration channel (PRD §24.1 reporting 'CRDT relay'). Currently AI is request/response only.

requirements.title: §23.3 gRPC between internal services and to the daemon
requirements.evidence: No .proto files anywhere (find *.proto = none); Program.cs has no AddGrpc/MapGrpc. Desktop-daemon bridge (sidecar_manager.rs) is an HTTP health/sidecar pattern, not gRPC over UNIX socket / named pipe as PRD §24.7 specifies.
requirements.gap: Backend is a single monolith so internal gRPC is moot, but the desktop↔daemon gRPC bridge described in §24.7 is not implemented (HTTP only). Add gRPC IPC if/when the daemon split is real.

requirements.title: §23.3 GraphQL (optional, post-GA)
requirements.evidence: No GraphQL schema/server. PRD marks this optional/post-GA.
requirements.gap: None required pre-GA; implement only if read-heavy admin views need it.

requirements.title: §23.3 AsyncAPI for event channels (NATS subjects, webhooks)
requirements.evidence: No AsyncAPI document; no NATS (grep NATS only in docs). Webhooks exist (ingest + Stripe + FHIR signature) but are undocumented in an AsyncAPI spec.
requirements.gap: Author an AsyncAPI spec for webhook/event channels. Lower value while there is no event bus.

requirements.title: §23.3 SDKs: TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust
requirements.evidence: docs/08-user-docs/sdk-guide.md explicitly: 'RadioPad does not ship a first-party SDK in v0.x ... planned for Phase 2'. No sdks/ directory; interim guidance is to copy frontend/lib/api.ts or hand-roll HttpClient.
requirements.gap: Generate/publish at least TypeScript + Python SDKs from the OpenAPI spec; Go/Rust later. Currently documentation-only.

requirements.title: §24.1 Service modules (identity, tenant, reporting, rulebook, template, integration, audit, billing, etc.)
requirements.evidence: All listed RESPONSIBILITIES exist as classes/controllers/services inside the single ASP.NET Core solution (Domain/Application/Validation/Infrastructure/Api): identity (Oidc/Saml/Scim/WebAuthn), tenant (TenantedController), reporting (ReportsController/ReportingService), rulebook, template, ai-gateway (AiGateway provider routing + policy), validation (ReportValidator), integration (Dicom/HL7/FHIR), audit (IAuditLog hash chain), billing, marketplace, analytics (AnalyticsService), notification (push), governance (rulebook governance), peer-review.
requirements.gap: Per locked stack, function over microservice topology is the correct judgment — implemented as a modular monolith. Gaps are the few responsibilities not built at all: federation/FL (EE), desktop-sync as a service, and the worklist/notification critical-results functions (tracked in their own rows).

requirements.title: §24.1 federation (EE) — FL coordinator, DP accountant
requirements.evidence: No federated-learning coordinator or differential-privacy accountant code (grep federation/FL/DP-accountant → none in backend). EE-only.
requirements.gap: Implement FL coordinator + DP accountant for the EE tier. Large, research-grade; lowest priority.

requirements.title: §24.2 Data stores — Postgres primary OLTP (per-tenant)
requirements.evidence: Program.cs L47-61 AddDbContext UseNpgsql (prod) / UseSqlite (dev/test); EF Core migrations under Infrastructure/Migrations; tenant isolation via TenantedController.ResolveContextAsync + TenantId predicates.
requirements.gap: Per-tenant schema separation / dedicated EE clusters are not implemented (single shared schema with TenantId scoping). Acceptable for current scale; revisit for EE isolation.

requirements.title: §24.2 Data stores — ClickHouse, Redis, NATS, OpenSearch, pgvector/Qdrant, MinIO/S3, SQLite+SQLCipher
requirements.evidence: SQLite present (dev). Daemon-local SQLCipher: desktop uses OS-keyring-backed encrypted local cache/offline drafts (local_cache.rs, offline_drafts.rs, crypto_keyring.rs) — function present though not literally SQLCipher. No ClickHouse (audit/analytics run on the OLTP DB), no Redis (rate-limit/session are in-process), no NATS event bus, no OpenSearch text search, no pgvector/Qdrant semantic search, no MinIO/S3 object store (PDF/DOCX rendered on the fly, not stored).
requirements.gap: These are scale/feature substrates: add object storage (S3/MinIO) for exports, a cache/rate-limit store (Redis), event bus (NATS), analytics columnar store (ClickHouse), and vector search (pgvector) when those features (similar-case retrieval, high-volume analytics) are built. Functionally substituted today.

requirements.title: §24.3 Inference — local daemon embeds llama.cpp + ONNX, backend switches per platform
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Infrastructure/Providers/Local/LlamaCppProvider.cs + OllamaProvider.cs (local PHI-safe inference); AiGateway routes providers + enforces compliance policy (ProviderPolicyException). Tests: LlamaCppProviderTests.cs, OllamaProviderTests.cs.
requirements.gap: llama.cpp/Ollama covered; ONNX Runtime backend and per-platform backend switching not implemented. NPU/quantization rows below.

requirements.title: §24.3 Inference — hosted vLLM/TGI autoscaling, speculative decoding, prefix caching, continuous batching
requirements.evidence: No vLLM/TGI container orchestration, no speculative-decoding/prefix-cache/continuous-batching code (grep → none). Hosted inference is via external API providers (Anthropic etc.) per integration-architecture.md.
requirements.gap: Stand up self-hosted vLLM/TGI with queue-depth autoscaling if hosted GPU inference is a product requirement; otherwise rely on external providers.

requirements.title: §24.3 Inference — model registry (ids, versions, hashes, license, eval, drift baseline)
requirements.evidence: Governance/model-registry surface referenced (governance module, rulebook governance, drift watcher in §24.1 list) and RulebookGovernanceTests.cs; provider catalog hardening in PROGRESS.md iter-46. A full model-registry with hashes/license/eval/drift baseline for inference models is not confirmed in code.
requirements.gap: Implement a first-class model registry table tracking model id/version/hash/license/eval scores/drift baseline distinct from rulebook governance.

requirements.title: §24.3 Inference — quantized models (GGUF/INT8/FP16) + NPU acceleration (CoreML/DirectML/OpenVINO/ROCm/Vulkan)
requirements.evidence: No quantization/NPU backend selection code; LlamaCppProvider delegates to whatever the host llama.cpp build supports but RadioPad does not select GGUF Q4/Q5, INT8 ONNX, or NPU runtimes.
requirements.gap: Add explicit quantized-model selection and NPU/accelerator backend switching per platform.

requirements.title: §24.4 Twelve-factor adherence (stateless, env config, stdout JSON logs, disposable workers, parity)
requirements.evidence: Config via env (BillingEnv, RADIOPAD_* env vars throughout); secrets via env:NAME indirection + KMS providers (AwsKms/AzureKeyVault/GcpKms); structured logging + PHI redaction (PhiRedactingLoggerProvider.cs); BackgroundServices with graceful shutdown (SiemPushService, Hl7MllpListener); Docker Compose for dev/prod parity (deploy/docker-compose*.yml).
requirements.gap: OTel-aware stdout JSON logging is partial (structured logging yes; confirm OTel exporter). 'Build=release=run via signed artifacts' is enforced for desktop (signed Tauri bundles) but server artifact signing not evidenced. Vault not integrated (KMS used instead).

requirements.title: §24.5 Deployment — Helm, Docker Compose, k3s air-gapped, Nix flakes, Tauri installers, CLI distribution
requirements.evidence: Docker Compose present (deploy/docker-compose.yml + .prod.yml, Dockerfile.api, Caddyfile.prod, vps/nginx.conf). Tauri installers configured (tauri.conf.json bundle targets msi/dmg/deb/appimage/rpm) + signed auto-updater + CI (.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml, tauri-updater.yml, desktop-installer-verify.yml). CLI is a .NET global tool (cli/RadioPad.Cli).
requirements.gap: No Helm charts, no k3s air-gapped bundle, no Nix flakes (find → none). CLI distribution channels (Homebrew/Scoop/winget/apt/dnf/AUR) not set up. MSIX/PKG(notarized)/Flatpak/Snap not in bundle targets.

requirements.title: §24.6 Frontend — React+Vite (locked→Next.js), route code-splitting ≤200KB, CSP+Trusted Types+SRI
requirements.evidence: Next.js 16 App Router is the LOCKED stack (CLAUDE.md) — React+Vite substitution is correct. Route-based code splitting is inherent to App Router. CSP enforced via reverse proxy (deploy/Caddyfile.prod, vps/nginx.conf) and Tauri CSP (tauri.conf.json security.csp); recent commit 0a94af26 tuned CSP for hydration.
requirements.gap: No explicit ≤200KB initial-JS budget gate; Trusted Types and SRI (integrity=) not configured; CSP allows 'unsafe-inline' for hydration. Add Trusted Types policy, SRI on external assets, and a bundle-size budget check.

requirements.title: §24.6 Frontend — module federation for plugin UI, Service Worker PWA + offline drafts, Web Workers, WASM NLP
requirements.evidence: No service worker (frontend/public/sw.js etc. = none found; CSP/serviceWorker grep hits are .next build artifacts only). No module federation, no new Worker(...) usage, no WASM NLP in frontend source. Offline drafts exist on DESKTOP (Tauri offline_drafts.rs) not as a web PWA.
requirements.gap: Add a PWA service worker + offline draft cache for the web client, web workers for parsing/validation/FHIR transforms, and module federation (or App-Router equivalent) for plugin UIs.

requirements.title: §24.7 Desktop (Tauri) — hotkeys, native notifications, auto-updater, crash reporter, sidecar, daemon gRPC bridge
requirements.evidence: desktop/src-tauri/src/main.rs registers global hotkeys (tauri-plugin-global-shortcut, DESK-003), secure clipboard, device pairing, PHI log redaction; sidecar_manager.rs spawns + health-monitors the radiopad-api sidecar (DESK-015); tauri.conf.json configures signed updater + installer bundles; pacs_plugins.rs signed-plugin loader.
requirements.gap: Daemon bridge is HTTP sidecar health-check, NOT gRPC over UNIX socket / named pipe (§24.7). Native notifications and a crash reporter are not evidenced in the Rust modules. rdev-based OS hotkeys are via the Tauri plugin (acceptable).

requirements.title: §24.8 Mobile (Capacitor) — biometrics, WebAuthn passkeys, push (APNs/FCM), screenshot protection, encrypted local store
requirements.evidence: mobile/capacitor.config.ts configures PushNotifications (MOB-007); frontend/lib/biometric.ts wraps @aparajita/capacitor-biometric-auth (Face ID/Touch ID/BiometricPrompt); frontend/lib/secureAuth.ts for secure auth storage; WebAuthn passkeys via backend WebAuthnController.cs + frontend; PushController.cs registers ios/android/web tokens; APNs sender (Push/PushSender.cs ApnsSender).
requirements.gap: Push: APNs sender present but FCM (Android) sender not confirmed. Screenshot protection (FLAG_SECURE / iOS screen-capture block) not found in mobile/. Encrypted local store is via Capacitor plugin abstraction; confirm Keychain/Keystore backing. Add FCM sender + screenshot protection native module.

summary: RadioPad implements the core, P0 security/privacy controls of §18 well on the locked .NET/Next.js/Tauri stack: PHI policy enforcement (AiGateway.EnforcePhiPolicy + ProviderPolicyException, fail-closed, audited), AES-256-GCM column encryption with per-tenant KMS envelope encryption and real AWS/Azure/GCP CMK adapters, a SHA-256 hash-chained append-only audit log with a verification routine, fine-grained RBAC/ABAC (PermissionCatalog + EndpointPermissionMatrix), SSO (OIDC presets + SAML ACS), MFA via WebAuthn with hand-rolled attestation parsing + FIDO MDS3 root pinning, SCIM 2.0 provisioning, IP allowlists + anomaly detection/SIEM push, signed desktop binaries (Tauri ed25519 via OIDC->SSM), env-ref/OAuth-vault secret storage with a 15-minute OAuth rotation worker, CSP/HSTS/X-Frame security headers, and a model-drift BackgroundService for post-market monitoring. The compliance/regulatory posture (§18.3, §19) is documented thoroughly (22 security docs + 12 regulatory docs: BAA template, EU AI Act/GDPR profile, PMS plan, SaMD classification, ISO 14971 risk register, IEC 62304 SDLC, intended-use, traceability matrix). The principal gaps are supply-chain automation and process-as-code: SBOM (SEC-019) and SLSA-3 provenance (SEC-020) are documented but NOT generated in CI; the §19.5 claims-policy is doc-only (no CI lint gate for banned words like 'diagnose'/'interpret'); §18.1 mTLS/SPIFFE-SPIRE and the full secrets-rotation cadence (90d API keys / 30d mTLS certs) are aspirational; Trusted Types + SRI (SEC-018), confidential computing (SEC-023), container image signing/admission (SEC-021), honeypots (SEC-022), and BYOK+HYOK depth (SEC-003) are partial/missing. Note CLAUDE.md locks the stack, so Go/Rust/Keycloak absence is not counted against function; judgments are by capability. Several P2/EE controls and AI Act runtime artifacts (PCCP runtime, post-market field-failure workflow UI) remain documentation rather than enforced code.

requirements.title: SEC-001 — TLS 1.3 only on perimeter (1.2 for legacy MLLP)
requirements.evidence: deploy/vps/nginx.conf and deploy/Caddyfile.prod terminate TLS and set HSTS (max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains). Backend binds 127.0.0.1 (RADIOPAD_BIND) behind an operator TLS reverse proxy; traceability-matrix.md SEC-001 = done. No app-level TLS version pinning.
requirements.gap: TLS version (1.3-only with 1.2 legacy exception) is delegated to the operator proxy config, not pinned/asserted in repo. Add a documented hardened nginx/Caddy TLS block (ssl_protocols TLSv1.3; legacy vhost TLSv1.2) and a conformance check.

requirements.title: SEC-002 — AES-256-GCM at rest; per-tenant KMS keys
requirements.evidence: backend/.../Infrastructure/Security/AesGcmColumnEncryptor.cs (AES-256-GCM, versioned [ver|nonce|ct|tag] envelope, enc:v1: prefix, EF ValueConverters via ColumnEncryptorAccessor, prod guard). Per-tenant DEK envelope wrap/unwrap in Application/Services/Kms/KmsProvider.cs + Infrastructure/Kms/TenantDekCache.cs with tenant-bound AAD.
requirements.gap: Production requires operator to set RADIOPAD_COLUMN_KEY_REF + wrapped DEK; dev fallback key is deterministic (guarded out of Production). Full DB/volume-level at-rest encryption is delegated to the storage layer (documented).

requirements.title: SEC-003 — Customer-managed keys (BYOK + HYOK)
requirements.evidence: Infrastructure/Kms/AwsKmsProvider.cs, AzureKeyVaultKmsProvider.cs, GcpKmsProvider.cs (real cloud adapters, schemes aws:/azkv:/gcp:), EnvKmsProvider/LocalKmsProvider, IKmsResolver, tenant-aware Wrap/Unwrap with encryption-context binding, VerifyAsync health check + /api/tenant/settings/kms/verify, TenantSettings.CmkKeyRef/CmkLastVerifiedAt. KmsAdapterTests cover the adapters.
requirements.gap: BYOK (customer-supplied CMK) is covered via CmkKeyRef. HYOK (hold-your-own-key, customer-side key never leaving customer HSM with split-knowledge / remote unwrap proxy) is not implemented — current model unwraps the DEK in-process. Add HYOK remote-unwrap path + key-residency attestation.

requirements.title: SEC-004 / SEC-012 — Per-tenant PHI policy + provider compliance enforcement
requirements.evidence: Application/Services/AiGateway.cs EnforcePhiPolicy: blocks disabled/Blocked providers, requires PhiApproved or LocalOnly for ContainsPhi, throws ProviderPolicyException, fail-closed, audits AuditAction.ProviderBlocked. ProviderComplianceClass enum, Tenant.RequirePhiApprovedProvider. Tests: AiGatewayPolicyTests, ProviderEndpointPolicyTests, AiPolicyHttpTests.
requirements.gap: PRD says 'enforced in daemon + gateway'; on the locked stack the gateway is the enforcement point (no separate Go/Rust daemon). Desktop/daemon-side pre-flight PHI gate (defense-in-depth before the request leaves the workstation) is not present.

requirements.title: SEC-005 — Audit log immutability (hash-chained, append-only, optional timestamping)
requirements.evidence: Infrastructure/Repositories/Repositories.cs EfAuditLog.AppendAsync computes IntegrityChain = sha256(id|tenantId|action|detailsJson|prev) under a Serializable transaction; VerifyAsync walks the chain and reports FirstBrokenEventId. Entities.cs AuditEvent.IntegrityChain. Append-only by convention (no UPDATE/DELETE paths).
requirements.gap: Immutability is by-convention (DB rows are technically mutable; no WORM object store / DB row-level immutability trigger). Optional RFC-3161 trusted timestamping is not implemented.

requirements.title: SEC-006 — Least-privilege RBAC + ABAC
requirements.evidence: Application/Security/PermissionCatalog.cs (30+ granular RbacPermission defs incl. clinical flag), RolePermissionMap.cs, RolePermissionService.cs, IPermissionService.cs, EndpointPermissionMatrix.cs; enforced across controllers; Integration/EndpointPermissionRbacTests.cs. Tenant isolation via TenantedController.ResolveContextAsync.
requirements.gap: ABAC is attribute-influenced (clinical flag, tenant scope) rather than a full policy-engine (e.g., OPA/Cedar) for arbitrary resource attributes. Sufficient for stated needs; richer attribute conditions would harden 'sensitive resources'.

requirements.title: SEC-007 — SSO (OIDC/SAML), MFA (WebAuthn preferred), SCIM
requirements.evidence: Middleware/OidcBearerMiddleware.cs + Auth/OidcProfiles.cs (presets); Controllers/SamlController.cs (ACS); Controllers/WebAuthnController.cs with WebAuthn/AttestationParser.cs (packed/fido-u2f/none, x5c chain) + FidoMdsMetadataSources.cs (MDS3 root pinning); Controllers/ScimController.cs (RFC 7644 Users CRUD/PATCH + Groups + bearer rotation). Tests: Iter32AuthTests, WebAuthnAttestationTests, Iter35WebAuthnRootPinTests, frontend admin/sso page.
requirements.gap: TOTP MFA + WebAuthn both present; SAML is SP-side ACS (no full IdP-metadata signing-cert rotation UI). Minor: SCIM filter beyond userName eq is limited.

requirements.title: SEC-008 — IP allowlists + device posture checks
requirements.evidence: Middleware/IpAllowlistMiddleware.cs + GlobalIpAllowlistMiddleware.cs (per-tenant TenantSettings.IpAllowlistJson + RADIOPAD_IP_ALLOWLIST CIDR fallback); device_fingerprint + device_pairing_token in iter-31; SuspensionGuardMiddleware. Iter32NetworkDefenseTests.
requirements.gap: IP allowlist is complete; 'device posture checks' (OS patch level, disk-encryption, EDR attestation for Studio + daemon) are only a device-pairing token, not true posture assessment.

requirements.title: SEC-009 — Secret storage in OS keychain + Vault; never plaintext on disk
requirements.evidence: ApiKeySecretRef='env:<NAME>' indirection (never inline secret), Application/Services/OAuthRefreshVault.cs (column-encrypted OAuth refresh tokens), Infrastructure/Pacs/PacsSecretResolver.cs, SecretHandlingTests.cs. Desktop secrets via Tauri.
requirements.gap: Env-var + encrypted-DB vault is the implemented path; no integration with HashiCorp Vault or OS keychain (desktop keychain) as named. Add Vault provider + OS-keychain binding for desktop.

requirements.title: SEC-010 — PHI redaction in debug logs; structured logging only
requirements.evidence: AiGateway logs only SHA-256 hashes of prompt/output (Sha256), never raw text; SIEM export deliberately excludes DetailsJson; traceability-matrix SEC-010 references server log filters + desktop log_redactor.rs (tracing-subscriber MakeWriter). Structured logging via ILogger.
requirements.gap: Redaction relies on hashing-at-source + a desktop redactor; no central allow-list-based PHI scrubber middleware over all log sinks to catch accidental PHI in arbitrary log statements.

requirements.title: SEC-011 — Intrusion detection + anomaly alerts
requirements.evidence: Api/Services/AnomalyDetector.cs (BackgroundService, four anomaly patterns) + SiemPushService.cs continuous push + SiemController/SiemContracts (NDJSON/CEF export). Iter32 promoted SEC-011.
requirements.gap: Custom anomaly detection exists; the PRD-named Falco (host/container runtime IDS) is not present (out-of-process infra concern). Add infra-layer IDS deployment manifests.

requirements.title: SEC-013 — Signed binaries; verified updates; rollback on failed health check
requirements.evidence: .github/workflows/desktop-release.yml: Tauri ed25519 signing keys pulled via GitHub OIDC -> AWS IAM -> SSM with-decryption; signed bundle + signature uploaded; tauri-updater.yml; desktop/UPDATE_SIGNING.md. Update verification is Tauri's signature check.
requirements.gap: Signing + verified updates implemented; automatic rollback if first run fails health checks is not evidenced in the desktop updater flow. Add post-update health-probe + rollback-to-previous-version logic.

requirements.title: SEC-014 — Crypto: Ed25519 sigs, X25519 KEX, AES-GCM-SIV (nonce-misuse resistance)
requirements.evidence: Ed25519 used for desktop update signing (Tauri). AES-256-GCM used for column + KMS wrap. SHA-256 audit chain.
requirements.gap: X25519 key exchange not used (no in-app E2E channel needing it; TLS handled by proxy). AES-GCM-SIV is NOT used — the implementation uses plain AES-GCM with random 12-byte nonces (acceptable but not the nonce-misuse-resistant mode the PRD specifies). Migrate at-rest cipher to AES-GCM-SIV or document risk acceptance.

requirements.title: SEC-015 — Encrypted backups; quarterly restore drills
requirements.evidence: docs/04-security/disaster-recovery.md + business-continuity.md describe backup/restore; data is column-encrypted so backups inherit encryption.
requirements.gap: No automated encrypted-backup job or restore-drill harness in repo; restore drills are a documented process only. Add backup automation + a scheduled restore-verification job.

requirements.title: SEC-016 — Secrets rotation policy (API keys 90d, mTLS certs 30d, session tokens 15m)
requirements.evidence: Api/Services/OAuthRefreshRotationService.cs rotates OAuth refresh tokens on a 15-minute scan cadence; SessionEpoch supports session invalidation; ScimBearerRotated audit action; docs/04-security/secrets-management.md states the policy.
requirements.gap: Concrete enforced cadences for API keys (90d) and mTLS certs (30d) are policy-doc only — no automated 90d API-key expiry/rotation job and no mTLS at all (see SEC-014/§18.1). Session-token 15m TTL not explicitly asserted in code.

requirements.title: SEC-017 — Phishing-resistant MFA enforced for admin roles
requirements.evidence: WebAuthn (phishing-resistant) implemented with attestation + MDS3 root pinning; Auth/LockoutPolicy.cs; admin roles defined in RBAC.
requirements.gap: WebAuthn exists but there is no policy that REQUIRES WebAuthn (vs TOTP) specifically for admin/SecurityManage roles. Add a role-conditional MFA-strength enforcement gate.

requirements.title: SEC-018 — Browser CSP (strict), Trusted Types, SRI for scripts
requirements.evidence: deploy/vps/nginx.conf + Caddyfile.prod set Content-Security-Policy (default-src 'self', frame-ancestors 'none', base-uri 'self', form-action 'self'), HSTS, X-Frame-Options DENY. Commit 0a94af26 added CSP.
requirements.gap: CSP currently allows script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' (relaxed for Next.js hydration) — NOT strict. No Trusted Types (require-trusted-types-for 'script') and no Subresource Integrity (integrity= hashes) on script tags. Move to nonce/hash-based strict CSP + Trusted Types + SRI.

requirements.title: SEC-019 — SBOM (SPDX + CycloneDX) published per release
requirements.evidence: docs/04-security/sbom.md exists but is explicitly 'Draft (manual)' with a manual inventory and an 'automation planned' section. .github/workflows/ci.yml has NO SBOM step; no SPDX output anywhere.
requirements.gap: No automated SBOM generation in CI (cyclonedx-dotnet/cyclonedx-npm not wired), no SPDX format, no per-release publication as artifacts. Add a CI job generating + attaching CycloneDX and SPDX SBOMs.

requirements.title: SEC-020 — SLSA-3 provenance attestations
requirements.evidence: No match for SLSA/in-toto/provenance generation in .github/workflows/. desktop-release.yml signs the binary but produces no SLSA provenance attestation.
requirements.gap: No build provenance (slsa-github-generator / in-toto attestations), no hermetic/reproducible build pipeline. Add SLSA provenance generation + verification to the release workflows.

requirements.title: SEC-021 — Container images signed; admission rejects unsigned
requirements.evidence: docs/07-devops/containers.md references containers; no cosign image signing in CI and no admission policy (Kyverno/Sigstore policy-controller) manifests in repo.
requirements.gap: No container image signing (cosign) and no Kubernetes admission policy rejecting unsigned images. Add cosign signing + admission policy.

requirements.title: SEC-022 — Per-tenant honeypot endpoints for credential probing
requirements.evidence: No match for honeypot/canary endpoint logic in backend.
requirements.gap: Honeypot/canary endpoints not implemented. Add tenant-scoped decoy endpoints feeding AnomalyDetector/SIEM.

requirements.title: SEC-023 — Confidential computing (SGX/TDX, SEV-SNP)
requirements.evidence: No match for SGX/TDX/SEV/enclave in repo.
requirements.gap: No confidential-computing / enclave attestation path. Highest-tier EE feature; absent. Requires deployment-target enclave support + remote attestation integration.

requirements.title: SEC-024 / SEC-025 — Network segmentation (data/control plane VPCs); egress allowlist
requirements.evidence: docs/07-devops/terraform.md mentions infra; no NetworkPolicy / VPC-segmentation / egress-firewall manifests in repo. Backend 127.0.0.1 default bind is app-level only.
requirements.gap: Data-plane/control-plane VPC separation and Kubernetes NetworkPolicy egress allowlists are not codified as deployable manifests. Add IaC + NetworkPolicy/firewall rules.

requirements.title: SEC-026 — Hardware security keys for break-glass
requirements.evidence: WebAuthn supports hardware keys generally, but no dedicated break-glass workflow (sealed credential, dual-control, audited emergency access).
requirements.gap: No break-glass emergency-access procedure backed by hardware keys (split-knowledge / dual approval / time-boxed). Add break-glass flow.

requirements.title: §18.1 — Zero-trust + mTLS between internal services; SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity
requirements.evidence: Auth is per-request bearer/OIDC at the API edge; no mTLS between internal services and no SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity in repo. (On the locked monolithic .NET stack there are few internal service hops.)
requirements.gap: Service-to-service mTLS + SPIFFE/SPIRE not implemented. Largely N/A for a single API process, but EE multi-service deployments would need a service mesh (mTLS) + workload identity. Document architecture decision or add mesh config.

requirements.title: §18.1 — Defense in depth (WAF, RASP, dependency scanning, SAST, DAST, fuzzing)
requirements.evidence: AnomalyDetector + rate limiting (RateLimitMiddleware) + IP allowlist at app layer; docs/04-security/vulnerability-management.md, penetration-test-plan.md, owasp-asvs-checklist.md. RTK/seed validation in CI.
requirements.gap: No dependency scanning (Dependabot/Snyk), SAST (CodeQL), DAST, or fuzzing jobs in .github/workflows/ci.yml. No WAF/RASP. Add CodeQL + dependency scanning + (optionally) DAST to CI.

requirements.title: §18.4 — AI provider compliance profiles (BAA/DPA/ZDR) surfaced; first-class object; no-downgrade fallback; exportable risk registry
requirements.evidence: ProviderComplianceClass + ProviderConfig fields drive AiGateway routing; PhiApproved/LocalOnly required for PHI; fallback never downgrades — non-compliant provider throws ProviderPolicyException rather than silently downgrading (EnforcePhiPolicy). Provider retention behavior modeled (Iter34ProviderRetentionTests). docs/09-regulatory/vendor-risk-register.md exportable; frontend admin governance page.
requirements.gap: BAA/DPA/ZDR statuses + jurisdictional notes are modeled and doc-tracked; surface 'endpoint eligibility' + ZDR attestation explicitly per-provider in the admin UI and ensure the risk registry export is machine-readable (CSV/JSON) end-to-end.

requirements.title: §18.3 — Compliance posture (HIPAA, HITECH, GDPR, ISO 27001/13485, IEC 62304/82304, NIST, SOC2, etc.)
requirements.evidence: 22 security docs + docs/09-regulatory: baa-template.md, eu-aiact-gdpr-profile.md, dpia.md, iso-14971-risk-register.md, iec-62304-sdlc.md, compliance-matrix.md, nist-ssdf-mapping.md, traceability-matrix.md, incident-response.md (breach workflow), data-retention.md, business-continuity.md.
requirements.gap: Frameworks are addressed as documentation/mappings (appropriate for many), but several technical safeguards they require are still partial in code (backups SEC-015, rotation SEC-016, breach-notification workflow is doc-only not a built-in reportable-incident system). 42 CFR Part 2, HDS, HITRUST, PCI scope are doc-only. Operationalize breach-notification + accounting-of-disclosures as features.

requirements.title: §19.1 / §19.2 — Intended-use statement + risk-classification matrix (non-SaMD posture)
requirements.evidence: docs/09-regulatory/intended-use.md + samd-classification.md capture the intended-use statement and the capability/risk-tier matrix; non-SaMD posture chosen and recorded in PROGRESS.md. Safety boundaries enforced in code (never auto-signs; .ai-mark on AI text; deterministic rulebook-backed recommendations).
requirements.gap: Captured as policy + reflected in product safeguards. To reach 100%, bind the intended-use/claims to the UI (display intended-use disclaimer in-app) and assert the risk-tier triggers in CI.

requirements.title: §19.3 — Predetermined Change Control Plan (PCCP)
requirements.evidence: docs/09-regulatory/iec-62304-sdlc.md + clinical-evaluation-plan.md + ce-mark-checklist.md describe model-change governance; drift baselines exist (Migrations/20260515_DriftBaselines.cs, DriftController, ModelDriftDetectionService).
requirements.gap: PCCP is a document; the runtime change-envelope (architecture/training-data/hyperparameter bounds), acceptable-performance-bounds gating, and re-training triggers are not enforced as code/config. EE feature — operationalize the change envelope + automated bound checks.

requirements.title: §19.4 — Post-market monitoring (drift watch, field-failure reporting, incident SLAs, annual review)
requirements.evidence: Api/Services/ModelDriftDetectionService.cs (6h golden-case drift probes, configurable threshold, raises SystemAlert/SecurityAlert) + DriftController + DriftDetectionTests + docs/09-regulatory/pms-plan.md; incident-response.md.
requirements.gap: Drift watch is real. Field-failure reporting workflow (clinician-submitted defect intake), incident classification + notification SLAs, and the annual Clinical Safety Working Group review are doc-only — no built-in field-failure report UI/endpoint. Add a field-failure intake + SLA tracker.

requirements.title: §19.5 — Claims governance (banned-word CI gate for 'diagnose'/'interpret'/'decide'/'automated reading')
requirements.evidence: docs reference claims/over-claiming policy (ai-safety.md, copywriting), and ReportingService/RoutingPreviewController contain the words incidentally, but there is NO CI lint step scanning marketing/UI/doc strings for banned claim words. ci.yml has no claims-policy job.
requirements.gap: The claims policy is not enforced — no CI check flags 'diagnose/interpret/decide/automated reading' in UI strings, docs, or marketing copy for human review. Add a claims-lint CI job over frontend strings + docs.

area: Testing Strategy + DevEx/APIs + Acceptance Criteria (PRD v2 §28, §29, §31)
summary: RadioPad has a genuinely substantial test and DevEx foundation on the locked .NET/Next.js/Tauri/Capacitor stack, but it is well short of PRD v2 §28/§29/§31 as written. STRONG: ~85-test xUnit backend suite (unit + WebApplicationFactory integration against SQLite), 24 frontend Vitest component suites, golden-case CI (22 rulebooks + 52 cases via `radiopad rulebook test`), k6 perf scripts with P95/P99 thresholds wired to `perf-smoke.yml`, nightly live SIEM/KMS suites, an in-repo OpenAPI spec, multi-OS desktop signing + post-release installer signature verification, CONTRIBUTING/SECURITY/CODE_OF_CONDUCT, a rich 130-row traceability matrix, plus real implementations behind many acceptance criteria (SCIM 2.0, KMS/CMK, OIDC/SAML/WebAuthn, SIEM CEF/NDJSON, governance dashboard, validation packs, FHIR DiagnosticReport serializer, locale scaffolding, audit hash-chain, PHI policy enforcement, provider fallback by compliance class). GAPS vs the PRD text: NO branch-coverage gate in CI (≥80% / ≥90% ungated — only a documented target); NO property-based, fuzz, mutation, chaos, load, or AI-snapshot/visual-regression tests; NO Playwright/tauri-driver e2e (explicitly "planned"), so every acceptance criterion verified "by UI e2e" is unverified; NO SAST/DAST/Trivy/grype/Cosign/SBOM(CycloneDX) wired into CI (security-test-plan marks them "planned", no scanning workflow exists); NO testcontainers integration matrix (Postgres/Redis/NATS/MinIO/Orthanc/HAPI), NO public Conformance Suite or dashboard; NO SDK packages (@radiopad/sdk, python/go/rust), NO AsyncAPI specs, NO schema-first codegen, OpenAPI is 3.0.3 not 3.1; NO docs site (Pagefind/MDX) — only a `docs/` markdown tree; NO ARCHITECTURE.md/GOVERNANCE.md; local-dev bootstrap differs from the §29.1 recipe (no `./scripts/bootstrap`, no `docker compose up` of the OSS infra, no Makefile targets). Many §31 criteria are clinically/operationally aspirational and absent in code: bias eval, hallucination/prompt-injection corpora, contradiction-recall ≥95% on a 500-case set, WER≤12% whisper benchmark (whisper is documented sidecar wiring only), RECIST 1.1 tracking, peer review/concordance, MIRC teaching files, federated learning, air-gapped install test, SOC2/ISO/HITRUST/EU-AI-Act packs, signed model-eval reports, fleet-config API, `radiopad migrate` N→N+2, contract tests, reproducible builds. CLAUDE.md stack-substitution rule was applied throughout — items are NOT marked missing merely for lacking Go/Rust/Keycloak/Vite; they are marked on whether the FUNCTION exists.

requirements.title: §28.1 Unit tests — every package, ≥80% branch coverage gate
requirements.evidence: Large xUnit suite at backend/RadioPad.Api/tests/RadioPad.Api.Tests/ (~85 test files: ValidationTests, AiGatewayPolicyTests, KmsAdapterTests, provider adapter tests, etc.) plus 24 frontend Vitest suites under frontend/__tests__/. docs/06-testing/test-strategy.md sets coverage TARGETS (Validation ≥90%, Application ≥85%). BUT .github/workflows/ci.yml runs `dotnet test` with NO coverage collection (no coverlet/`--collect:"XPlat Code Coverage"`) and NO threshold gate; frontend uses typecheck+build, no vitest --coverage gate.
requirements.gap: Wire coverlet + a CI-enforced branch-coverage threshold (fail under 80%); add vitest coverage gate for frontend logic. No package-completeness audit exists.

requirements.title: §28.1 Property-based tests (proptest/hypothesis) for parsers, schema, validators
requirements.evidence: No FsCheck/Hedgehog/property-test dependency or generator-based tests found; validation/parsers tested only by hand-written example cases (ValidationTests.cs, RulebookGovernanceTests.cs).
requirements.gap: Add a property-based testing library and generators for RulebookSpec.FromYaml, the validation rule engine, and FHIR/schema serialization.

requirements.title: §28.1 Integration tests with testcontainers (Postgres, Redis, NATS, MinIO, Orthanc, HAPI FHIR)
requirements.evidence: Integration tests exist via WebApplicationFactory<Program> against in-memory SQLite (RadioPadAppFactory.cs); HL7 MLLP, FHIR webhook, DICOMweb client, SIEM sinks covered with stubs. NO Testcontainers package; no real Postgres/Redis/NATS/MinIO/Orthanc/HAPI containers spun up. Nightly live suites hit real cloud SIEM/KMS only when operator secrets present.
requirements.gap: Add Testcontainers-based integration matrix against real Postgres + an Orthanc + HAPI FHIR reference server (function = integration coverage on real backing services).

requirements.title: §28.1 End-to-end tests with Playwright (web) and tauri-driver (Studio)
requirements.evidence: No playwright.config, no *.spec.ts anywhere; docs/06-testing/e2e-test-plan.md and test-strategy.md §5 explicitly say 'Playwright (planned)' and the `pnpm e2e` script is 'Pending implementation'. tauri-driver not present.
requirements.gap: Implement the 7 documented e2e flows in Playwright + a CI job; add a tauri-driver Studio smoke. This blocks every §31 criterion verified 'by UI e2e'.

requirements.title: §28.1 Performance tests (k6/Vegeta) with enforced perf budgets
requirements.evidence: perf/k6/scripts/{ai-draft,impression,validate,audit-write}.js with P95/P99 thresholds; .github/workflows/perf-smoke.yml spins up the backend (Mock provider), seeds tenant, runs each scenario and fails on threshold violation; backend PerfBudgets OTel histograms verified by Iter33/PerfBudgetTests.cs.
requirements.gap: Manual-trigger only (not gated per-PR) and runs against the Mock provider, not staging; Vegeta not used. Function (k6 perf budgets enforced) is present.

requirements.title: §28.1 Monthly load tests (synthetic 10× steady-state burst)
requirements.evidence: perf-smoke is a 3-VU/30s smoke; no high-VU load scenario, no monthly schedule, no 10× burst profile in perf/ or any workflow.
requirements.gap: Add a scheduled high-volume k6 load profile (10× steady-state) and a monthly workflow.

requirements.title: §28.1 Chaos tests (Litmus/Chaos Mesh); SLOs hold under failure
requirements.evidence: No Litmus/Chaos Mesh manifests or fault-injection tests found; SLO machinery exists (AvailabilityMonitorService, Alertmanager webhook) but no chaos harness exercises it.
requirements.gap: Add chaos/fault-injection experiments and assert SLOs under tested failure modes (equivalent tooling acceptable per stack-substitution).

requirements.title: §28.1 Fuzz tests for FHIR/HL7/DICOMweb parsers (cargo-fuzz/go-fuzz/atheris)
requirements.evidence: No fuzz targets; parsers (Hl7MllpListener, FHIR serializer, DICOMweb client) covered by example tests only. SharpFuzz (the .NET equivalent) absent.
requirements.gap: Add coverage-guided fuzzing (e.g. SharpFuzz) for the HL7/FHIR/DICOMweb parsing surface.

requirements.title: §28.1 Mutation tests for safety-critical validators
requirements.evidence: No Stryker.NET config or mutation-testing job; the validation engine (ReportValidator.cs) is the safety-critical target but has no mutation coverage.
requirements.gap: Add Stryker.NET against RadioPad.Validation with a mutation-score gate.

requirements.title: §28.1 Snapshot tests for AI outputs vs golden cases (rulebook regression)
requirements.evidence: Golden-case execution exists: rulebooks/_tests/<id>/*.json run via `radiopad rulebook test` in ci.yml; CLI `eval` command (Program.cs) runs golden cases against a deployed provider and prints pass/total. These assert expected validation findings (expectFlagged), not snapshot/diff of AI free-text output.
requirements.gap: Add deterministic golden-output snapshots with tolerance bands for AI-generated text (overlaps MVP-β-04); current harness checks flags, not output diffs.

requirements.title: §28.2 Per-subspecialty golden-case packs (≥50 at GA → ≥500 at LTS)
requirements.evidence: 22 rulebooks with golden suites under rulebooks/_tests/ totalling ~52 cases (most subspecialties have 1-5 cases each, e.g. chest_ct: clean/missing_impression/laterality_conflict).
requirements.gap: Cases are ~1-5 per subspecialty, far below ≥50/subspecialty at GA and ≥500 at LTS. Needs a large clinically-curated case-pack expansion.

requirements.title: §28.2 Hallucination harness with crafted cases
requirements.evidence: backend HallucinationDetector.cs exists and UnsupportedClaimFinding surfaces unsupported sentences (AI-007); docs/05-data-ai/hallucination-policy.md present.
requirements.gap: No dedicated crafted-case hallucination test corpus or eval harness measuring detection rate; detector exists but is not exercised by an adversarial fixture set.

requirements.title: §28.2 Adversarial prompt-injection corpus
requirements.evidence: Prompt-injection appears only in docs (agent-safety.md, threat-model.md); no test corpus or eval harness in the repo exercises injection resistance.
requirements.gap: Build an adversarial prompt-injection corpus and an automated eval asserting the gateway/validator resists it.

requirements.title: §28.2 Bias evaluation across cohorts (sex/age/language)
requirements.evidence: No bias-eval dataset, script, or test; only conceptual mention in docs/05-data-ai. No cohort-stratified evaluation exists.
requirements.gap: Implement a bias-evaluation harness over public+synthetic cohorts with reported metrics.

requirements.title: §28.3 SAST (semgrep/gosec/cargo-audit/npm audit)
requirements.evidence: docs/06-testing/security-test-plan.md lists Roslyn analyzers + ESLint security rules per-PR and `dotnet list package --vulnerable`/`pnpm audit`. NO semgrep/gosec/cargo-audit and NO dedicated security-scan workflow in .github/workflows/ (ci.yml has no scanning step).
requirements.gap: Add a SAST + dependency-audit CI job (semgrep or equivalent + dotnet/pnpm vulnerable-package scan) that fails the build.

requirements.evidence: ZAP listed as 'planned (weekly)' in security-test-plan.md; no ZAP workflow or config in repo.
requirements.gap: Stand up a staging DAST scan (ZAP baseline/full) on a schedule.

requirements.evidence: Trivy/Grype only documented (security-test-plan, docs/04-security/vulnerability-management.md); no Trivy/Grype workflow, no .github/dependabot.yml found.
requirements.gap: Add Dependabot config and a Trivy/Grype scan job with a high/critical CVE gate (covers MVP-α-13).

requirements.title: §28.3 Container scan in CI; admission controller rejects unsigned/vuln images
requirements.evidence: No container-scan step in any workflow; no Kubernetes admission-controller policy (Kyverno/OPA/cosign-verify) in deploy/. Deploy uses docker-compose/Caddy/nginx, not a signed-image admission gate.

requirements.title: §28.4 Public RadioPad Conformance Suite (runnable by providers/connectors/rulebook authors)

Plugin/MCP manifest signature verification exists but is not a public conformance runner.

requirements.title: §28.4 Public conformance dashboard per integration
requirements.evidence: No conformance registry/dashboard in repo or docs.

requirements.title: §29.1 Local development bootstrap (./scripts/bootstrap, docker compose up, make targets)

requirements.gap: Function (a documented one-command local setup) partly exists but diverges from §29.1; deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml is production-only. Add a dev compose + bootstrap/make-equivalent if exact recipe is desired.

requirements.title: §29.3 Schema-first development (code generated from specs)

requirements.gap: Adopt schema-first codegen or at least a spec/code drift-check in CI.

requirements.title: §29.4 Conventions — Semver, Conventional Commits, release-please, edition/version pins, MSRV CI-checked
requirements.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.md mandates Conventional-Commit prefixes; CHANGELOG.md present; VERSIONING.md referenced; TS strict mode used; SDKs target .NET 8 / Node 20. NO release-please automation, NO MSRV CI check, no enforced semver gate for rulebook spec/plugin ABI.
requirements.gap: Add release-please (or equivalent automated changelog/release), an MSRV/toolchain-version CI check, and explicit semver gates for the rulebook spec + plugin ABI.

requirements.title: §29.5 Contributor docs — CONTRIBUTING.md, ARCHITECTURE.md per subdir, SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, GOVERNANCE.md
requirements.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md exist at root; rich docs/03-architecture/ tree. MISSING: per-subdir ARCHITECTURE.md files and a GOVERNANCE.md (no root or non-node_modules match).
requirements.gap: Add GOVERNANCE.md (steering committee/RFC/working groups) and per-subdir ARCHITECTURE.md (or a single architecture doc satisfying the function).

requirements.title: §29.6 Documentation site (docs.radiopad.dev, MDX, Pagefind, versioned, public reference)

requirements.title: §31 P0 gating — no release until every P0 criterion green in CI for two consecutive nightly runs
requirements.evidence: No machine-readable §31 acceptance-criteria suite mapped to CI; docs/00-product/acceptance-criteria.md is a manual feature checklist (F-01..F-08), not the §31 matrix; no 'two consecutive nightly' release gate exists. Nightly workflow only runs live SIEM/KMS smoke.
requirements.gap: Encode §31 criteria as automated checks and a release gate requiring two green nightlies.

requirements.title: MVP-α-01 Draft from free-text/paste in ≤2 clicks (UI e2e)

requirements.title: MVP-α-03 AI text distinct visual treatment until edited/accepted (visual regression + unit)

requirements.evidence: GET /api/audit/siem?format=json|cef (CEF + NDJSON) per traceability INT-010; Sinks.cs Splunk/Sentinel/Elastic/Syslog (RFC5424); SiemSinkTests/SiemLiveSmokeTests.
